target_output.thinking_summary: The key fix in round 3 is ISS-003: OAuth token expiry lifecycle management. I need to add a token expiry tracking field to marketplace_credentials, implement 24-hour pre-expiry warnings in the settings UI, and ensure the poller checks token freshness before each poll cycle. Amazon LWA, eBay OAuth 2.0, and Walmart each have different refresh token TTLs so the expiry monitoring must be per-marketplace. I will also document Walmart Marketplace rate limits which were flagged as missing.

fix_report.action_taken: Added token_expires_at field to marketplace_credentials; poller module now checks token freshness before each poll cycle and attempts refresh if within 2 hours of expiry; Settings page shows expiry countdown per marketplace with amber warning at 24 hours and red warning at 2 hours; Sentry alert raised if automatic refresh fails
fix_report.expected_outcome: Resellers will see proactive expiry warnings before marketplace API failures occur; automatic refresh will silently renew tokens in the happy path; manual re-authentication flow is clearly surfaced when refresh fails

target_output.title: Marketplace Reseller Desktop Repricing Tool
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron 32 desktop application with Vue 3 frontend and embedded Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar, backed by a local DuckDB database, that automates competitor price monitoring and bulk repricing across Amazon, eBay, and Walmart Marketplace. OAuth2 tokens for each marketplace are stored in the OS keychain via Electron safeStorage with proactive expiry monitoring and automatic token refresh. A 15-minute poller refreshes competitor price points; the RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against margin rules and populates a diff-style approval queue showing current price, proposed price, and expected margin before batch submission.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 32 main process manages the app lifecycle, OS keychain access via safeStorage, and auto-updater. The Vue 3 renderer process handles all UI via a typed IPC bridge exposed through a preload script. A Python 3.12/FastAPI sidecar is spawned as a child process on app start and listens on loopback 127.0.0.1:8923 — the Electron preload script proxies API calls to the sidecar via HTTP. DuckDB stores all listing snapshots and competitor price point history locally with no cloud dependency. The Poller module runs APScheduler jobs per connected marketplace on 15-minute intervals, checking token freshness before each cycle and refreshing tokens within 2 hours of expiry. The RepricingEngine processes each listing_snapshot against the active margin profile and pushes candidates to the reprice queue. The BatchSubmitter groups approved items by marketplace and submits reprices in rate-limit-compliant batches.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 32, Vue 3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PrimeVue, Pinia, Vue Query. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, APScheduler, sp-api-python, ebaysdk, DuckDB Python SDK, cryptography library for keychain IPC. Data: DuckDB (columnar, local file). Distribution: Electron Forge with NSIS (Windows) and DMG (macOS); code-signed; self-hosted auto-update server. Observability: Sentry Electron SDK; local rotating log files.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Marketplace Connections: connect Amazon, eBay, and Walmart via OAuth2 flows; settings UI shows token expiry countdown per marketplace with amber/red warnings; automatic background token refresh at 2-hour threshold. Listing Snapshot Import: fetch active listings from each connected marketplace via API, upsert into DuckDB listing_snapshots table. Competitor Price Polling: APScheduler 15-minute jobs per marketplace fetch competitor prices for all tracked SKUs and upsert competitor_price_points. Reprice Queue: RepricingEngine evaluates each listing against active margin profile, computes proposed_price, and populates queue with current vs. proposed diff and expected_margin_pct. Batch Reprice Submission: user approves or overrides items in queue; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace and submits in rate-limit-compliant batches. Analytics Dashboard: DuckDB queries powering margin trend per ASIN, reprice action history, competitor price history charts in Vue renderer.
target_output.system_components: Electron 32 main process (app lifecycle, safeStorage, auto-updater); Vue 3 renderer (SPA via preload IPC bridge); Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar child process on loopback:8923; DuckDB file in OS app data directory; APScheduler for marketplace pollers; Sentry Electron SDK; self-hosted Electron Forge auto-update server.
target_output.workflows: Token Expiry Monitoring and Refresh: On each Poller tick, the sidecar checks marketplace_credentials.token_expires_at for the target marketplace; if within 2 hours of expiry, attempts token refresh using the stored refresh token via the marketplace OAuth2 token endpoint; on success, updates token and token_expires_at in keychain and database; on failure, sets token_status=refresh_failed and emits an IPC event to the renderer causing the Settings page to display a red re-authenticate banner. The poller aborts the current cycle and logs the failure to avoid sending requests with an expired token. Settings page polls token status every 60 seconds and shows per-marketplace countdown: green (>24 hours), amber (2-24 hours), red (<2 hours or refresh_failed). Reprice Queue Approval and Batch Submit: User opens Reprice Queue page, views diff table with columns: SKU, marketplace, current_price, proposed_price, expected_margin_pct; clicks approve on individual rows or 'Approve All' for bulk; approved items submitted to BatchSubmitter; BatchSubmitter groups by marketplace, respects rate limits (Amazon SP-API: 1 req/sec with burst 5; eBay Sell API: 5 req/sec; Walmart Marketplace: 10 req/sec); submission results written to reprice_run_logs with status submitted or failed; failed items surfaced as retryable in queue.
target_output.data_model: marketplace_credentials (marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/walmart, token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, refresh_token_ref VARCHAR keychain key, token_status ENUM active/expiring_soon/refresh_failed, connected_at). listing_snapshots (id, marketplace, seller_sku, asin_or_item_id, title, current_price_cents INT, cost_price_cents INT, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ). competitor_price_points (id, asin_or_item_id, marketplace, competitor_seller_id, price_cents INT, condition, polled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE per polled_at). repricing_rules (id, name, floor_price_cents INT, target_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), ceiling_price_cents INT, applies_to ENUM all/category/sku_list, active BOOLEAN). reprice_run_logs (id, run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, marketplace, seller_sku, old_price_cents INT, proposed_price_cents INT, expected_margin_pct DECIMAL(5,2), status ENUM pending/approved/submitted/failed/overridden, submitted_at). channel_performance_reports (id, marketplace, report_date DATE, total_listings INT, repriced_count INT, avg_margin_pct DECIMAL).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI sidecar on loopback:8923. GET /listings?marketplace=amazon&min_margin_gap=5 (listings with margin below threshold), GET /queue (pending reprice items with diff data), POST /queue/approve (body: {item_ids:[...]} or {approve_all: true}), POST /queue/submit (trigger BatchSubmitter for approved items), GET /analytics/margin-trend?sku=:sku&days=30 (DuckDB query), GET /analytics/reprice-history?days=7, GET /settings/connections (marketplace status + token expiry_at + token_status), POST /settings/connect/:marketplace (initiate OAuth2 flow), POST /settings/refresh/:marketplace (manual token refresh trigger), GET /health.
target_output.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 token storage: all access tokens and refresh tokens stored via Electron safeStorage (OS AES-256 keychain) referenced by key name only in DuckDB marketplace_credentials; actual token values never written to DuckDB or log files. Token lifecycle: token_expires_at tracked in DuckDB (non-sensitive metadata); automatic refresh attempt at 2-hour threshold; refresh_failed status triggers UI warning and polling abort. Authorization headers redacted in sidecar debug logs via FastAPI middleware. DuckDB file stored in OS app data directory with user-only file permissions (chmod 600 on macOS/Linux, restricted ACL on Windows). TLS 1.3 enforced for all outbound marketplace API calls. Marketplace API rate limits enforced by token bucket per marketplace to comply with ToS.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Electron Forge builds DMG (macOS, notarized with Apple Developer ID) and NSIS installer (Windows, EV code-signed) via GitHub Actions matrix runners. Binaries uploaded to self-hosted Electron auto-update server on tag push. GitHub Actions CI: pytest for FastAPI sidecar (Poller token refresh logic, RepricingEngine margin calculation, BatchSubmitter rate limit compliance tests at 90% coverage), Vitest for Vue 3 components (queue table, settings page), Electron Forge build on macOS-latest and windows-latest runners. Environments: dev (npm run dev with hot reload), beta (code-signed build distributed to 10 reseller testers), release (full distribution via auto-update). DuckDB file stored in OS app data; no server infrastructure required for v1.
target_output.observability: Sentry Electron SDK captures renderer and main process crashes with stack traces; per-version Sentry projects for regression tracking. Local rotating log file in OS app data directory (7-day retention): structured JSON logs from FastAPI sidecar with traceId per poll cycle and per reprice run. Key log events: poll_started, poll_completed (with SKU count), token_refresh_attempted, token_refresh_failed, reprice_submitted (with marketplace and count), reprice_failed (with error code). No server-side monitoring for v1; Sentry alert rules for token_refresh_failed error spike.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Near-zero infrastructure cost for v1: self-hosted auto-update server on 10 USD/month VPS; no cloud DB. DuckDB handles 10,000 SKUs with 90-day competitor price history (approx 260 million rows at 15-minute intervals) in under 500 MB; queries complete in <500ms via columnar scan. Growth path: multi-seat v2 will require central DuckDB or MotherDuck cloud sync; current local architecture scales to single-user workload without modification.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Amazon SP-API OAuth2 connection with token expiry monitoring and auto-refresh, listing snapshot import, 15-minute competitor price poller, margin rule builder (floor/ceiling/target margin), reprice queue with diff table and approve/approve-all flow, batch reprice submission to SP-API, basic analytics (margin trend per ASIN), DuckDB 90-day retention cleanup job, Electron Forge macOS and Windows builds with code signing, Sentry integration. Phase 2 (8 weeks): eBay and Walmart Marketplace connections with their respective OAuth2 and rate limit profiles, cross-marketplace analytics dashboard, channel performance reports, Parquet archive export for DuckDB purge. Phase 3 (future): multi-seat licensing with central sync server, AI margin rule suggestions, supplier cost import from CSV/API.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python sidecar uses mypy strict mode and Ruff linting; RepricingEngine and Poller modules have 90% pytest coverage. Vue 3 components use TypeScript strict; Vitest for component unit tests. Electron preload IPC types generated from FastAPI OpenAPI spec via openapi-typescript. Marketplace API credentials never appear in logs — custom Sentry before-send hook strips Authorization headers. PR review required for all changes; changelog maintained per release for auto-updater release notes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Amazon SP-API throttling during batch reprice submission for resellers with large catalogs. Mitigation: BatchSubmitter implements token bucket (1 req/sec default, configurable) with request queue; user sees progress bar during submission. Risk: DuckDB file corruption on crash during write. Mitigation: DuckDB WAL mode; Sentry crash report triggered on abnormal exit; user prompted to run CHECKPOINT on next launch if WAL file detected. Tradeoff: local DuckDB removes cloud egress costs but limits multi-device sync for v1 — accepted by target single-seat resellers; multi-seat sync deferred to v2 with MotherDuck or central Postgres.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Token expiry monitoring: per-marketplace token_expires_at stored in DuckDB; auto-refresh at 2-hour threshold; UI warning at 24 hours — ISS-003 resolved. Walmart Marketplace rate limits: 10 req/sec per credential, confirmed in Walmart Marketplace API docs. eBay rate limits: 5 req/sec for Sell Inventory API. DuckDB file size warning threshold set at 2 GB with archive-and-purge guidance in settings.

metadata.notes: Round 3 desktop repricing tool fixing token expiry lifecycle UX (ISS-003); Electron 32/Vue 3/Python FastAPI/DuckDB for marketplace resellers.

profile.domain: Consignment Boutique Inventory and Settlement Management

project_goal.value: Desktop inventory reconciliation app for multi-location consignment boutiques migrating from spreadsheet-based tracking to a structured system with barcode scanning, location transfers, and consignor settlement calculations
project_goal.rationale: Modernization need from boutique operations manager; spreadsheets cause settlement errors and inventory discrepancies

target_users.value: Boutique staff for item intake and barcode scanning; boutique managers for settlement approval and payout recording
target_users.rationale: Two distinct roles identified in operations review

project_class.rationale: Local desktop allows barcode scanner USB HID integration and offline operation without internet dependency

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, payments, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Barcode scanner HID integration, settlement payout calculations, and multi-location inventory sync

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-location inventory with consignor split logic; no distributed systems required

risk_level.rationale: Financial settlement data and consignor payout records require data integrity controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Consignor payout amounts and sale transactions are financially sensitive business records

external_exposure.rationale: Single-location or LAN-only operation; no internet-facing API required

access_model.value: Staff PIN login (4-8 digits) maps to JWT role; roles: staff (item intake, scan lookup), manager (all staff plus settlement approval, payout recording, user management)
access_model.rationale: Simple PIN-based auth appropriate for boutique environment; no password manager needed

feature_scope.value: Barcode scanning for item intake and lookup, consignor account management, location bin assignment and transfer, settlement period management, auto-calculated consignor payouts, payout approval and lock, itemized payout report PDF export, sale transaction import from POS
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature list from boutique manager requirements

mvp_scope.value: Barcode item intake, consignor accounts, location bins, settlement period auto-calculation, manager payout approval, payout record locking
mvp_scope.rationale: Core settlement accuracy improvement sufficient for initial rollout

security_baseline.value: Staff PIN hashed with bcrypt; SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher; settlement period records locked (immutable) after manager approval; no internet connectivity required
security_baseline.rationale: Local-only app; data integrity of financial records is primary security concern

frontend_stack.value: Tauri 2 with Svelte 5 and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri 2 + Svelte provides lightweight desktop bundle; Svelte reactivity suits real-time barcode scan feedback

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum embedded sidecar
backend_stack.rationale: Rust ensures type-safe SQLite operations and barcode HID event handling without runtime dependency

data_platform.value: SQLite with SQLCipher encryption
data_platform.rationale: Embedded DB with no server process; SQLCipher for data-at-rest protection

hosting_target.value: Local desktop binary; distributed via USB drive or internal network share; no internet update server required for v1
hosting_target.rationale: Boutique environments have unreliable internet; USB distribution is simpler for non-technical staff

privacy_retention_policy.value: Consignor payout records retained 7 years; sale transactions retained 7 years; settlement periods locked and immutable after approval; SQLite database backed up daily to external drive
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Standard financial record-keeping requirements

future_scope.value: Multi-location LAN sync between boutique stores, POS system direct API integration, consignor self-service portal
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap pending adoption

constraints.value: Must run on Windows 10/11; barcode scanner is USB HID device; no internet required; SQLite backup to external USB drive
constraints.rationale: Boutique hardware and connectivity constraints

observability_baseline.value: Local application event log file; no external monitoring for v1
observability_baseline.rationale: Local-only app; log file sufficient for troubleshooting

execution_preference.value: 3-week sprints with boutique manager UAT after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Settlement calculation correctness requires frequent real-world validation

compliance_context.value: Financial record retention per local commercial law (7 years); consignor agreement terms must be recorded per consignor account
compliance_context.rationale: Consignor payouts are financial obligations; record retention required

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet-based consignment tracking with Tauri 2 desktop app supporting barcode scanning, location tracking, and settlement automation
project.target_users: Boutique staff and managers

frontend.state: Svelte stores for auth state and active scan session; SvelteKit routing for desktop pages

backend.api: Tauri IPC commands for DB mutations; local HTTP for read queries

security.auth: Staff PIN hashed bcrypt rounds 12; JWT HS256; manager role required for settlement approval

data.db: SQLite with SQLCipher; daily backup to external USB drive via Tauri file API

reasoner_reviews.product: The settlement period lock mechanism is the most critical feature — once a manager approves a settlement, payout records must be immutable to prevent retroactive edits that would undermine consignor trust. The PDF payout report is a hard requirement for consignors who want documentation of their earnings. Barcode intake flow must be fast enough for busy intake sessions where staff scan dozens of items per hour.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri 2 with Svelte 5 and Rust Axum sidecar is appropriate — Svelte 5 runes give fine-grained reactivity for the live barcode scan feedback UI without heavy framework overhead. The settlement calculation logic should be in a dedicated Rust module with comprehensive unit tests since payout errors have direct financial consequences. SQLite WAL mode is required to prevent database locks during concurrent sidecar reads and UI writes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Settlement period immutability must be enforced at the database layer, not just the UI — insert a trigger or CHECK constraint that prevents updates to payout_records where settlement_period.status='locked'. SQLCipher key derivation must use PBKDF2 with adequate iterations. Daily backup to USB drive should verify backup integrity via checksum after write.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Windows 10/11 USB HID barcode scanner input can be captured via Tauri plugin or as raw keyboard events in the WebView focus context. The no-internet constraint means updates must be distributed via USB or LAN share; include an in-app version display so boutique staff can identify which version to report when requesting support.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2 app with Svelte 5 SPA; Tauri IPC commands bridge Svelte to Rust sidecar; pages: Login (PIN pad), Dashboard (intake queue count, open settlement status), Item Intake (barcode scan input with instant consignor lookup), Inventory (searchable item grid by location bin), Transfers (move item between bins), Settlement (period list, approve flow, payout breakdown), Reports (PDF export), Settings
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (PIN pad UI with role display after auth), Item Intake (barcode field auto-focused, on scan: lookup item or prompt new consignor item form, assign to bin), Inventory Browser (filter by consignor, bin, status), Transfer Screen (scan item barcode, select target bin, confirm), Settlement Period (create period, auto-calculate payouts, line-item review, manager approve and lock), Payout Report (print PDF per consignor), Settings (backup trigger, version display, user management)
frontend.state_management: Svelte stores: authStore (JWT, role, staff_name), scanStore (last scanned barcode, lookup result, intake form state); no server-side state management needed for local-only app
frontend.accessibility_notes: Large PIN pad buttons for boutique touchscreen PCs; barcode scan input auto-focuses on page load; high-contrast item status badges for quick visual triage

backend.service_design: Rust Axum sidecar on loopback:7891; modules: auth (PIN verify, JWT issue), inventory (item CRUD, bin assignment, transfer), consignors (account CRUD, split config), settlement (period calculation engine, payout generation), reports (PDF generation via printpdf crate), backup (SQLite file copy to USB path)
backend.business_modules: SettlementEngine: for a given period date range, queries sale_transactions joined to consigned_items, computes net_sale * consignor_split_pct for each item, aggregates per consignor, writes payout_records; validates no pending transfers within period; marks period as calculated. PeriodLocker: when manager approves, sets settlement_periods.status=locked and triggers Postgres-style CHECK via SQLite trigger preventing further payout_records updates for that period. BackupService: on manual trigger or daily schedule, copies SQLite file to configured USB drive path, writes SHA-256 checksum file alongside backup
backend.failure_handling: Barcode lookup miss surfaced immediately in Svelte scan UI with sound cue and form to register new item; settlement calculation errors (e.g., missing sale transaction data) halt calculation and surface itemized error list for manager review

security.auth_design: Staff PIN (4-8 digits) hashed with bcrypt rounds 12 stored in SQLite staff_accounts table; JWT HS256 with 4-hour expiry; Rust sidecar validates JWT on every IPC route; PIN change requires manager role
security.authorization_model: Roles in JWT: staff (item intake, scan lookup, transfers), manager (all staff plus settlement approve, payout lock, user management, backup trigger); Axum middleware enforces role per route
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All settlement calculations and payout approvals written to audit_log table with staff_id, action, period_id, timestamp; settlement period lock recorded as immutable audit entry; log file rotated weekly

data.entities: consignor_accounts (id, name, contact_email, phone, split_pct DECIMAL, agreement_signed_at). consigned_items (id, barcode VARCHAR UNIQUE, consignor_id FK, title, description, category, condition, asking_price_cents, status ENUM available/sold/returned/transferred, intake_at). location_bins (id, bin_code UNIQUE, location_name, capacity). transfer_events (id, item_id FK, from_bin_id, to_bin_id, transferred_by FK, transferred_at). settlement_periods (id, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, status ENUM draft/calculated/locked, created_by FK, approved_by FK, approved_at). payout_records (id, period_id FK, consignor_id FK, item_id FK, sale_price_cents INT, consignor_split_pct DECIMAL, payout_amount_cents INT, locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). sale_transactions (id, item_id FK, sale_price_cents INT, sold_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pos_transaction_ref VARCHAR). barcode_registry (barcode VARCHAR PK, item_id FK, registered_at).
data.storage_design: SQLite with SQLCipher AES-256 in WAL journal mode; single database file per boutique location in OS app data directory; daily backup to configured USB drive path with SHA-256 checksum verification
data.migration_notes: One-time migration script reads boutique manager's Excel spreadsheets (consignor names, item list, historical sales); Python script normalizes and imports into SQLite via Tauri file picker; manager validates imported data against paper records before go-live

devops.deployment_topology: Tauri 2 NSIS installer for Windows 10/11; distributed via USB drive or LAN network share; no internet update server for v1; version string displayed in app settings for support identification
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: cargo clippy and cargo test (SettlementEngine and PeriodLocker unit tests 90% coverage), Svelte component tests via Vitest, Tauri NSIS build on Windows runner, installer artifact stored in GitLab package registry
devops.backup_and_recovery: Daily automated backup copies SQLite file to configured USB drive path with timestamp suffix; SHA-256 checksum written alongside; BackupService verifies checksum after copy; manager receives in-app confirmation; restore procedure: copy backup file to app data directory and restart app
devops.devops_risks: USB drive not always present on boutique PC — BackupService emits warning in UI if USB path is not accessible at scheduled backup time; manager must confirm backup before closing app at end of day

ISS-001.title: Settlement calculation does not handle items transferred between locations mid-period
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan omitted transfer_events table; settlement engine counted items in current bin regardless of which location made the sale. Round 2 added transfer_events and updated SettlementEngine to join sale_transactions to item location at time of sale.

ISS-002.title: Period locking enforced only in UI, not at database layer
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan locked settlement_periods.status in application code only. Round 3 added SQLite trigger preventing UPDATE on payout_records where parent settlement_period.status=locked.

ISS-003.title: Backup integrity not verified after USB copy
ISS-003.detail: Round 3 plan added SHA-256 checksum file alongside backup. BackupService verifies checksum post-copy and surfaces failure in UI.

ISS-004.title: PDF payout report does not include consignor agreement split percentage for audit trail
ISS-004.detail: Round 3 plan generates PDF payout report but omits consignor split_pct at time of period from report, making it non-auditable if split percentage changes after period close.

focus_issues.title: PDF payout report does not include consignor agreement split percentage for audit trail
focus_issues.detail: Round 3 plan generates PDF payout report but omits consignor split_pct at time of period from report, making it non-auditable if split percentage changes after period close.

latest_recommendations: Snapshot consignor split_pct into payout_records at calculation time so PDF report reflects the rate in effect at settlement; never read split_pct from live consignor_accounts for historical reports
Add split_pct_snapshot column to payout_records and populate it during SettlementEngine calculation
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Consignment Boutique Desktop Inventory and Settlement App v3

convergence_state.reason: ISS-004 PDF audit trail split_pct snapshot still unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Round 3 resolved the database-layer period locking (ISS-002) and backup integrity verification (ISS-003) effectively. The only remaining issue is the PDF payout report missing the historical split percentage snapshot, which creates an audit trail gap if consignor terms change.
strengths: SQLite trigger for period lock enforcement is correct approach — database-layer immutability not dependent on application logic
Backup SHA-256 checksum verification addresses integrity risk
SettlementEngine transfer-aware calculation from round 2 is solid
concerns: PDF payout report reads split_pct from live consignor_accounts rather than snapshotting at calculation time
No mention of what happens when a consignor split_pct is changed after a period is calculated but before it is locked
blocking_issues: ISS-004: PDF payout report omits historical split_pct; consignors could dispute payouts if rates change between calculation and lock
recommendations: Add split_pct_snapshot DECIMAL column to payout_records; populate during SettlementEngine.calculate() from consignor_accounts.split_pct at that moment
PDF generator should use payout_records.split_pct_snapshot not live consignor_accounts.split_pct

issue_updates.title: Period locking database layer
issue_updates.detail: SQLite trigger added in round 3 prevents payout_records updates when period is locked

issue_updates.title: PDF payout report split_pct audit trail
issue_updates.detail: PDF still reads live split_pct rather than snapshot at calculation time
