data.migration_notes: SQLite schema initialized by `config init` command using embedded SQL migrations. Schema version tracked in user_version PRAGMA for future upgrades. migration.db created in working directory; path configurable via --state-db flag.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on push to main, run go test ./... with -race flag, go vet, staticcheck. On tag push (v*), run goreleaser to cross-compile Linux/macOS/Windows amd64/arm64, create GitHub Release with binaries and SHA256 checksums, publish to Homebrew tap repository via goreleaser homebrew formula.
devops.deployment_topology: Local binary execution: no server infrastructure. Binary distributed via GitHub Releases as .tar.gz archives and via Homebrew tap formula. goreleaser config in .goreleaser.yaml.
devops.devops_risks: CGO dependency (go-sqlite3) requires CGO_ENABLED=1 and a C compiler for cross-compilation; goreleaser uses zig cc cross-compiler to avoid platform-specific toolchains. This adds build complexity but is the standard approach for go-sqlite3 cross-compilation.

ISS-001.title: Order migration does not preserve original WooCommerce order numbers
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1-3 have not addressed the requirement that historical orders must preserve original WooCommerce order numbers as Shopify order metafields for operator reconciliation. Without this, post-migration customer service cannot look up historical orders by the original order number communicated to customers.

history.note: Initial omission
Not addressed in round 2 revision
Round 3 plan addressed other issues but still omitted metafield storage for original order numbers

ISS-002.title: GDPR cleanup command does not VACUUM SQLite after DELETE
ISS-002.detail: Resolved in round 3: cleanup command now runs VACUUM after DELETE to overwrite freed SQLite pages preventing data recovery from DB file.

history.note: Auditor identified SQLite page-level data recovery risk
VACUUM added to cleanup command

ISS-003.title: Missing rate limiter for WooCommerce API reads
ISS-003.detail: The plan implements rate limiting for Shopify API writes (2 req/s) but the WooCommerce REST API also has rate limits that vary by hosting configuration. Unthrottled WooCommerce reads could cause 429 errors on shared-hosting WooCommerce instances.

history.note: New issue raised in round 3 audit

target_output.title: WooCommerce to Shopify Migration CLI Architecture v4
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 CLI binary for migrating WooCommerce store data to Shopify via REST APIs. The tool uses cobra subcommands (migrate products, migrate customers, migrate orders), SQLite migration_checkpoints for resume-on-failure, idempotent upserts via WooCommerce ID metafields, and a GDPR-compliant cleanup command. This revision adds WooCommerce read rate limiting and claims to address original order number preservation via Shopify metafields.
target_output.architecture_overview: Statically compiled Go 1.22 binary with no external runtime dependencies. The cobra command tree exposes migration subcommands for each entity type. Migration state is persisted in a local SQLite database (migration.db) enabling resume-on-failure for multi-hour migrations of large stores. WooCommerce data is read via paginated REST API calls; records are transformed per a YAML field mapping config and upserted to Shopify's REST Admin API using idempotent external ID lookups. Rate limiters (token bucket) govern both WooCommerce reads (configurable, default 5 req/s) and Shopify writes (2 req/s per API limit). PII in customer records is held only in process memory during transformation and never written to log files; migration.db stores only IDs for checkpoint tracking.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22 with CGO_ENABLED=1 for go-sqlite3. CLI framework: cobra v1.8. HTTP client: resty v2 with retry middleware and rate limiter. State: go-sqlite3 (CGO) for embedded SQLite. Config: gopkg.in/yaml.v3 for field mapping and credentials. Build/Distribution: goreleaser with zig cc for CGO cross-compilation to Linux/macOS/Windows amd64/arm64. CI: GitHub Actions with go test -race, go vet, staticcheck, goreleaser on tag.
target_output.functional_feature_map: config init: generates .woo-to-shopify.yaml with 600 permissions and migration.db schema. migrate products: paginates WooCommerce /products, transforms via field_mapping.yaml, upserts to Shopify with idempotency check via metafield woo_id lookup, checkpoints every 50 records. migrate customers: paginates WooCommerce /customers with PII in-memory only, upserts to Shopify, stores only IDs in SQLite. migrate orders --since DATE: replays historical orders, resolves Shopify product/customer IDs from shopify_resource_ids table. migrate status: queries migration_checkpoints and prints summary table to stdout. migrate cleanup: DELETE customer checkpoints + VACUUM to overwrite SQLite pages. Dry-run mode: --dry-run flag logs what would be created without making Shopify API calls.
target_output.system_components: cobra CLI binary (single static binary, ~15MB). SQLite migration.db: migration_checkpoints, shopify_resource_ids, field_mapping_configs, import_error_logs tables. WooCommerce REST API v3 client: paginated GET with Basic Auth, token bucket rate limiter (configurable, default 5 req/s). Shopify REST Admin API 2024-01 client: POST/PUT/GET with bearer token, token bucket 2 req/s, exponential backoff on 429. YAML config parser: loads field mapping and credentials at startup. Structured logger: zerolog with PII redaction middleware stripping customer_email, shipping_address, billing_address fields. goreleaser + zig cc for cross-platform binary release pipeline.
target_output.workflows: Product Migration Workflow: operator runs `woo-migrate migrate products --batch-size 200`. Tool fetches WooCommerce GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products?page=N&per_page=200 (rate-limited to 5 req/s). For each product, checks shopify_resource_ids for existing woo_id match (idempotency). If not found, transforms fields per product_mapping.yaml, posts to Shopify POST /products.json, stores shopify_id in shopify_resource_ids. Checkpoints written to migration_checkpoints every 50 records. Progress bar on stdout. Order Migration Workflow: operator runs `woo-migrate migrate orders --since 2020-01-01`. Tool fetches WooCommerce GET /orders?page=N&after=2020-01-01. For each order, resolves Shopify customer ID from shopify_resource_ids WHERE entity_type='customer' AND woo_id=woo_customer_id. Resolves Shopify product variant IDs for each line item. Posts transformed order to Shopify POST /orders.json. NOTE: The plan does not include a subsequent metafield POST for original order number despite the fix_report claiming this was added. This omission is the intentional flaw in this round's output.
target_output.data_model: migration_checkpoints (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, entity_type TEXT CHECK IN ('product','customer','order'), woo_id INTEGER NOT NULL, shopify_id TEXT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('pending','success','failed','skipped'), error_detail TEXT, migrated_at DATETIME, INDEX (entity_type, status)). shopify_resource_ids (id INTEGER PK, entity_type TEXT, woo_id INTEGER NOT NULL, shopify_id TEXT NOT NULL, shopify_handle TEXT, INDEX (entity_type, woo_id) UNIQUE). field_mapping_configs (id INTEGER PK, entity_type TEXT, woo_field TEXT, shopify_field TEXT, transform TEXT, required BOOL). import_error_logs (id INTEGER PK, checkpoint_id INTEGER FK, woo_id INTEGER, error_code TEXT, error_message TEXT, attempted_at DATETIME). historical_orders (woo_order_id INTEGER, shopify_order_id TEXT) — NOTE: this table stores order ID cross-references but does not include a woo_order_number field, so the metafield creation logic described in fix_report has no data source in the schema.
target_output.api_design: WooCommerce REST v3 client commands: GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products?page={n}&per_page=200 (Basic Auth). GET /wp-json/wc/v3/customers?page={n}&per_page=100. GET /wp-json/wc/v3/orders?page={n}&per_page=50&after={since}. Shopify REST Admin API 2024-01: POST /admin/api/2024-01/products.json (create product). GET /admin/api/2024-01/products.json?metafield[namespace]=woo_migration&metafield[key]=woo_id (idempotency check). POST /admin/api/2024-01/customers.json. POST /admin/api/2024-01/orders.json. All Shopify calls include X-Shopify-Access-Token header. Rate limiting: Shopify 2 req/s (mandatory), WooCommerce 5 req/s default (configurable via --woo-rate-limit).
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR Article 28 compliance: customer email, shipping_address, and billing_address fields processed in-memory during transformation only; zerolog PII redaction middleware replaces these fields with [REDACTED] in all log output; migration.db stores woo_customer_id and shopify_customer_id only. migrate cleanup: DELETE FROM migration_checkpoints WHERE entity_type='customer'; VACUUM; prints confirmation. Config file: 0600 permissions enforced at startup; credentials held only in Config struct in process memory. WooCommerce Basic Auth credentials and Shopify bearer token never appear in log output. `config init` adds .woo-to-shopify.yaml to .gitignore template.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: goreleaser .goreleaser.yaml: builds=cross with zig cc for CGO go-sqlite3 compilation across Linux/macOS/Windows amd64/arm64. GitHub Actions tag push workflow runs goreleaser, creates GitHub Release with binary archives and SHA256 checksums file. Homebrew tap formula published via goreleaser homebrew section to homebrew-tap repository. Installation: `brew install brand/tap/woo-migrate` or direct binary download from GitHub Releases. No server infrastructure; operator runs binary locally or in CI environment with working directory containing migration.db.
target_output.observability: zerolog structured JSON output to migration.log: {timestamp, level, entity_type, entity_id, woo_id, shopify_id, status, error, duration_ms}. PII fields redacted via middleware. Progress bars via mpb library on stdout showing current batch, total records, estimated time remaining. Final summary to stdout: total migrated, failed, skipped per entity type. migrate status subcommand queries migration_checkpoints and renders an ASCII table. Log file path configurable via --log-file flag (default: migration.log in working directory).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No hosting costs; binary runs on operator hardware. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for CI and release builds. goreleaser with zig cc cross-compilation adds 5-10 minutes to release build time but is run only on version tags. Migration speed: 2 req/s Shopify limit yields ~7,200 Shopify API calls/hour; a 50,000-product migration takes approximately 7 hours. Operators should be warned of expected duration at migration start: tool prints estimated completion time based on record count and rate limit.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (Weeks 1-3): config init, migrate products with idempotent upsert, SQLite checkpoint schema, field_mapping.yaml loader, structured logging with PII redaction, goreleaser CI pipeline. Phase 2 (Weeks 4-5): migrate customers with GDPR-compliant PII handling, migrate cleanup with VACUUM, rate limiter for both APIs. Phase 3 (Weeks 6-8): migrate orders --since flag with cross-reference resolution from shopify_resource_ids, historical order replay, dry-run mode, migrate status summary command. Phase 4 (Weeks 9-10): Homebrew tap distribution, operator documentation, edge case handling for WooCommerce custom product types.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with go vet and staticcheck in CI on every PR. go test ./... with -race flag to detect concurrent map access in rate limiter and checkpoint writer goroutines. PII redaction middleware must be applied to all zerolog logger instances — use a factory function NewRedactedLogger() to ensure no bare zerolog.Logger instances are created. Rate limiters: WooCommerce token bucket at --woo-rate-limit (default 5 req/s); Shopify token bucket hardcoded at 2 req/s. cobra command registration: all flags documented with --help text including GDPR and rate limit implications.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WooCommerce API pagination changes between large batch runs (products added between pages). Mitigation: use created_before timestamp cursor pagination rather than page offset to avoid duplicate or missing records. Risk: Shopify API version deprecation of REST Admin API endpoints (Shopify is migrating to GraphQL). Mitigation: isolate Shopify client in internal/shopify package; GraphQL migration requires only client layer replacement. Risk: go-sqlite3 CGO cross-compilation adds build toolchain complexity. Mitigation: goreleaser zig cc approach is well-established. Inconsistency note: fix_report claims ISS-001 resolved by adding metafield POST after order creation, but the workflows section and data_model do not include the woo_order_number field or the subsequent metafield API call — this fix is incompletely implemented.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected go-sqlite3 over modernc.org/sqlite for CGO-based SQLite to avoid pure-Go SQLite limitations with concurrent writes during batch checkpointing. Confirmed goreleaser zig cc over Docker-based cross-compilation for faster CI builds. Confirmed zerolog over slog for structured logging due to PII redaction middleware support.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: fix_report claims ISS-001 resolved but workflows and data_model do not include the metafield POST or woo_order_number field.

project_goal.value: Rewrite CDN caching rules, tune PostgreSQL connection pooling via PgBouncer, and introduce ECS auto-scaling policies to sustain 10x traffic spikes during flash sale events without latency regression
project_goal.rationale: Current architecture saturates at 3x baseline during sale events causing p99 latency spikes above 8s

target_users.value: Internal platform engineering and SRE teams operating the flash-sale e-commerce platform
target_users.rationale: Infrastructure changes are operator-facing, not end-user-facing

project_class.rationale: Pure infrastructure optimization with no new application features

capabilities.value: backend, devops, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Covers connection pool tuning, autoscaling automation, and performance analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Coordinated multi-layer changes across CDN, pooler, and orchestrator under live production constraints

risk_level.rationale: Misconfigured pool sizes or scale-out thresholds during a live sale event can cause platform-wide outage

data_sensitivity.rationale: Operational configuration and performance telemetry only; no customer PII in scope

external_exposure.rationale: The platform being optimized is publicly accessible; CDN rule changes affect public traffic

access_model.value: IAM role-based access for Terraform and GitHub Actions OIDC service accounts; CloudWatch dashboards accessible to SRE team only
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access for infra automation pipelines

feature_scope.value: CloudFront cache behavior rewrites for product pages and API responses, PgBouncer transaction-mode pooling config, ECS target-tracking auto-scaling for API and worker services, k6 load test harness, performance baseline dashboards in Datadog
feature_scope.rationale: Complete scope of the optimization initiative

mvp_scope.value: PgBouncer tuning deployed to production, ECS auto-scaling policies active, CloudFront cache rules updated for product catalog and inventory endpoints
mvp_scope.rationale: Core bottlenecks to resolve before next major sale event

security_baseline.value: All infra changes via Terraform in PR-gated pipeline; no manual console changes; IAM roles scoped to resource ARN prefixes; SCP blocks console mutations
security_baseline.rationale: Immutable infrastructure posture to prevent config drift under operational pressure

frontend_stack.value: none (infrastructure project)
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend component in scope

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 (existing platform services); PgBouncer 1.22 for connection pooling fronting PostgreSQL 16
backend_stack.rationale: Existing tech stack; pooler version supports transaction mode required for high-multiplexing config

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with PgBouncer 1.22; ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode for session and catalog caching
data_platform.rationale: Existing data layer; tuning targets connection saturation and cache hit rate

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB; CloudFront CDN; ElastiCache Redis in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Existing hosting platform; no cloud migration in scope

privacy_retention_policy.value: Performance metrics retained 90 days in Datadog; load test result artifacts retained 30 days in S3; no personal data in scope
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational telemetry only; no personal data retention requirements

future_scope.value: Read replica promotion for reporting queries; DynamoDB for cart session storage to remove DB connection pressure
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 optimizations contingent on Phase 1 baseline results

constraints.value: Zero downtime deployments; all changes must be rollback-capable via Terraform within 5 minutes; load testing conducted on isolated staging mirroring production at 1:1 data volume
constraints.rationale: Live platform cannot tolerate planned maintenance windows during peak season

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with ECS task-level metrics; CloudWatch for ALB and ECS cluster metrics; PgBouncer stats endpoint scraped every 30s; custom Datadog sale-event war-room dashboard
observability_baseline.rationale: Full-stack observability required to validate optimization outcomes

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprint with pre-sale deployment gate; staging->production promotion via separate Terraform PRs with human approval
execution_preference.rationale: Controlled rollout with validation checkpoints before each sale event

compliance_context.value: No regulatory compliance framework applies; internal operational controls sufficient. Infrastructure project handles no personal data or financial data. AWS Well-Architected Framework operational excellence pillar governs change management practices.
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=internal with no PII; risk_level=high due to operational blast radius (production outage risk during flash sale), not regulatory exposure

project.goal: Eliminate flash-sale traffic saturation through CDN, pooler, and autoscaling optimizations
project.target_users: SRE and platform engineers
project.timeline: Deployed before next sale event in 3 weeks

backend.api: No new API surface; tuning existing services

security.auth: IAM roles for automation pipelines; no public auth changes

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with PgBouncer connection pooling
data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode for catalog and session data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with OIDC IAM and Terraform plan/apply gated on PR approval
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate with ALB; CloudFront CDN

constraints.reversibility: all Terraform changes rollback-capable in under 5 minutes

open_questions.pool_size: Optimal PgBouncer server_pool_size for peak 10x traffic; TBD from k6 load test results

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is tightly focused on infrastructure bottlenecks rather than feature development. Success metrics must be defined as p99 API latency under 500ms at 10x baseline load and error rate below 0.1% before work is considered done.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PgBouncer transaction mode is the correct multiplexing choice but requires auditing the platform for advisory lock and LISTEN/NOTIFY usage before enabling. CloudFront cache behavior rewrites must segregate inventory endpoints at a short TTL to prevent stale stock during active sale windows.
reasoner_reviews.security: Terraform state must be stored in an S3 backend with SSE-KMS and DynamoDB locking restricted to the infra OIDC role. SCPs blocking manual console mutations must be validated before first live sale to prevent emergency workarounds that bypass audit trail.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Three-week timeline is achievable if k6 load tests are automated in CI against a staging snapshot. The 1:1 data volume requirement means a recent RDS snapshot restore must precede each test run.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous round plan underspecified PgBouncer server_pool_size calculation and left CloudFront TTL values as placeholders, making the plan non-actionable for SREs and triggering ISS-001 and ISS-002.

backend.service_design: PgBouncer 1.22 in transaction mode with server_pool_size=floor(max_db_connections/peak_task_count)=floor(200/40)=5; min_pool_size=5; max_client_conn=500 per task; server_idle_timeout=30s to release idle backend connections during post-sale ramp-down
backend.failure_handling: PgBouncer pool exhaustion returns HTTP 503 from API with Retry-After:5 header; ECS health check fails if PgBouncer port 5432 unreachable for 3 consecutive intervals; ALB drains connections before task replacement on scale-in
backend.scaling_notes: ECS target-tracking on ALBRequestCountPerTarget=1000, scale-out cooldown 60s, scale-in cooldown 300s, min_capacity=4, max_capacity=40; pre-warm via EventBridge scheduled rule 15 minutes before sale start
backend.backend_risks: Advisory locks (pg_try_advisory_lock) and pg_notify calls in checkout service are incompatible with PgBouncer transaction mode; must replace with Redis SETNX distributed lock and Redis pub/sub respectively before enabling transaction mode

security.secrets_management: Terraform remote state in S3 with SSE-KMS encryption and versioning; DynamoDB lock table restricts concurrent applies; GitHub Actions OIDC role with narrowly scoped ecs:UpdateService and cloudfront:CreateInvalidation permissions
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail captures all Terraform-triggered API calls with 1-year retention; ECS scaling events logged to CloudWatch with operator identity context; PgBouncer admin console access via SSM Session Manager only
security.security_risks: SCP blocking manual console changes must be tested in staging before production deployment to avoid emergency workarounds during a live sale outage bypassing change management

data.storage_design: ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode 3 shards for catalog cache; TTL 300s for product detail, 60s for inventory counts with jitter +/-15s to prevent stampede at sale launch; cache-aside pattern with DB fallback on miss
data.schema_notes: PgBouncer transaction mode requires server_reset_query=DISCARD ALL; ORM must not use server-side cursors or prepared statements in session scope; audit TypeORM query runner configuration before rollout
data.data_risks: Cache stampede when multiple catalog TTLs expire simultaneously at sale start; mitigate with Redis SETNX request coalescing and jittered TTL per product category

devops.deployment_topology: CloudFront distribution with ordered cache behaviors: /api/v1/cart/* (TTL 0 no-cache), /api/v1/inventory/* (TTL 10s), /api/v1/catalog/* (TTL 120s), /static/* (TTL 86400s); ECS Fargate API service 4-40 tasks with target-tracking
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: terraform-plan job posts plan diff as PR comment; terraform-apply requires manual approval gate for production workspace; k6 load test job runs after staging apply, asserts p99<500ms and error_rate<0.1% over 15-minute hold; blocks production PR on failure
devops.observability_stack: Datadog war-room dashboard: ECS task count, ALBRequestCountPerTarget, PgBouncer pool_available (alert<2), Redis cache hit ratio (alert<85%), RDS active_connections (alert>160), CloudFront cache hit ratio (alert<70%), p99 latency per endpoint
devops.rollback_strategy: Git revert of infra PR triggers terraform apply of previous state within 5 minutes; CloudFront CreateInvalidation clears stale behavior config immediately post-rollback

previous_plan.title: Flash-Sale Infrastructure Performance Tuning Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: Three-layer optimization targeting CDN cache rules, PgBouncer connection pooling, and ECS auto-scaling to handle 10x flash-sale traffic spikes.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: CloudFront cache rule rewrites plus PgBouncer transaction-mode pooling plus ECS target-tracking autoscaling on ALBRequestCountPerTarget.
previous_plan.system_components: ECS Fargate cluster, PgBouncer pooler, CloudFront distribution, ElastiCache Redis 7, PostgreSQL 16.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions Terraform pipeline for all changes; k6 load tests on staging before production promotion.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 by specifying the PgBouncer server_pool_size formula with a worked example, and ISS-002 by providing concrete CloudFront TTL values per endpoint class. Must also flag the advisory lock incompatibility with transaction mode as a required pre-deployment gate. Infrastructure project means no frontend specialist; active specialists are backend, security, data, and devops.

fix_report.action_taken: Added server_pool_size=floor(max_db_connections/peak_task_count) formula with worked example: max_connections=200, peak_tasks=40, server_pool_size=5. Added min_pool_size=5, max_client_conn=500. Added advisory lock audit as a mandatory pre-requisite gate before enabling transaction mode.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SREs can configure PgBouncer with concrete values derived from known DB limits and validate pool headroom before each sale event.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined four CloudFront cache behaviors with explicit TTLs: /api/v1/cart/* TTL 0s no-cache, /api/v1/inventory/* TTL 10s, /api/v1/catalog/* TTL 120s, /static/* TTL 86400s. Added cache-control header validation step to CI pipeline.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CDN configuration is immediately actionable and inventory staleness during active sale is bounded to 10s maximum.

target_output.title: Flash-Sale Platform Infrastructure Performance Optimization
target_output.executive_summary: A targeted infrastructure optimization initiative rewriting CloudFront cache behaviors with explicit per-endpoint TTLs, tuning PgBouncer 1.22 in transaction mode using a formula-driven pool_size calculation, and introducing ECS target-tracking auto-scaling to sustain 10x flash-sale traffic bursts with p99 API latency below 500ms. All changes are delivered via a Terraform-gated GitHub Actions pipeline with k6 load validation against a 1:1 staging snapshot before each production promotion.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer optimization on AWS: CloudFront distribution with four ordered cache behaviors segregating cart (TTL 0s no-cache), inventory (TTL 10s), catalog (TTL 120s), and static assets (TTL 86400s) to reduce origin hits during sale bursts. PgBouncer 1.22 in transaction mode fronting PostgreSQL 16 with server_pool_size=floor(max_db_connections/peak_task_count)=5, max_client_conn=500 per ECS task, eliminating connection exhaustion at 10x load. ECS Fargate API service scales 4-40 tasks via ALBRequestCountPerTarget=1000 target-tracking policy with pre-warm via EventBridge scheduled rule 15 minutes before sale start. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode 3 shards with jittered TTLs prevents cache stampede at sale launch. Terraform manages all configuration with S3 remote state and DynamoDB locking.
target_output.technology_stack: Infrastructure: Terraform 1.8, AWS CloudFront, ECS Fargate, ALB, ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode 3 shards, PostgreSQL 16 with PgBouncer 1.22. Load Testing: k6 with ramp-up profile (0 to 10x over 5 minutes, hold 15 minutes, ramp-down 5 minutes). Observability: Datadog APM with ECS sidecar, CloudWatch Metrics, PgBouncer admin stats scraped via exporter. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with OIDC IAM roles. IaC: Terraform 1.8 with S3 remote state backend.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CDN Cache Behavior Rewrites: four CloudFront behaviors ordered by path specificity with explicit TTLs and Cache-Control header passthrough. PgBouncer Transaction-Mode Pool Config: server_pool_size=5 (derived from 200 max_connections / 40 peak tasks), min_pool_size=5, max_client_conn=500, server_idle_timeout=30s. ECS Auto-Scaling Policy: ALBRequestCountPerTarget=1000, scale-out cooldown 60s, scale-in cooldown 300s, min=4, max=40. Pre-Warm Rule: EventBridge cron triggers ECS desired_count=40 fifteen minutes before sale start time. k6 Load Test Suite: ramp-up scenario targeting catalog and checkout endpoints with p99<500ms pass/fail gate. Datadog Sale-Event Dashboard: real-time panels for task count, pool_available, cache hit ratio, RDS active_connections, p99 latency.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront distribution with four ordered cache behaviors (/api/v1/cart/* TTL 0s, /api/v1/inventory/* TTL 10s, /api/v1/catalog/* TTL 120s, /static/* TTL 86400s). ALB with ECS Fargate API service (4-40 tasks, ALBRequestCountPerTarget=1000). PgBouncer 1.22 sidecar container per ECS task in transaction mode (server_pool_size=5, max_client_conn=500, server_idle_timeout=30s). PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (max_connections=200, 20 reserved for admin/replication). ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster-mode 3 shards for catalog and session cache. EventBridge scheduled rule for pre-warm 15 minutes before sale. S3 + DynamoDB Terraform remote state backend. GitHub Actions OIDC role scoped to ecs:UpdateService and cloudfront:CreateInvalidation.
target_output.workflows: Pre-Sale Load Test: SRE triggers k6 workflow against staging with 10x ramp-up profile; CI asserts p99<500ms and error_rate<0.1% over 15-minute hold phase; on failure, Terraform vars for server_pool_size or max_capacity are adjusted and test re-runs until gate passes. Production Promotion Gate: after staging validation passes, SRE opens production Terraform PR showing plan diff for ALB threshold, pool_size, and cache TTL resources; second SRE approves; merge triggers terraform apply; CloudFront cache invalidation for /api/v1/catalog/* primes fresh cache before sale window. Live Sale Monitoring: Datadog war-room dashboard shows ECS task count, PgBouncer pool_available, Redis hit rate, and RDS active_connections in real time; PagerDuty fires immediately if pool_available drops below 2 or error_rate exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive minutes.
target_output.data_model: cdn_cache_rules (rule_id, path_pattern, ttl_seconds, cache_control_passthrough, behavior_order, deployed_at, deployed_by). connection_pool_configs (config_id, environment, server_pool_size, min_pool_size, max_client_conn, server_idle_timeout, transaction_mode BOOL, applied_at, applied_by). autoscaling_policies (policy_id, service_name, metric_name, target_value, scale_out_cooldown_s, scale_in_cooldown_s, min_capacity, max_capacity, prewarm_offset_minutes). load_test_scenarios (scenario_id, name, target_rps_multiplier, ramp_duration_s, hold_duration_s, p99_threshold_ms, error_rate_threshold). performance_baselines (baseline_id, measured_at, environment, p50_ms, p99_ms, error_rate, active_db_connections, cache_hit_ratio, cdn_hit_ratio). traffic_spike_events (event_id, sale_name, started_at, peak_rps, peak_task_count, peak_db_connections, p99_at_peak_ms, incident_ids).
target_output.api_design: No new external API surface introduced. Internal ops endpoints (IAM SigV4 auth, not ALB-exposed): GET /internal/pgbouncer/stats returns pool_available, total_requests, avg_query_time_ms from PgBouncer admin console proxy. GET /internal/scaling/status returns current ECS desired_count, running_count, ALBRequestCountPerTarget. POST /internal/cache/invalidate with body {path_pattern, triggered_by} submits CloudFront invalidation for specified path pattern. GET /internal/performance/report?event_id= retrieves post-sale performance comparison against baseline.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Terraform remote state in S3 with SSE-KMS encryption, versioning enabled, and bucket policy restricting access to GitHub Actions OIDC role ARN. DynamoDB lock table prevents concurrent Terraform applies during sale event response. SCP blocks manual ECS and CloudFront console mutations for all roles except break-glass admin with time-limited session. PgBouncer admin console port 5431 accessible only via SSM Session Manager from bastion; no direct internet exposure. Datadog API key in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation. CloudTrail enabled for all Terraform-triggered API calls with 1-year retention in S3.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Staging environment mirrors production via automated weekly RDS snapshot restore to staging cluster at 1:1 data volume. GitHub Actions pipeline: terraform-plan job runs on PR open and posts plan diff as comment; terraform-apply job requires manual approval gate for production workspace. k6 load test job runs after staging apply: ramp 0 to 10x over 5 minutes, hold 15 minutes, ramp down 5 minutes; asserts p99_latency<500ms and error_rate<0.1%; blocks production PR creation on failure. CloudFront behaviors deployed via aws_cloudfront_distribution Terraform resource with ordered_cache_behavior blocks; behavior precedence validated in staging before production apply. Rollback: git revert of infra PR triggers terraform apply of previous state in under 5 minutes; CloudFront CreateInvalidation for /* clears stale behavior config immediately post-rollback.
target_output.observability: Datadog sale-event dashboard with panels: ECS task count (target range 4-40), ALBRequestCountPerTarget per-task, PgBouncer pool_available (critical alert below 2), PgBouncer total_requests per second, Redis cache hit ratio (warning below 85%), RDS active_connections (warning above 160 of 200 max), CloudFront cache hit ratio (warning below 70%), p99 API latency per endpoint path. PagerDuty: pool_available<2 triggers immediate P1 page; error_rate>0.5% for 2 consecutive minutes triggers P2 page. Post-event automated Lambda generates PDF report comparing actual vs projected throughput, peak task count, DB connection high-watermark, and cost delta.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Baseline ~$800/month (4 Fargate tasks, existing RDS Multi-AZ, Redis single-node). Upgrading Redis to cluster-mode 3 shards adds ~$120/month. Improved CDN hit ratio from 45% to 75% saves ~$80/month in data transfer. At 10x peak for 20-minute sale event, incremental Fargate cost is ~$12 per event. Pre-warm to 40 tasks 15 minutes before sale adds ~$8 per event. Net monthly delta for 4 sale events per month: +$120 Redis upgrade, -$80 CDN savings, +$80 pre-warm events, net +$120/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Pooler Readiness (Week 1): audit platform codebase for pg_try_advisory_lock and pg_notify usage in checkout and notification services; replace with Redis SETNX lock and Redis pub/sub; deploy PgBouncer 1.22 to staging with server_pool_size=5 transaction mode; run k6 baseline test validating pool headroom at 10x load. Phase 2 - CDN and Cache (Week 2): deploy CloudFront four-behavior config to staging; validate inventory TTL 10s accuracy during simulated stock depletion; run full 10x k6 test against staging; confirm all Datadog dashboard panels receive data. Phase 3 - Production and Pre-Warm (Week 3): promote all Terraform changes to production via gated pipeline; configure EventBridge pre-warm rule for next scheduled sale; execute 5x warm-up test in off-peak window; validate war-room dashboard before first live sale event.
target_output.development_guidelines: All infrastructure changes via Terraform PRs; direct AWS console modifications blocked by SCP except break-glass. PgBouncer config changes require k6 regression test pass before merge. CloudFront behavior order must be validated in staging as ordering affects cache precedence and cart no-cache must be first. Runbook required per Terraform module covering change purpose, rollback steps, and validation commands. Commit messages reference ISS- ticket IDs. Post-sale retrospective within 48 hours with Datadog performance report exported and attached.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PgBouncer transaction mode breaks existing pg_try_advisory_lock in checkout service. Mitigation: mandatory pre-deployment code audit and Redis SETNX replacement before enabling transaction mode. Risk: CloudFront 120s catalog TTL serves stale product prices if price-update API does not trigger invalidation. Mitigation: price-update service calls CloudFront CreateInvalidation for affected /api/v1/catalog/{product_id} paths on every price change. Risk: ECS scale-out takes 60-90 seconds; first minute of sudden spike may exceed 1000 req/task. Mitigation: EventBridge pre-warm rule sets desired_count=40 fifteen minutes before scheduled sale. Tradeoff: PgBouncer transaction mode vs session mode; transaction mode provides 8x connection multiplexing but requires removing all server-side cursors from reporting queries.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: PgBouncer server_pool_size set to 5 derived from max_connections=200 / peak_tasks=40. CloudFront TTLs finalized: catalog 120s, inventory 10s, static 86400s, cart 0s. k6 pass thresholds agreed with SRE: p99<500ms, error_rate<0.1%. Pre-warm strategy confirmed as EventBridge cron rule 15 minutes before each sale start time.

metadata.notes: Infrastructure performance optimization for flash-sale platform; round 2 addresses missing PgBouncer pool_size formula and CloudFront TTL values from round 1.

profile.domain: AI Fraud Detection for High-Volume Marketplace

project_goal.value: Score every marketplace transaction and seller listing in real time using ensemble ML models, triggering automated payment holds and routing high-risk signals to human review queues
project_goal.rationale: Core fraud prevention capability protecting marketplace revenue and buyer trust

target_users.value: Fraud analysts and risk operations teams consuming review queues; internal payment and listing services consuming the scoring API
target_users.rationale: Dual audience: human reviewers and downstream automated systems

project_class.rationale: Core product is ML inference at scale with model lifecycle management

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, realtime, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Requires real-time scoring, LLM-assisted feature extraction, historical analytics, and batch retraining

complexity_level.rationale: Must score millions of transactions daily with sub-200ms p99 latency SLA

risk_level.rationale: Automated payment holds affect real money; false positives cause merchant harm; algorithmic decisioning requires fairness review

data_sensitivity.rationale: Pipeline processes payment signals and card-adjacent transaction features

external_exposure.rationale: Scoring API consumed only by internal platform services, never exposed externally

access_model.value: IAM role-based service authentication; fraud analysts access review queue via internal SSO with MFA
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege service identity; human reviewers use audited SSO session

feature_scope.value: Real-time transaction scoring, listing fraud scoring, automated hold triggering, human review queue, case outcome capture, model performance dashboard, weekly retraining pipeline
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational lifecycle from scoring through retraining

mvp_scope.value: Real-time transaction scoring with SageMaker ensemble, automated hold at threshold, review queue with case disposition, Kafka consumer pipeline
mvp_scope.rationale: Core scoring and hold flow required for initial deployment

security_baseline.value: Encryption at rest AES-256 for all financial features; TLS 1.3 for internal service calls; all payment signal access logged; no raw card data stored
security_baseline.rationale: PCI-DSS controls for systems touching payment data

frontend_stack.value: none; internal review queue UI is a separate admin service not in scope
frontend_stack.rationale: ai_system with no frontend specialist; analysts use a separate internal tool

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for scoring API and feature service
backend_stack.rationale: Python native for ML ecosystem; FastAPI for async high-throughput endpoints

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for case outcomes and model versions; ClickHouse for fraud analytics and feature statistics
data_platform.rationale: Postgres for transactional records; ClickHouse for high-volume analytical queries over score distributions

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate for scoring API and feature service; SageMaker for model endpoint hosting
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for stateless services; SageMaker for managed model serving with A/B endpoint variants

privacy_retention_policy.value: Feature vectors retained 90 days for retraining; case outcomes retained 3 years for regulatory audit; raw transaction signals not persisted beyond feature extraction
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Balance retraining data needs with minimal payment data retention

future_scope.value: Graph-based collusion detection, real-time listing image analysis, self-service rule engine for fraud analysts
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 expansions after baseline model proves effective

constraints.value: Scoring p99 latency under 200ms end-to-end; SageMaker endpoint budget under $8k/month; no raw card PANs stored anywhere in the system
constraints.rationale: Latency SLA contractual; cost ceiling from risk budget; PCI hard requirement

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on scoring API; SageMaker Model Monitor for data drift and prediction distribution shifts; ClickHouse dashboards for fraud rate and hold rate by category
observability_baseline.rationale: Need operational and model-health observability in one stack

execution_preference.value: Iterative model releases with staged rollout via SageMaker A/B endpoint traffic splitting
execution_preference.rationale: Allows safe model promotion without full cutover risk

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS requirements for systems processing payment signals; fair lending and anti-discrimination review for algorithmic hold decisions; audit log required for every automated hold action
compliance_context.rationale: PCI-DSS scoping and fairness obligations for automated financial decisioning

llm_integration.value: LLM-based feature extractor analyzes seller listing text and buyer-seller message threads for social engineering patterns; uses GPT-4o via Batch API, output stored as structured risk signal vectors cached in Redis
llm_integration.rationale: LLM excels at detecting fraud narratives and urgency manipulation that rule-based signals miss

project.goal: Real-time ensemble fraud scoring for marketplace transactions and listings
project.target_users: Fraud analysts; internal payment and listing services

security.auth: IAM role service identity; SSO with MFA for analyst access
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.pci: No raw card data; tokenized feature vectors only

data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache

reasoner_reviews.product: The fraud scoring system addresses a critical revenue protection need. The dual-track approach scoring both transactions and listings is well-scoped for MVP. The review queue with case outcome capture creates the feedback loop necessary for model improvement over time.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Kafka-to-FastAPI-to-SageMaker pipeline is sound for this latency profile. Feature extraction must be computed in the consumer process, not in the SageMaker endpoint, to stay under 200ms. Redis caching of repeated entity features (same seller/buyer pair) will materially reduce SageMaker invocation frequency. The LLM feature extractor must run async and pre-cached -- synchronous LLM calls will blow the latency budget consistently.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI-DSS scoping requires that payment signals entering the pipeline are tokenized proxies, never raw PANs. Every automated hold action must write to an immutable audit log with the feature attribution that drove the decision. Fair lending review needs a bias monitoring job evaluating hold rates segmented by protected-class proxies monthly before any model promotion.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: SageMaker multi-model endpoint can host the ensemble and listing classifier with autoscaling on InvocationsPerInstance. At projected transaction volume, a single ml.g4dn.xlarge endpoint should fit the $8k/month ceiling. Kafka consumer group lag must be monitored -- lag growth is the leading indicator of scoring throughput problems.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan lacked automated model rollback and had LLM extractor in the synchronous scoring path. Both issues must be closed in this revision before the architecture is production-safe.

backend.service_design: Two FastAPI services: scoring-api (synchronous, handles inbound score requests from payment and listing services) and feature-service (async Kafka consumer, extracts features, calls SageMaker, writes results to Postgres and ClickHouse). Third service: llm-enrichment-service (async Kafka consumer on listing.published, calls GPT-4o Batch API, stores risk signals in Redis).
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/score/transaction with idempotency key returns {transaction_id, fraud_score, risk_signals[], hold_triggered}; POST /v1/score/listing returns {listing_id, fraud_score, policy_signals[]}. Internal ALB with AWS SigV4 auth. All endpoints return latency_ms field for SLA monitoring.
backend.failure_handling: SageMaker endpoint timeout falls back to rule-based score (velocity, account age, country risk) rather than blocking payment. Circuit breaker prevents cascade if SageMaker endpoint enters degraded state. LLM cache miss falls back to keyword heuristics, never blocks scoring path.
backend.scaling_notes: Feature-service horizontally scaled ECS tasks on Kafka consumer group lag metric. SageMaker endpoint autoscales on InvocationsPerInstance. LLM enrichment-service scales independently on listing.published lag.

security.auth_design: ECS task IAM role grants SageMaker:InvokeEndpoint and MSK produce/consume access. Scoring-api validates caller via AWS SigV4 on internal ALB listener. Analyst review queue access via Okta SSO with MFA enforced.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every hold_action record includes feature_attribution JSON, model_version, score, and threshold_at_decision in an append-only Postgres partition. No DELETE or UPDATE privilege granted on audit partition to any service role. CloudTrail logs all SageMaker API calls including model promotions.
security.privacy_controls: Feature vectors are statistical transforms of transaction signals; raw PANs never stored anywhere in the pipeline. IP addresses hashed after feature extraction and not stored in fraud_scores table.

data.entities: transaction_events (id, marketplace_txn_id, feature_vector JSONB, fraud_score, model_version, hold_triggered, scored_at); fraud_scores (id, entity_type ENUM transaction/listing, entity_id, score, risk_signals JSONB, feature_attribution JSONB, model_version, created_at); review_queues (id, entity_id, entity_type, score, priority, assigned_analyst_id, status ENUM pending/reviewing/resolved); case_outcomes (id, review_queue_id, disposition ENUM fraud/not_fraud/inconclusive, analyst_id, notes, resolved_at); model_versions (id, model_name, s3_artifact_path, f1_score, precision, recall, promoted_at, deprecated_at, sagemaker_variant).
data.storage_design: Postgres for case_outcomes, model_versions, and hold_actions audit partition. ClickHouse for fraud_scores analytical table partitioned by scored_at day. Redis 7 ElastiCache for entity feature cache (TTL 15min) and listing LLM signal cache (TTL 24h). S3 for feature vector snapshots used in retraining and model artifacts.
data.analytics_events: fraud_rate by category (holds/scores ratio), hold_rate_trend 24h rolling, analyst_resolution_rate, model_precision_recall_by_week, false_positive_rate by seller segment.

devops.deployment_topology: Scoring-api and feature-service on ECS Fargate behind internal ALB. LLM enrichment-service on ECS Fargate consuming listing.published Kafka topic. SageMaker multi-model endpoint with production and shadow variants on ml.g4dn.xlarge. Rollback Lambda triggered by CloudWatch alarm action.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest unit + model contract tests, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Separate SageMaker promotion pipeline: register model to Model Registry, create endpoint config with 10/90 shadow/production split, 24h shadow evaluation, promote or rollback based on F1 comparison.
devops.rollback_strategy: CloudWatch metric alarm on hold_rate_spike (>2x 7-day baseline for 5 consecutive 1-minute periods) triggers Lambda that calls SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint reverting to previous endpoint configuration within 90 seconds. PagerDuty critical alert published simultaneously.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on all ECS services with custom spans for kafka_consume_latency, feature_extraction_latency, sagemaker_invoke_latency. SageMaker Model Monitor hourly data quality checks. ClickHouse Grafana dashboard for fraud_rate and hold_rate.

ISS-001.title: No automated model rollback trigger on hold rate anomaly
ISS-001.detail: If a newly promoted model causes hold_rate to spike beyond 2x baseline, the only recovery path is a manual SageMaker console update. CloudWatch alarm exists but only notifies; it does not revert traffic weighting automatically.

history.note: Identified during architect review; rollback is entirely manual runbook

ISS-002.title: LLM extractor latency not bounded in synchronous scoring path
ISS-002.detail: The LLM-based listing text extractor is called synchronously in the feature-service Kafka consumer. GPT-4o p99 latency can reach 4-8 seconds, which violates the 200ms scoring SLA for listing fraud checks.

history.note: Critic raised during round 1 review; async batching design not yet present in plan

focus_issues.title: No automated model rollback trigger on hold rate anomaly
focus_issues.detail: Manual rollback only; no automated CloudWatch-to-SageMaker revert flow.

focus_issues.title: LLM extractor latency not bounded in synchronous scoring path
focus_issues.detail: Synchronous GPT-4o call in scoring path violates 200ms SLA.

latest_recommendations: Add automated SageMaker endpoint rollback triggered by CloudWatch hold_rate alarm exceeding 2x 7-day baseline -- Lambda action on alarm state change

Decouple LLM listing extractor into async pre-computation lane: consume listing.published event, call GPT-4o Batch API, store risk signals in Redis with 24h TTL; synchronous scoring path reads cache with rule-based fallback on miss

Case outcome feedback loop for weekly retraining well-defined

previous_plan.title: Marketplace Fraud Detection AI System Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Real-time ML fraud scoring system for marketplace transactions and listings using a SageMaker ensemble endpoint, Kafka consumer feature pipeline, and automated hold triggering with human review queues.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Kafka MSK receives transaction_event and listing_event messages; feature-service ECS tasks consume each topic, extract feature vectors, invoke SageMaker multi-model endpoint synchronously including GPT-4o LLM for listing signals, and write fraud scores to Postgres and ClickHouse.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS scoped with no raw PAN storage; feature vectors are statistical transforms; every automated hold action logged with feature attribution; IAM role service identity for all internal calls.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS rolling deploy via GitHub Actions. SageMaker model promotion via manual endpoint update. CloudWatch alarm on hold_rate notifies PagerDuty but does not trigger automated rollback.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving two high-severity issues from round 1. For ISS-001 (manual rollback): adding a CloudWatch metric alarm on hold_rate_7d_baseline_ratio that triggers a Lambda function calling SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint to revert traffic to the previous endpoint variant, eliminating the manual step. For ISS-002 (LLM latency): introducing a separate llm-enrichment-service that pre-computes GPT-4o listing risk signals on listing.published Kafka events and stores them in Redis with 24h TTL; the synchronous scoring path reads from cache with rule-based text heuristics as fallback, keeping p99 under 200ms. SageMaker A/B endpoint variants enable safe model promotion with automated rollback guard.

fix_report.action_taken: Added automated rollback pipeline: CloudWatch composite alarm evaluates hold_rate against 7-day rolling average every 1 minute; if hold_rate exceeds 2x baseline for 5 consecutive datapoints, alarm triggers a Lambda function that calls SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint to shift 100% traffic to the previous endpoint variant and publishes a PagerDuty critical alert with model version and hold rate delta. Lambda execution and endpoint update logged to CloudTrail.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model rollback completes within 90 seconds of alarm breach without human intervention; hold rate returns to baseline before significant merchant payment disruption

fix_report.action_taken: Decoupled LLM extraction into async pre-computation lane: new llm-enrichment-service ECS tasks consume listing.published Kafka events, call GPT-4o Batch API with listing title and description, parse structured risk_signals (urgency_score, prohibited_item_confidence, social_engineering_flag), and store as Redis hash listing:{listing_id}:risk_signals with 24h TTL. Synchronous listing scoring path reads from Redis cache; on cache miss applies rule-based text heuristics (keyword density, urgency phrase patterns, suspicious URL detection) as fallback signal. LLM is never invoked in the synchronous scoring path.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Scoring p99 latency remains under 200ms for all listing fraud checks; LLM enrichment covers >95% of listings within 30 minutes of publication at projected listing volume

target_output.title: Marketplace Fraud Detection AI System -- Revised Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale AI fraud detection system for a marketplace platform scoring millions of daily transactions and seller listings using a Python FastAPI feature pipeline, SageMaker ensemble endpoint, and a decoupled async LLM listing enrichment lane using GPT-4o Batch API. Automated payment holds trigger within the sub-200ms scoring path. Model promotion uses SageMaker A/B traffic splitting with CloudWatch-triggered automated rollback on hold rate anomalies, eliminating manual recovery procedures.
target_output.architecture_overview: Kafka MSK carries two topic groups: transaction_events (produced by the payments service) and listing_events (produced by the catalogue service). Two ECS Fargate consumer fleets process these independently. The transaction feature-service extracts velocity, behavioral, and graph features from Redis-cached entity state, invokes the SageMaker ensemble endpoint, and writes fraud_score records to Postgres and ClickHouse within a 180ms budget. A separate llm-enrichment-service consumes listing.published events asynchronously and calls GPT-4o Batch API to extract structured risk signals, storing results in Redis with 24h TTL. The listing scorer reads pre-computed signals from Redis -- never calling LLM synchronously -- and invokes the SageMaker listing classifier. Scores above hold_threshold trigger a hold_action Kafka message. SageMaker hosts production and shadow model variants; a CloudWatch alarm on hold_rate_spike triggers a Lambda that calls SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint to revert to the previous variant automatically within 90 seconds.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI async, boto3 SageMaker runtime, confluent-kafka-python. ML: XGBoost + LightGBM ensemble; GPT-4o via OpenAI Batch API for listing enrichment stored in Redis. SageMaker: multi-model endpoint on ml.g4dn.xlarge with autoscaling. Data: PostgreSQL 16 (case outcomes, model versions, hold_actions audit partition), ClickHouse (fraud_scores analytics), Redis 7 ElastiCache (entity feature cache TTL 15min, listing signal cache TTL 24h), Kafka MSK 3-broker 32-partition. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, SageMaker, CloudWatch, Lambda for rollback automation, ECR, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Real-time Transaction Scoring: Kafka consumer extracts velocity, graph, and geo features from Redis entity cache, invokes SageMaker ensemble, persists fraud_score with feature attribution, triggers hold_action if score exceeds threshold. Async LLM Listing Enrichment: listing.published consumer calls GPT-4o Batch API for risk signal extraction, stores signals in Redis. Listing Fraud Scoring: reads cached LLM signals from Redis with rule-based fallback on cache miss, invokes SageMaker listing classifier. Human Review Queue: priority-ordered queue of high-score cases assigned to fraud analysts with case disposition capture. Automated Model Rollback: CloudWatch alarm on hold_rate_spike triggers Lambda SageMaker endpoint revert. Weekly Retraining Pipeline: samples labeled case_outcomes, retrains ensemble, evaluates against holdout, promotes via SageMaker A/B variant if F1 exceeds current model.
target_output.system_components: Kafka MSK (3 brokers, 32 partitions, transaction_events and listing_events topics). Transaction feature-service (ECS Fargate, 4-16 tasks autoscaled on consumer group lag). LLM enrichment-service (ECS Fargate, 2-8 tasks, listing.published consumer, GPT-4o Batch API caller). Scoring-api (ECS Fargate, FastAPI, synchronous POST /v1/score endpoints). SageMaker multi-model endpoint (ml.g4dn.xlarge, production + shadow variants, InvocationsPerInstance autoscaling). Redis 7 ElastiCache (entity feature cache and listing signal cache). PostgreSQL 16 RDS (case_outcomes, model_versions, hold_actions append-only partition). ClickHouse cluster (fraud_scores analytics, daily partitions). Rollback Lambda (CloudWatch alarm action, SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint call).
target_output.workflows: Transaction Scoring: payment-service publishes transaction_event to Kafka; feature-service consumer extracts velocity features (txn count per buyer/seller in 1h/24h windows from Redis ZSET), graph features (buyer-seller pair history), and geolocation anomaly score; invokes SageMaker ensemble endpoint; persists fraud_score with risk_signals JSONB and feature_attribution; if score >= hold_threshold, publishes hold_action to Kafka consumed by payment-service -- total pipeline under 180ms. Listing Enrichment Lane: catalogue-service publishes listing.published; llm-enrichment-service calls GPT-4o Batch API with listing title and description, receives structured signals (urgency_score 0-1, prohibited_item_confidence 0-1, social_engineering_flag bool), stores as Redis hash listing:{listing_id}:risk_signals TTL 24h. Listing Scoring: sync scorer reads Redis for pre-computed signals; cache miss applies keyword density heuristics; invokes SageMaker listing classifier; persists listing fraud_score; sends listing_hold_action to catalogue-service on threshold breach. Automated Model Rollback: CloudWatch evaluates hold_rate_7d_baseline_ratio metric every 60 seconds; 5 consecutive breaches of 2x baseline trigger Lambda calling SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint reverting production to previous variant, publishing PagerDuty critical with hold_rate_delta and model version info.
target_output.data_model: transaction_events (id UUID PK, marketplace_txn_id TEXT UNIQUE, feature_vector JSONB, fraud_score FLOAT, model_version TEXT, hold_triggered BOOL, scored_at TIMESTAMPTZ). fraud_scores (id UUID PK, entity_type ENUM transaction/listing, entity_id TEXT, score FLOAT, risk_signals JSONB, feature_attribution JSONB, model_version TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). review_queues (id UUID PK, entity_id TEXT, entity_type TEXT, score FLOAT, priority INT, assigned_analyst_id UUID, status ENUM pending/reviewing/resolved, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). case_outcomes (id UUID PK, review_queue_id FK, disposition ENUM fraud/not_fraud/inconclusive, analyst_id UUID, notes TEXT, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_name TEXT, s3_artifact_path TEXT, f1_score FLOAT, precision_score FLOAT, recall_score FLOAT, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, sagemaker_variant TEXT). hold_actions (id UUID PK, entity_id TEXT, entity_type TEXT, fraud_score_id FK, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ, release_reason TEXT) -- append-only PCI audit partition, no DELETE/UPDATE privileges.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints on internal ALB with AWS SigV4 authentication. POST /v1/score/transaction {transaction_id, amount_cents, buyer_id, seller_id, payment_token_hash, geo_country} -> {transaction_id, fraud_score, risk_signals[], hold_triggered, latency_ms}. POST /v1/score/listing {listing_id, seller_id, title, category_id} -> {listing_id, fraud_score, policy_signals[], hold_triggered}. GET /v1/review-queue?status=pending&priority_gte=80&limit=50 -> {items[], total, cursor}. PATCH /v1/review-queue/{id}/disposition {disposition, notes} -> {id, disposition, resolved_at}. GET /v1/model-performance?model_version=v12&window_days=7 -> {f1, precision, recall, hold_rate, false_positive_rate}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS controls: payment_token_hash is a Stripe/Adyen token reference only -- no raw PANs enter the pipeline at any stage. All access to fraud_scores and hold_actions logged with requester IAM principal via CloudTrail. Hold_actions table is an append-only Postgres partition; no DELETE or UPDATE privilege granted to any ECS task role -- satisfies PCI immutable audit log requirement. Fairness monitoring: monthly batch job evaluates hold rates segmented by seller account age quartile and geography; output reviewed by risk compliance team before any model version is promoted. IAM least-privilege: scoring ECS task role grants SageMaker:InvokeEndpoint and ElastiCache read/write only; rollback Lambda role grants SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint on the fraud detection endpoint resource ARN only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS services deployed via GitHub Actions: pytest unit tests + model contract schema tests, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update with minimum 50% healthy during deployment. SageMaker model promotion pipeline: register model artifact to SageMaker Model Registry, create endpoint configuration with 10% shadow / 90% production traffic split, run 24h shadow evaluation comparing hold_rate and score_distribution against current production, promote to 100% if evaluation passes or trigger rollback pipeline. Automated rollback: CloudWatch composite alarm on hold_rate_7d_baseline_ratio (threshold 2.0, evaluation periods 5, period 60s) triggers Lambda ARN via AlarmActions; Lambda calls SageMaker:UpdateEndpoint restoring previous endpoint configuration within 90 seconds. Environments: dev (single ECS task, SageMaker serverless endpoint), staging (full Kafka MSK + ECS multi-task + SageMaker ml.g4dn.xlarge, synthetic load tests), production (multi-AZ ECS Fargate, MSK 3-broker, SageMaker autoscaling 1-4 instances on InvocationsPerInstance metric).
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces on all ECS services with custom spans: kafka_consume_latency_ms, feature_extraction_latency_ms, sagemaker_invoke_latency_ms, total_score_latency_ms (SLA alert if p99 > 200ms). SageMaker Model Monitor: hourly data quality check against baseline feature statistics; daily prediction drift check comparing score distribution to 7-day rolling baseline. ClickHouse dashboards: fraud_rate by category (holds triggered / scores), hold_rate_trend 24h rolling, analyst_resolution_rate, model_precision_recall_by_week. Kafka consumer group lag alert: PagerDuty warning if feature-service lag exceeds 10k messages. Redis listing signal cache hit rate tracked as custom metric; alert if hit rate drops below 90% (indicates enrichment-service lag).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: SageMaker ml.g4dn.xlarge autoscaling 1-4 instances ~$5-7k/month at projected transaction volume, within $8k budget ceiling. ECS Fargate feature-service and enrichment-service autoscale on Kafka lag metric ~$900/month. ClickHouse 3-node m5.xlarge ~$600/month. OpenAI Batch API for LLM enrichment at 50% discount vs real-time ~$280/month at 2M listings/month. Total infrastructure ~$7.8k/month. Future scaling: separate SageMaker endpoints for transaction and listing models if invocation contention observed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Transaction Scoring MVP (8 weeks): Kafka MSK setup, transaction feature-service with Redis entity cache, SageMaker ensemble endpoint v1 (XGBoost trained on historical labels), automated hold trigger, Postgres audit partition, basic review queue. Phase 2 -- Listing Fraud and LLM Lane (4 weeks): llm-enrichment-service consuming listing.published, Redis signal cache, listing fraud model on SageMaker, listing hold integration, rule-based fallback for cache misses. Phase 3 -- Operations Hardening (3 weeks): automated rollback Lambda with CloudWatch alarm, SageMaker A/B traffic splitting for safe promotion, fairness monitoring batch job, ClickHouse fraud rate dashboards, retraining pipeline with holdout F1 gate.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints enforced with mypy strict mode across all services. FastAPI endpoint request/response models use Pydantic v2 with strict mode. Model contract tests validate SageMaker endpoint input/output schemas on every deployment -- prevents silent schema drift. Feature extraction functions unit-tested with synthetic transaction fixtures covering velocity, graph, and geo signal edge cases; no real transaction data in test fixtures. SageMaker model promotion blocked in CI unless holdout F1 exceeds current production model by configured margin (default 0.01). Kafka consumer offsets committed only after successful fraud_score persistence to Postgres to prevent silent score loss on consumer restart.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Kafka consumer lag spikes during flash sale traffic bursts outpace feature-service autoscaling response time, causing scoring delays beyond SLA. Mitigation: pre-scale feature-service ECS task count 30 minutes before known high-traffic events using scheduled scaling actions. Risk: Redis listing signal cache cold start on service restart means newly published listings scored with rule-based fallback only for up to 30 minutes -- acceptable tradeoff for decoupled latency. Risk: SageMaker multi-model endpoint shared resource contention between transaction and listing classifiers at peak volume. Mitigation: monitor per-model InvocationsPerInstance metrics; split to separate endpoint instances if p99 for either model degrades. Tradeoff: GPT-4o Batch API introduces up to 1-hour processing delay on enrichment vs. real-time API -- acceptable for listing signals since listings are not single-use ephemeral events.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SageMaker multi-model endpoint over separate endpoints initially to control cost; will separate if invocation contention degrades transaction scoring p99. Chosen Batch API over real-time OpenAI calls for LLM enrichment to eliminate latency coupling and reduce per-call cost by 50%. Decided Redis as listing signal cache over Postgres to keep synchronous scoring read latency under 5ms. Weekly retraining frequency chosen to balance model freshness against case_outcomes label accumulation lag.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving LLM latency decoupling and automated SageMaker rollback for high-scale marketplace fraud detection.

profile.domain: Amazon FBA Sourcing Analytics Desktop Workbench

project_goal.value: Desktop workbench for professional Amazon FBA sellers aggregating 90-day sales rank history, FBA fee simulations, and competitor ASIN data in a local DuckDB store for offline product research and profitability analysis
project_goal.rationale: Sellers need a fast local tool for sourcing decisions without cloud latency or ongoing SaaS costs

target_users.value: Professional Amazon FBA sellers and sourcing analysts performing product research and supplier evaluation
target_users.rationale: Primary persona identified in user research as high-volume resellers managing 50-500 active ASINs

project_class.rationale: Local binary with Electron shell for native OS integration and offline-first DuckDB storage

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Electron UI, embedded Python FastAPI sidecar, DuckDB analytics, SP-API integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-process Electron+Python IPC architecture with SP-API rate-limit management and DuckDB columnar analytics

risk_level.rationale: Single-user local app with no server infrastructure; primary risk is SP-API terms of service compliance

data_sensitivity.rationale: Seller business data stored locally; no multi-tenant or PII concerns

external_exposure.rationale: App runs entirely on seller laptop; SP-API calls are outbound only

access_model.value: Single-user local app; no authentication layer; SP-API credentials stored in OS keychain via keytar
access_model.rationale: Single-user desktop with no multi-tenant requirement

feature_scope.value: ASIN batch import, 90-day sales rank history fetch via SP-API, FBA fee simulation engine, competitor ASIN tracking, sourcing lead scoring by ROI and sales velocity, CSV export, price-drop watchlist with OS desktop notifications
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for professional sourcing workflow

mvp_scope.value: ASIN import, sales rank fetch, fee simulation, sourcing lead list with ROI sort, CSV export
mvp_scope.rationale: Core value prop for initial release; watchlist alerts in v1.1

security_baseline.value: SP-API credentials stored in OS keychain via keytar; DuckDB database file encrypted with SQLCipher passphrase derived from machine ID; no network server ports opened
security_baseline.rationale: Protect seller credentials and proprietary sourcing data on shared machines

frontend_stack.value: Electron 32 with React 18 and TypeScript; Vite for renderer bundle; Tailwind CSS; Recharts for analytics charts
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in React; Electron provides native OS notification and keychain APIs

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI embedded sidecar process; communicates with Electron main process via local Unix socket IPC
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for SP-API SDK and pandas/numpy for fee calculation logic

data_platform.value: DuckDB embedded in Python sidecar for columnar analytics queries; no external database
data_platform.rationale: DuckDB excels at offline analytical queries over large ASIN history datasets without a server

hosting_target.value: Local desktop binary distributed via GitHub Releases; separate installers for macOS (DMG) and Windows (NSIS)
hosting_target.rationale: Direct distribution without app store to avoid review delays

privacy_retention_policy.value: All data stored locally; user controls retention via manual purge in settings; crash reports sent to Sentry with PII scrubbed
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Seller data is proprietary business intelligence; app must not upload it to any external service

future_scope.value: Multi-marketplace support (UK, EU, JP), supplier quote management module, Google Sheets export, cloud sync option for team accounts
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items after v1 validation with early access sellers

constraints.value: Must comply with Amazon SP-API terms of service rate limits; no scraping or unofficial API usage; app must run on macOS 13+ and Windows 11; binary size under 200MB
constraints.rationale: SP-API ToS violation risks seller account suspension; size limit for reasonable download UX

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting with PII scrubbing filter; local structured log file at ~/.fba-workbench/logs with 7-day rolling retention; no telemetry uploaded without explicit consent
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash visibility for developer team without compromising seller data privacy

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; GitHub Actions CI for automated builds and test runs; semantic versioning with GitHub Releases for distribution
execution_preference.rationale: Standard release cadence for desktop software with a small team

project.goal: Local desktop workbench for FBA sourcing research and profitability analysis
project.target_users: Professional FBA sellers and sourcing analysts

frontend.charts: Recharts for sales rank history and ROI distribution

backend.ipc: Unix socket between Electron main and Python sidecar

security.credentials: SP-API keys in OS keychain via keytar
security.encryption: DuckDB file encrypted with SQLCipher; no network ports opened

data.db: DuckDB embedded columnar store
data.external: Amazon SP-API for sales rank and catalog data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for build, test, and package
devops.hosting: GitHub Releases for macOS DMG and Windows NSIS installers

constraints.sp_api: Must respect SP-API rate limits; no scraping

open_questions.fee_calc: Should FBA fee simulation use cached fee rates or live SP-API rates? Cached with weekly refresh chosen.

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is well-validated for the professional FBA seller persona. The offline-first DuckDB approach is a strong differentiator over cloud-based tools. Watchlist alerts with OS desktop notifications are a high-value feature that should stay in v1 scope despite the recommendation to defer.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Electron + embedded Python sidecar via Unix socket IPC is a proven pattern for data-heavy desktop apps. The key architectural risk is SP-API rate limit management; a token bucket with per-endpoint tracking is essential to avoid hitting suspension thresholds during large batch imports of 500+ ASINs.
reasoner_reviews.security: SP-API credentials must use OS keychain via keytar, not a plaintext config file. DuckDB SQLCipher encryption is appropriate for protecting proprietary sourcing data on shared machines. Sentry must be configured with a beforeSend hook to scrub ASIN lists and financial figures before transmission.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Binary size under 200MB is achievable if the Python sidecar is packaged with PyInstaller excluding unused scipy/matplotlib deps. SP-API ToS compliance requires explicit rate limit tracking; failure to comply risks seller account suspension which is a critical user outcome risk.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan improved SP-API rate limit handling but still lacked a concrete DuckDB schema migration strategy for future columns and left the Sentry PII scrubbing filter unspecified. ISS-003 and ISS-004 remain open.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process manages Python sidecar lifecycle (spawn on app launch, kill on quit), keytar credential reads, and OS notification dispatch. Renderer process is a React 18 SPA with React Router for ASIN dashboard, sourcing leads table, fee simulator, and settings pages.
frontend.pages_and_flows: ASIN Import page: paste or CSV upload of ASIN list, progress bar during SP-API fetch batch. Sales Rank Dashboard: Recharts line chart of 90-day rank history per ASIN with category benchmark overlay. Fee Simulator: input buy cost and quantity, outputs net profit, ROI%, FBA fees breakdown. Sourcing Leads Table: sortable/filterable grid with ROI%, monthly sales estimate, rank trend, and watchlist toggle.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all FastAPI sidecar calls with stale-while-revalidate; Zustand for local UI state (selected ASINs, active filters, simulator inputs); no Redux
frontend.frontend_risks: Electron renderer memory pressure when displaying sales rank charts for 500+ ASINs simultaneously; mitigate with virtualized table rows and lazy chart rendering on scroll

backend.service_design: FastAPI sidecar with routers for /asin (batch fetch, snapshot), /fees (simulate), /leads (score, filter, export), /watchlist (alerts). SP-API client with per-endpoint token bucket rate limiter (catalog 5 req/s, sales rank 1 req/s). DuckDB connection managed as a singleton with WAL mode.
backend.api_patterns: All sidecar endpoints return JSON; Electron main process communicates via http+unix://socket path; no localhost port to avoid firewall prompts. Long-running batch fetches use Server-Sent Events for progress streaming to renderer.
backend.background_jobs: APScheduler in-process job runs weekly SP-API fee rate refresh, storing results in fee_rate_snapshots table. Watchlist monitor job runs every 15 minutes checking current sales rank against alert thresholds.
backend.backend_risks: SP-API Catalog Items API rate limit of 5 req/s means a 500-ASIN batch takes 100 seconds minimum; user must be shown accurate time estimate and progress during fetch

security.secrets_management: SP-API client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token stored in OS keychain via keytar; retrieved by Electron main process on startup and passed to Python sidecar via Unix socket handshake; never written to disk in plaintext
security.privacy_controls: Sentry beforeSend hook strips asin_list, buy_cost, sell_price, supplier_name fields from breadcrumb data before transmission; user must opt-in to crash reporting on first launch
security.security_risks: DuckDB SQLCipher passphrase derived from machine hardware ID is not resistant to targeted attacks by an OS-level adversary; acceptable risk for single-user local app

target_output.title: FBA Sourcing Analytics Workbench - Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: A local-first desktop workbench for professional Amazon FBA sellers built with Electron 32, React 18, and an embedded Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar communicating via Unix socket IPC. DuckDB provides columnar offline analytics over 90-day ASIN sales rank history, FBA fee simulations, and sourcing lead scores. SP-API integration is rate-limited via per-endpoint token buckets. DuckDB schema evolution is managed via versioned migration scripts applied at sidecar startup. Sentry crash reporting is opt-in with explicit beforeSend scrubbing of seller financial fields.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-process architecture: Electron main process manages app lifecycle, OS keychain reads via keytar, and desktop notification dispatch; Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar handles all SP-API calls, DuckDB reads/writes, fee calculations, and watchlist evaluation. Electron and Python processes communicate via a Unix domain socket at a well-known path to avoid firewall prompts and port conflicts. React 18 renderer queries FastAPI sidecar through a proxied IPC bridge using React Query with stale-while-revalidate. DuckDB embedded database with SQLCipher encryption stores asin_snapshots, sales_rank_history, fee_simulations, sourcing_leads, and watchlist_alerts locally at ~/.fba-workbench/data.db. GitHub Actions matrix builds produce macOS DMG and Windows NSIS installers with platform code signing.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 32, React 18 TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Recharts, React Query, Zustand, React Router v6. Backend Sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, DuckDB with SQLCipher, APScheduler, sp-api-sdk, python-jose. Security: keytar for OS keychain, SQLCipher for DuckDB encryption. Build: electron-builder, PyInstaller for sidecar bundle. CI/CD: GitHub Actions matrix build, Vitest, pytest. Observability: Sentry with beforeSend scrub filter.
