target_output.functional_feature_map: ASIN Batch Import: paste or CSV upload of up to 500 ASINs, parallel SP-API Catalog Items fetch with 5 req/s token bucket, progress stream via SSE to renderer. Sales Rank History Viewer: Recharts line chart of 90-day rank per ASIN with category average benchmark overlay. FBA Fee Simulator: input buy cost and quantity, compute net_profit, ROI%, FBA fulfillment fee, and referral fee using cached weekly fee rate snapshots. Sourcing Leads Table: sortable/filterable grid ranked by ROI%, with rank trend indicator and watchlist toggle. CSV Export: export filtered leads with all metrics to CSV for spreadsheet analysis. Watchlist Alerts: 15-minute background poll triggers Electron OS notification when sales rank crosses configured threshold.
target_output.system_components: Electron 32 main process (keytar credential read, Unix socket server, OS notification dispatch, sidecar spawn/kill). React 18 renderer SPA (ASIN import, rank dashboard, fee simulator, leads table, settings). Python 3.12 FastAPI sidecar process (SP-API client with token bucket, DuckDB singleton connection, APScheduler for weekly fee refresh and 15-min watchlist poll, Unix socket listener). DuckDB embedded columnar store at ~/.fba-workbench/data.db with SQLCipher. migrations/ directory with versioned SQL scripts and schema_versions tracking table. GitHub Releases for macOS DMG and Windows NSIS binary distribution. Sentry SDK in both Electron and Python processes with beforeSend scrub hook.
target_output.workflows: ASIN Batch Fetch: seller pastes 200 ASINs into import dialog; renderer POSTs to /asin/batch endpoint on sidecar; sidecar dequeues ASINs through SP-API token bucket (catalog 5 req/s, sales rank 1 req/s); each completed ASIN streams progress event via SSE; results written to asin_snapshots and sales_rank_history in DuckDB; renderer invalidates React Query cache on stream close and refreshes leads table. Sourcing Lead Evaluation: analyst filters leads table by ROI%>=30 and monthly_sales_est>=50; sorts by rank_trend_30d ascending to surface improving ASINs; selects top 20 for watchlist; exports filtered set to sourcing_leads_YYYYMMDD.csv for supplier outreach. Watchlist Alert: APScheduler job fetches current sales rank for watchlist ASINs every 15 minutes; compares against threshold in watchlist_alerts table; on breach, calls Electron main process via IPC to dispatch OS notification with ASIN title and current rank.
target_output.data_model: asin_snapshots (asin VARCHAR PK, marketplace VARCHAR, title TEXT, category VARCHAR, current_rank INT, bsr_category VARCHAR, rank_captured_at TIMESTAMP). sales_rank_history (asin VARCHAR, rank INT, captured_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (asin, captured_at)). fee_simulations (id UUID PK, asin VARCHAR, buy_cost DECIMAL, sell_price DECIMAL, quantity INT, fba_fee DECIMAL, referral_fee DECIMAL, net_profit DECIMAL, roi_pct DECIMAL, simulated_at TIMESTAMP). competitor_asins (source_asin VARCHAR, competitor_asin VARCHAR, relationship_type VARCHAR, added_at TIMESTAMP). sourcing_leads (id UUID PK, asin VARCHAR, score DECIMAL, roi_pct DECIMAL, monthly_sales_est INT, rank_trend_30d INT, status VARCHAR, updated_at TIMESTAMP). supplier_quotes (id UUID PK, asin VARCHAR, supplier_name VARCHAR, unit_cost DECIMAL, moq INT, lead_days INT, added_at TIMESTAMP). watchlist_alerts (id UUID PK, asin VARCHAR, alert_type VARCHAR, threshold_value INT, last_triggered_at TIMESTAMP, enabled BOOL). schema_versions (id INT PK, name VARCHAR, applied_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI sidecar endpoints (Unix socket, not HTTP port): POST /asin/batch {asins: string[]} returns SSE stream of fetch progress events. GET /asin/{asin}/history returns 90-day sales_rank_history array. POST /fees/simulate {asin, buy_cost, sell_price, quantity} returns fee_simulations object with roi_pct. GET /leads?min_roi=&min_monthly_sales=&sort_by= returns filtered sourcing_leads array. GET /leads/export returns CSV bytes for current filter. POST /watchlist {asin, alert_type, threshold_value} creates watchlist_alert. GET /watchlist/active returns enabled watchlist_alerts. All endpoints return JSON except /leads/export.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SP-API client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token stored in OS keychain via keytar; retrieved by Electron main on startup and passed to Python sidecar via Unix socket handshake message; never written to disk in plaintext. DuckDB database file encrypted with SQLCipher using a passphrase derived from machine hardware ID via node-machine-id; encryption applied at DuckDB open time. Sentry opt-in consent dialog on first launch; beforeSend hook strips asin_list, buy_cost, sell_price, supplier_name, roi_pct, unit_cost, net_profit from breadcrumb data and event extras before any transmission. No network server ports opened; all external calls are outbound SP-API requests only. Amazon SP-API usage restricted to authorized operations per ToS; no catalog scraping or unofficial endpoints.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix build on ubuntu, macos, and windows runners. Python sidecar bundled with PyInstaller (--onefile, exclude scipy/matplotlib) to app resources directory. electron-builder packages macOS DMG with Apple Developer code signing and Gatekeeper notarization; Windows NSIS installer with EV code signing certificate. CI pipeline: on PR run pytest for FastAPI sidecar tests (>80% coverage) and Vitest for React component tests; lint TypeScript; on main merge trigger release build workflow producing draft GitHub Release; on semver tag promote draft to public release with release notes. Auto-update via electron-updater checks GitHub Releases on launch. macOS notarization uses Apple notarytool with team credentials stored in CI secrets.
target_output.observability: Sentry crash reporting (opt-in) in Electron main process and Python sidecar with beforeSend scrub hook and release tagging for version correlation. Local structured log file at ~/.fba-workbench/logs/app.log with 7-day rolling retention; Winston logger in Electron, structlog in Python. SP-API rate limit metrics logged per endpoint: requests_made, tokens_remaining, throttle_events. DuckDB query timing logged for queries exceeding 500ms to detect table growth performance regression. No telemetry or usage data uploaded without explicit user consent.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure costs; app runs entirely on seller hardware. Development costs: GitHub Actions CI minutes (~$0/month on free tier for open source, or ~$20/month for private repo). Apple Developer Program $99/year for macOS signing. EV code signing certificate ~$400/year for Windows. One-time cost; scales to unlimited users with no additional infrastructure. Binary size target under 200MB: Python sidecar ~80MB PyInstaller bundle, Electron runtime ~120MB, leaving minimal headroom for assets.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core Data Pipeline (Weeks 1-4): Electron + Python sidecar IPC via Unix socket, SP-API token bucket client, DuckDB schema with migration runner, ASIN batch import with SSE progress, sales rank history storage and chart display. Phase 2 - Analytics and Leads (Weeks 5-7): FBA fee simulation engine with weekly fee rate refresh, sourcing leads scoring and filterable table, CSV export, competitor ASIN tracking. Phase 3 - Watchlist and Distribution (Weeks 8-10): watchlist alert system with OS desktop notifications, SQLCipher encryption, keytar credential storage, Sentry opt-in with scrub filter, electron-builder macOS and Windows installer pipeline, GitHub Releases auto-update.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python sidecar typed with mypy strict mode; pytest for all SP-API client, fee calculation, and migration runner logic (>80% coverage). TypeScript strict mode in renderer and Electron main. Vitest for React component tests covering ASIN import flow and leads filter logic. DuckDB migrations must be backward-compatible; no DROP COLUMN or RENAME COLUMN in minor versions. All SP-API calls must pass through token bucket; direct SDK calls outside the rate limiter are forbidden. Sentry beforeSend scrub list reviewed on every new field addition to fee_simulations or sourcing_leads tables.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SP-API access token revocation if rate limits are violated during large batch imports. Mitigation: per-endpoint token bucket with conservative limits below SP-API published thresholds; batch size capped at 500 ASINs per session. Risk: DuckDB WAL file corruption on hard process kill mid-write. Mitigation: CHECKPOINT before Electron quit event; periodic VACUUM in APScheduler job; user-accessible repair tool in settings invoking PRAGMA integrity_check. Risk: macOS Gatekeeper notarization failure blocks release pipeline. Mitigation: notarization step runs in separate retryable CI job; Apple Developer Program renewal alert in CI 30 days before expiry. Tradeoff: SQLCipher encryption with machine-ID passphrase vs no encryption; chosen for protection on shared machines at cost of non-portability of database file between machines.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: DuckDB migration strategy resolved: versioned SQL scripts in migrations/ with schema_versions table applied at sidecar startup. Sentry scrub fields finalized: asin_list, buy_cost, sell_price, supplier_name, roi_pct, unit_cost, net_profit. Fee rate caching confirmed: weekly SP-API refresh via APScheduler; cached rates used for all simulations. Watchlist poll interval set at 15 minutes balancing freshness and SP-API quota.

metadata.notes: Amazon FBA sourcing analytics desktop workbench; round 3 resolves DuckDB migration strategy and Sentry PII scrubbing specification.

project_goal.value: A mobile grocery ordering app for a regional quick-commerce startup scaling from 3 cities to 30, supporting tens of thousands of concurrent shoppers with dynamic slot availability and sub-hour delivery promises
project_goal.rationale: Core product enabling city-by-city geographic expansion with consistent delivery speed guarantees

target_users.value: Urban grocery shoppers aged 22-45 placing orders via iOS and Android apps; warehouse pickers fulfilling orders; delivery drivers receiving route assignments
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user personas with different app surfaces

project_class.rationale: Primary surface is React Native iOS/Android with native payment SDK integration

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, realtime, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires native mobile UI, order management backend, payment capture, real-time ETA updates, and driver integration

complexity_level.rationale: 30-city rollout with tens of thousands of concurrent shoppers and sharded city-level data architecture

risk_level.rationale: Handles payment card data, personal addresses, and real-time delivery operations at scale

data_sensitivity.rationale: Payment card tokenization and transaction history are core data assets

external_exposure.rationale: Consumer-facing mobile app with public App Store/Play Store distribution

access_model.value: Cognito user pools for shopper registration; separate Cognito pool for driver and picker apps; guest checkout not supported in MVP
access_model.rationale: Separate identity domains for consumers and operational staff

feature_scope.value: Product browse and search by city store zone, delivery slot selection with optimistic locking, cart management with real-time availability checks, checkout with Apple Pay/Google Pay and card, order tracking with live driver ETA, order history
feature_scope.rationale: Full shopper purchase journey for launch

mvp_scope.value: Product availability for 3 initial cities, slot selection and checkout with Stripe, order tracking with WebSocket ETA updates, Cognito auth, sharded PostgreSQL per city
mvp_scope.rationale: 3-city pilot to prove delivery promise before expansion

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic; Stripe tokenization for card data -- no PANs stored; JWT short-lived tokens 15min with refresh; certificate pinning on mobile clients
security_baseline.rationale: PCI SAQ A requirements and mobile client hardening

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript; Expo Router for navigation; react-native-stripe-sdk for payment sheet
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform React Native for shared iOS/Android codebase with native payment sheet

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with gRPC internal services and Envoy proxy; REST/JSON at API gateway for mobile clients
backend_stack.rationale: Go for high-throughput concurrent request handling; gRPC for internal service communication

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 sharded by city_id; Redis 7 ElastiCache for slot grid and session caching
data_platform.rationale: City-level sharding isolates data and limits blast radius for per-city incidents

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1; per-city shard on Aurora PostgreSQL clusters; Redis ElastiCache per region
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with Aurora managed failover per city

privacy_retention_policy.value: Order records retained 2 years; delivery addresses retained for 1 year post-last-order; payment methods retained until shopper deletion request; GDPR deletion within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Regulatory retention floors and GDPR right-to-erasure

future_scope.value: Subscription membership tier with priority slots, loyalty points, in-app chat with driver, alcohol age verification integration
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch growth features after 30-city scaling

constraints.value: City slot grid must be fetched and rendered under 800ms on 4G; payment capture must complete within Stripe's 30-second window; GDPR compliance for EU cities added in wave 2
constraints.rationale: User experience and compliance constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on all ECS services; RUM on React Native via Datadog React Native SDK; PagerDuty for slot availability errors and payment failures
observability_baseline.rationale: End-to-end visibility from mobile client through order placement

execution_preference.value: City-by-city phased rollout with canary deployments; each new city onboarded via a city_config record enabling routing to correct DB shard
execution_preference.rationale: Controlled expansion minimizes risk from each city launch

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ A for payment processing via Stripe Elements tokenization; GDPR Article 17 deletion rights for EU expansion cities in wave 2
compliance_context.rationale: PCI SAQ A applies because card data only touches Stripe-hosted payment sheet; GDPR for EU cities

project.goal: Mobile grocery ordering with sub-hour delivery for 30-city expansion
project.target_users: Shoppers, pickers, drivers

security.auth: Cognito user pools
security.payments: Stripe tokenization only, no PAN storage

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 sharded by city_id
data.cache: Redis 7 ElastiCache

reasoner_reviews.product: The shopper journey from slot selection through checkout is well-defined for MVP. City-sharded data model is the right approach for geographic isolation. Sub-hour delivery promise requires the slot locking mechanism to be bulletproof -- double-booking a slot is a critical failure mode that will drive negative reviews at launch.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with gRPC internal services is well-suited for the concurrency requirements at 30-city scale. The city-sharded PostgreSQL approach is sound for data isolation but requires a robust routing layer so the API gateway directs every request to the correct city shard without application-level shard key management bleeding into every service. The 10-minute optimistic slot lock is standard for quick-commerce -- ensure the expiry is implemented as a Postgres advisory lock or a Redis SETNX with TTL, not application-managed.
reasoner_reviews.security: PCI SAQ A scoping via Stripe payment sheet is the correct approach -- Stripe iframes handle card data entirely, keeping the app out of PCI scope for cardholder data. Certificate pinning on mobile clients is essential to prevent MITM attacks on the payment API. Cognito JWT refresh token rotation should be enabled. GDPR deletion pipeline must be designed from day one even if EU cities are wave 2.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Slot grid fetch under 800ms is achievable with Redis caching of the city slot grid refreshed every 60 seconds. Aurora city shards add latency if connection pooling (PgBouncer) is not in place -- cold connection establishment can cost 200-300ms per request at scale.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with Expo Router file-based navigation; three tab roots: ShopTab (product browse, cart), SlotsTab (slot picker, checkout), OrdersTab (active order tracking, history). Separate picker app and driver app are out of scope for this plan.
frontend.pages_and_flows: HomeScreen (city store zone product grid), ProductDetailScreen, CartScreen with real-time item availability polling, SlotPickerScreen with 2-hour grid rendered from Redis slot cache, CheckoutScreen with Stripe payment sheet, OrderTrackingScreen with WebSocket ETA display, OrderHistoryScreen.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for server state (product availability, order status); Zustand for cart state persisted to AsyncStorage; WebSocket connection managed via custom hook with reconnect logic.
frontend.frontend_risks: Slot grid stale state if shopper stays on SlotPickerScreen over 60 seconds without refresh. Certificate pinning requires per-release pin update -- pin rotation procedure must be documented.

backend.service_design: order-service (Go, handles cart, checkout, order lifecycle), slot-service (Go, manages slot grid and optimistic locks), catalog-service (Go, product availability per city shard), payment-service (Go, Stripe payment intent creation and capture), routing-service (Go, maps incoming requests to correct city shard config).
backend.api_patterns: REST/JSON at Envoy API gateway for mobile clients; gRPC for internal service communication. Slot lock: POST /v1/slots/{city}/{slot_id}/reserve returns lock_token valid 10 minutes. Checkout: POST /v1/orders uses lock_token, creates Stripe PaymentIntent, returns client_secret.
backend.failure_handling: Slot lock expiry handled by background Go routine sweeping expired locks every 30 seconds. Payment capture timeout: if Stripe webhook not received within 5 minutes of PaymentIntent creation, order moved to payment_pending_review status.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool per environment; JWT access tokens 15-minute expiry with refresh rotation enabled; certificate pinning public keys managed via CDK parameter store with rotation procedure.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting at Envoy: 60 requests/minute per authenticated user, 10 requests/minute per IP on auth endpoints. Slot reservation abuse detection: flag accounts attempting >5 slot reservations without checkout in a 1-hour window.
security.privacy_controls: Delivery addresses encrypted at rest with KMS. GDPR deletion: soft-delete shopper record, anonymize order delivery addresses, trigger Stripe customer deletion within 30-day SLA.

data.entities: shopper_accounts (id, cognito_sub, email, phone_encrypted, default_address_id, city_shard_id, created_at); store_zones (id, city_id, name, warehouse_id, active); product_availability (id, zone_id, sku, display_name, price_cents, available_qty, updated_at); delivery_slots (id, zone_id, window_start, window_end, capacity, reserved_count, status ENUM open/full/closed); cart_sessions (id, shopper_id, zone_id, items JSONB, expires_at); grocery_orders (id, shopper_id, zone_id, slot_id, items_snapshot JSONB, subtotal_cents, status ENUM placed/picking/out_for_delivery/delivered/cancelled, stripe_payment_intent_id); driver_assignments (id, order_id, driver_id, assigned_at, picked_up_at, delivered_at).
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL cluster per city group (groups of 5-6 cities per cluster initially); Redis ElastiCache for slot grid (HASH per zone per day refreshed every 60s) and cart sessions (TTL 30min); S3 for order receipt PDFs.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate services: order-service, slot-service, catalog-service, payment-service, routing-service, each behind Envoy proxy ALB. Aurora PostgreSQL per city group with Multi-AZ. Redis ElastiCache per region with cluster mode.
devops.environments: dev (single-shard, Stripe test mode), staging (3-city shard config, Stripe test mode, canary traffic split), production (per-city shard routing, Stripe live, Buildkite pipeline).
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite: Go unit tests + race detector, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Mobile: EAS Build for React Native iOS/Android builds; EAS Submit for App Store and Play Store releases. City onboarding: insert city_config record and provision Aurora shard via Terraform module.
devops.devops_risks: Aurora shard provisioning for new cities takes 15-20 minutes -- city launch must be pre-staged at least 1 hour before soft launch time.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a city-sharded mobile quick-commerce platform for geographic expansion from 3 to 30 cities. Key architectural decisions: PostgreSQL sharded by city_id to isolate blast radius and enable per-city Aurora provisioning via Terraform; Redis slot grid caching to hit 800ms fetch SLA at scale; optimistic slot lock via Redis SETNX with 10-minute TTL to prevent double-booking without database contention; Stripe payment sheet for PCI SAQ A compliance keeping card data out of the app entirely. Weak quality intentional: workflows and deployment sections are thinner than ideal.
target_output.title: Quick-Commerce Grocery App Architecture -- 30-City Scale
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale mobile grocery ordering platform built with React Native 0.74 and a Go microservices backend on AWS ECS Fargate, supporting tens of thousands of concurrent shoppers across 30 cities with sub-hour delivery promises. PostgreSQL is sharded by city to isolate data and scale city-by-city. Delivery slot availability is cached in Redis with optimistic 10-minute locking at checkout. Stripe payment sheet handles card capture keeping the platform in PCI SAQ A scope. Real-time driver ETA is pushed to shopper apps via WebSocket.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native mobile app communicates via HTTPS to an Envoy API gateway on ECS Fargate that routes requests to Go microservices. A routing-service reads the city_shard_id from the shopper JWT and injects the correct Aurora PostgreSQL shard DSN for downstream services. Slot availability is served from Redis ElastiCache refreshed every 60 seconds by the slot-service; checkout reserves a slot via Redis SETNX with 10-minute TTL. The payment-service creates a Stripe PaymentIntent and returns the client_secret to the mobile payment sheet -- no card data touches the backend. On order confirmation, an order-dispatched event triggers driver assignment via the routing-service. WebSocket connections to a dedicated ETA service push driver location updates to shopper devices.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React Native 0.74 TypeScript, Expo Router, TanStack Query, Zustand, react-native-stripe-sdk, Datadog React Native RUM SDK. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC for internal service calls, Envoy proxy as API gateway, REST/JSON at gateway edge. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 sharded by city_id group, Redis 7 ElastiCache for slot grid and sessions. Auth: AWS Cognito user pools with JWT 15min access tokens and refresh rotation. Payments: Stripe Elements payment sheet (SAQ A scope). CI/CD: Buildkite for backend, EAS Build/Submit for mobile. Monitoring: Datadog APM + RUM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: City Store Zone Browse: product availability served from city shard with Redis TTL-based freshness. Delivery Slot Selection: slot grid rendered from Redis HASH refreshed every 60s; shopper selects window. Slot Reservation: Redis SETNX lock with 10-minute TTL returned as lock_token. Cart Management: items persisted in Zustand local state synced to cart_sessions Redis TTL 30min. Checkout: lock_token validated, Stripe PaymentIntent created, client_secret returned for payment sheet. Order Tracking: WebSocket channel per order_id, driver location pushed every 15 seconds. Order History: paginated query on grocery_orders by shopper_id from city shard.
target_output.system_components: Envoy proxy ALB (API gateway, rate limiting, shard routing header injection). routing-service (city_shard_id resolution from JWT, shard config registry). slot-service (slot grid management, Redis SETNX locking, capacity enforcement). order-service (order lifecycle, cart management). payment-service (Stripe PaymentIntent creation, webhook handler for capture confirmation). catalog-service (product availability per zone from city shard). ETA service (WebSocket connections, driver location relay). Aurora PostgreSQL per city group (Multi-AZ). Redis ElastiCache per region (slot grid, sessions).
target_output.workflows: Slot Selection and Checkout: shopper opens SlotPickerScreen; app fetches slot grid from Redis via catalog-service returning available windows for the city zone; shopper selects 2-hour window; POST /v1/slots/{city}/{slot_id}/reserve acquires Redis SETNX lock returning lock_token valid 10min; shopper confirms cart on CheckoutScreen; payment-service creates Stripe PaymentIntent with order amount; payment sheet collects card in Stripe iframe; Stripe confirms capture; webhook updates grocery_orders.status to placed. Order Fulfillment and ETA: order-service emits order.placed event; driver assignment service assigns nearest available driver; ETA service opens WebSocket channel for order_id; driver app sends location pings every 15 seconds; ETA service relays updated ETA to shopper WebSocket connection until delivered status.
target_output.data_model: shopper_accounts (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT UNIQUE, email_encrypted TEXT, phone_encrypted TEXT, city_shard_id INT, created_at). store_zones (id UUID PK, city_id INT, name TEXT, warehouse_id UUID, active BOOL). product_availability (id UUID PK, zone_id FK, sku TEXT, display_name TEXT, price_cents INT, available_qty INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). delivery_slots (id UUID PK, zone_id FK, window_start TIMESTAMPTZ, window_end TIMESTAMPTZ, capacity INT, reserved_count INT, status ENUM open/full/closed). grocery_orders (id UUID PK, shopper_id FK, zone_id FK, slot_id FK, items_snapshot JSONB, subtotal_cents INT, status ENUM placed/picking/out_for_delivery/delivered/cancelled, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). driver_assignments (id UUID PK, order_id FK, driver_id UUID, assigned_at, picked_up_at, delivered_at).
target_output.api_design: REST/JSON at Envoy gateway: GET /v1/cities/{city_id}/slots returns slot grid for city zone. POST /v1/slots/{city_id}/{slot_id}/reserve {shopper_id} -> {lock_token, expires_at}. GET /v1/catalog/{zone_id}/products?page=1&limit=50 -> paginated product list with availability. POST /v1/orders {lock_token, cart_items[], payment_intent_id} -> {order_id, status}. GET /v1/orders/{order_id}/tracking -> {status, eta_minutes, driver_location_lat, driver_location_lng}. POST /v1/stripe/webhook (Stripe event handler, IP allowlisted to Stripe CIDR). All endpoints require Cognito JWT Bearer token except webhook.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI SAQ A scope: Stripe Elements payment sheet renders in Stripe-hosted iframe; react-native-stripe-sdk never exposes raw card data to application code. No card PANs stored in any Aurora shard or Redis cluster. Certificate pinning via react-native-ssl-pinning with pin hashes stored in CDK Parameter Store; rotation procedure documented for each mobile release. Cognito JWT 15-minute access tokens with refresh rotation; rate limiting at Envoy 60 req/min per authenticated user. GDPR deletion pipeline (wave 2 EU cities): soft-delete shopper, anonymize delivery addresses, call Stripe customer delete API within 30 days of deletion request.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Buildkite pipeline: Go unit tests with race detector, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update. Mobile builds: EAS Build generates iOS and Android binaries; EAS Submit to App Store and Play Store. City onboarding: Terraform module provisions Aurora PostgreSQL shard for city group, Redis cluster, ECS service config; city_config record inserted to enable routing. Environments: dev (single shard, Stripe test mode), staging (3-city shards, load test with k6), production (per-city shard routing, Stripe live). Aurora Multi-AZ per city group with automated failover. Redis ElastiCache with automatic failover enabled.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on all ECS Go services: slot_reserve_latency, order_checkout_latency, payment_intent_creation_latency, stripe_webhook_processing_latency. Datadog RUM on React Native: slot_fetch_duration (target < 800ms), payment_sheet_open_time, checkout_completion_rate. PagerDuty alerts: slot reservation error rate > 1%, payment capture failure rate > 0.5%, WebSocket connection drop rate > 5%, Kafka consumer lag (if applicable). Redis cache hit rate for slot grid tracked; alert if hit rate < 95%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Aurora PostgreSQL per city group ~$300-400/month per cluster (Multi-AZ db.r6g.large). Redis ElastiCache per region ~$200/month (cache.r6g.large). ECS Fargate microservices ~$600/month baseline autoscaling on CPU/request count. Stripe fees ~2.9%+30c per transaction standard. At 30 cities, estimated infrastructure ~$8-10k/month. Slot reservation contention at city level managed by Redis capacity counter; Aurora read replicas added per city group when read load exceeds primary threshold.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- 3-City MVP (10 weeks): React Native app with slot browse, reserve, Stripe checkout, order tracking. Go services: slot-service, order-service, payment-service, catalog-service. 3 Aurora city shards. Stripe test to live. Buildkite + EAS pipelines. Phase 2 -- 10-City Expansion (6 weeks): city onboarding Terraform module, driver assignment service, ETA WebSocket service, Datadog RUM rollout. Phase 3 -- 30-City Scale (8 weeks): per-region Redis ElastiCache, Aurora read replicas per high-traffic city, GDPR deletion pipeline for EU cities, subscription tier groundwork.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go services: golangci-lint in CI, table-driven unit tests for slot locking and checkout state machines. React Native: TypeScript strict mode, component tests via React Native Testing Library for checkout flow. Stripe webhook handler must validate Stripe-Signature header before processing; webhook idempotency key stored in Postgres to prevent duplicate order creation on retry. Aurora shard routing must be validated in integration tests using a multi-shard test fixture before any city onboarding. Pin rotation runbook required in repo before App Store submission.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis SETNX slot lock lost on ElastiCache failover during slot reservation. Mitigation: slot reservation writes a fallback lock record to Postgres with the same TTL as the Redis lock; checkout validates both. Risk: Aurora shard connection pool exhaustion at peak city launch traffic. Mitigation: PgBouncer sidecar on ECS tasks, max_connections tuned per shard; connection count alert in Datadog. Risk: city_shard_id in JWT becomes stale if shopper moves cities. Mitigation: shard assignment re-evaluated at login; city preference can be updated and JWT refreshed. Tradeoff: city-level sharding over global multi-tenant database for blast radius isolation at the cost of cross-city reporting complexity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed city-sharded Aurora over single global database for per-city isolation and blast radius control. Chosen Redis SETNX over PostgreSQL advisory locks for slot reservation to avoid database contention at checkout peak. Stripe SAQ A scope confirmed with Stripe Elements -- no in-app PAN handling required. EAS Build selected over custom CI for React Native to avoid native SDK dependency management.

metadata.notes: First-pass weak-quality plan for quick-commerce grocery mobile app; some sections thinner than optimal, limited detail in deployment and cost sections.

profile.domain: Subscription Box E-Commerce Global Multi-Region Deployment

project_goal.value: Establish active-active multi-region AWS infrastructure in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 for a subscription box e-commerce company launching globally, with subscriber data residency controls, automated Route 53 failover, and CockroachDB multi-region replication enforcing EU data isolation
project_goal.rationale: Global launch requires EU subscriber data to remain within eu-west-1 per GDPR and Schrems II; US data must stay in us-east-1

target_users.value: Global subscribers of the subscription box service; platform engineering and SRE teams operating the multi-region infrastructure
target_users.rationale: Infrastructure supports the subscriber-facing checkout and account platform

project_class.rationale: Greenfield multi-region network and data infrastructure with no new application features

capabilities.value: backend, devops, data
capabilities.rationale: Multi-region ECS deployments, Terraform automation, CockroachDB multi-region, and data residency controls

complexity_level.rationale: Active-active multi-region with global database, automated failover, subscriber home-region assignment, and GDPR data residency enforcement

risk_level.rationale: GDPR cross-border data transfer violations carry fines up to 4% of annual revenue; data residency misconfiguration is high severity

data_sensitivity.rationale: Subscriber names, shipping addresses, payment tokens, and subscription preferences are personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Subscriber-facing platform is publicly accessible via Route 53 global routing

access_model.value: Subscribers authenticated via Cognito regional user pools; API access via JWT with region claim; infra management via IAM roles with cross-region deny conditions
access_model.rationale: Region-scoped JWT claims enforce data residency at API layer

feature_scope.value: Active-active ECS Fargate API deployments in us-east-1 and eu-west-1, Route 53 latency-based routing with health check failover, CockroachDB multi-region cluster with restricted follower reads for EU partition, subscriber home-region assignment at signup, automated failover runbook, data residency audit logging
feature_scope.rationale: Complete scope for global launch infrastructure

mvp_scope.value: CockroachDB multi-region cluster with EU partition isolation, ECS Fargate API deployed in both regions, Route 53 latency routing active, subscriber home-region assignment at signup
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum required for GDPR-compliant global launch

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all inter-region and client-facing traffic; VPC peering with private subnets; IAM cross-region deny SCPs; CockroachDB RESTRICTED follower reads for EU partition; KMS per-region encryption keys
security_baseline.rationale: Layered controls to enforce data residency at network, database, and IAM levels

frontend_stack.value: none (infrastructure project; existing application platform consumes this infrastructure)
frontend_stack.rationale: Frontend is a separately deployed React application not in scope for this infrastructure initiative

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 existing platform deployed to both regions; CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region client via pgx driver
backend_stack.rationale: Existing Node.js platform extended to second region; CockroachDB chosen for multi-region active-active write capability

data_platform.value: CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region cluster with us-east1 and eu-west1 regional tables; Redis 7 regional ElastiCache clusters; no cross-region cache replication
data_platform.rationale: CockroachDB provides native multi-region SQL with RESTRICTED follower reads for EU data isolation; Redis is regional-only by design

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 and eu-west-1; Route 53 with latency-based routing and health check failover; CloudFront with regional origins
hosting_target.rationale: AWS chosen for existing account relationship and VPC infrastructure maturity

privacy_retention_policy.value: EU subscriber personal data retained in eu-west-1 partition only; US subscriber data in us-east-1 partition only; 3-year retention for subscription history; 90-day retention for access logs; right-to-erasure implemented via subscriber deletion workflow
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization and storage limitation principles; Schrems II cross-border transfer prohibition

future_scope.value: ap-southeast-1 region for APAC expansion; read-only replica for analytics workloads; CDN edge caching for product catalog
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after 6-month global launch validation

constraints.value: EU subscriber personal data must never replicate to us-east-1; all Terraform in monorepo with per-region workspaces; zero downtime during region bootstrap; CockroachDB cluster must tolerate single-node failure without data loss
constraints.rationale: Hard GDPR constraint and operational resilience requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog with multi-region APM; cross-region replication lag metric; Route 53 health check status dashboard; CockroachDB DB Console metrics; PagerDuty for replication lag >5s and region failover events
observability_baseline.rationale: Replication lag and regional health are the primary operational signals for this infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Terraform apply per region in sequence (us-east-1 first, eu-west-1 second) with validation checkpoint between regions
execution_preference.rationale: Sequential bootstrap reduces blast radius; us-east-1 serves as control plane baseline

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 44 cross-border transfer prohibition for EU subscriber data; Schrems II Standard Contractual Clauses required if any EU data touches us-east-1; DPA with CockroachDB as data processor
compliance_context.rationale: Global launch into EU requires GDPR compliance as a hard launch gate

project.goal: Active-active multi-region AWS infrastructure with GDPR-compliant EU data residency
project.target_users: Global subscribers and platform SRE team

backend.api: Existing platform API; no new endpoints in scope

security.auth: Cognito regional user pools with region claim in JWT
security.transport: TLS 1.3; VPC private subnets; IAM cross-region deny SCPs

data.db: CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region with EU partition RESTRICTED follower reads
data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 regional clusters; no cross-region replication

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with per-region Terraform workspaces
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate us-east-1 and eu-west-1; Route 53 latency routing

constraints.data_residency: EU personal data must never replicate to us-east-1
constraints.failover: Route 53 health check automated failover; 60s TTL

open_questions.crdb_version: CockroachDB 23.2 vs 24.1 for multi-region RESTRICTED follower reads; 23.2 chosen for stability

reasoner_reviews.product: Active-active multi-region is the correct architecture for a simultaneous global launch. Latency-based routing ensures EU subscribers hit eu-west-1 naturally. The subscriber home-region assignment at signup is essential for data residency enforcement and must be immutable post-assignment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: CockroachDB RESTRICTED follower reads on the eu-west-1 partition is the correct mechanism for GDPR data isolation at the database layer. The region-aware JWT claim provides defense-in-depth at the API layer. Must ensure CockroachDB zone configurations are applied via Terraform and validated before launch, not during incident response.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR requires that EU subscriber data not be processed in or accessible from us-east-1 nodes. CockroachDB RESTRICTED follower reads prevent cross-region reads. IAM SCPs with explicit deny on cross-region data service calls add a second layer. A DPA with CockroachDB as data processor must be signed before data ingestion begins.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Eight-week timeline is achievable for the infrastructure bootstrap with sequential region apply. CockroachDB multi-region cluster setup including zone configuration validation should be allocated 2 full weeks. The right-to-erasure workflow must be implemented before launch as it is a GDPR hard requirement.

backend.service_design: Node.js 22 ECS Fargate service deployed identically to us-east-1 and eu-west-1 via separate Terraform workspaces sharing a common module. Region-aware request routing: API reads subscriber_region claim from JWT and uses the corresponding CockroachDB connection pool targeting the local region gateway.
backend.failure_handling: Route 53 health checks poll /health endpoint every 10s with 3-failure threshold; on failure, DNS TTL 60s routes traffic to healthy region. ECS service minimum 2 tasks per region; ALB health check deregisters unhealthy tasks. CockroachDB circuit breaker in Node.js client falls back to read-only mode if local node unavailable.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS target-tracking on CPU 60% per region independently; min 2 tasks, max 20 tasks per region. CockroachDB scales horizontally with node additions; zone configuration ensures EU data remains on eu-west1 nodes.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool per region (us-east-1 and eu-west-1); at signup, subscriber assigned home region based on billing address country; region claim embedded in Cognito custom attribute and propagated to JWT. Cross-region token validation denied by API middleware.
security.authorization_model: Subscriber JWT includes region claim; API middleware rejects requests where subscriber region differs from serving region during normal operation; exception flow exists for runbook-driven migration only.
security.secrets_management: CockroachDB credentials and Cognito client secrets in AWS Secrets Manager per region; GitHub Actions OIDC roles with per-region resource ARN scope; no cross-region secret access.
security.security_risks: CockroachDB zone configuration misconfiguration could allow EU partition data to be read by us-east1 gateway nodes; must validate zone config after every schema change and Terraform apply.

data.entities: region_configs (region_id, aws_region, crdb_gateway_endpoint, cognito_pool_id, active), data_residency_policies (policy_id, subscriber_region, allowed_crdb_nodes, restricted_read_enabled, validated_at), replication_lag_metrics (measured_at, from_region, to_region, lag_ms, node_id), subscriber_region_assignments (subscriber_id, home_region, assigned_at, assignment_basis)
data.storage_design: CockroachDB multi-region cluster: 3 nodes in us-east1, 3 nodes in eu-west1. subscriber_region_assignments table uses REGIONAL BY ROW with crdb_region column; EU rows pinned to eu-west1 nodes with RESTRICTED follower reads preventing us-east1 access. regional ElastiCache Redis 7 clusters with no cross-region replication; session tokens are region-local.
data.retention_and_deletion: Subscriber deletion workflow issues DELETE on subscriber_region_assignments for home region partition; cascades to subscription_history and personal_data tables; deletion logged to audit_deletions table; right-to-erasure confirmed within 30 days per GDPR Article 17.
data.data_risks: CockroachDB replication lag during high write volume could delay cross-region consistency for non-restricted data; monitor lag_ms metric with alert threshold 5s.

devops.deployment_topology: Terraform monorepo with modules/multi-region-stack reused by workspaces/us-east-1 and workspaces/eu-west-1. Route 53 hosted zone with two latency-based A records pointing to regional ALBs; health check failover policy with 60s TTL. CloudFront with regional origins for static assets.
devops.environments: staging: us-east-1 only, single-region CockroachDB, used for application and migration testing. production: both regions, full multi-region CockroachDB cluster, Route 53 active.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR run terraform plan for both workspaces; on main merge apply us-east-1 workspace then run integration tests; if passed apply eu-west-1 workspace. CockroachDB zone configuration validated via crdb SQL check after each apply.
devops.rollback_strategy: Per-region Terraform workspace rollback via git revert; CockroachDB zone configuration rollback by re-applying previous zone config SQL; Route 53 failover can be forced manually via AWS console for emergency region isolation.
devops.devops_risks: CockroachDB multi-region cluster bootstrap requires coordinated node join sequencing; partial bootstrap leaves cluster in split-brain risk state; must complete full 6-node cluster before enabling zone configurations.

target_output.thinking_summary: Greenfield multi-region infrastructure for GDPR-compliant global launch. Key decisions: CockroachDB REGIONAL BY ROW with RESTRICTED follower reads for EU partition isolation enforces data residency at database layer; Cognito per-region pools with home-region JWT claim adds API-layer defense-in-depth; sequential Terraform apply (us-east-1 first) reduces bootstrap blast radius; Route 53 latency routing with 60s TTL provides automated failover. No frontend in scope. Compliance context required because risk_level=high and data_sensitivity=personal.
target_output.title: Global Multi-Region Infrastructure: Active-Active AWS Deployment with GDPR Data Residency
target_output.executive_summary: A greenfield active-active multi-region AWS infrastructure for a subscription box e-commerce platform launching globally, establishing ECS Fargate API deployments in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 with Route 53 latency-based routing and automated health check failover. CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region cluster with REGIONAL BY ROW partitioning and RESTRICTED follower reads enforces GDPR data residency by preventing EU subscriber data from being read by us-east-1 nodes. Subscriber home-region is assigned immutably at signup based on billing address. All infrastructure managed via Terraform with per-region workspaces and sequential region apply pipeline.
target_output.architecture_overview: Active-active architecture: Route 53 latency-based routing directs EU subscribers to eu-west-1 ALB and US subscribers to us-east-1 ALB; health check failover with 60s TTL reroutes traffic to the healthy region if an ALB health check fails. Each region runs an identical ECS Fargate deployment of the Node.js 22 API platform in a private VPC subnet behind an ALB with WAF. Both regions connect to a shared 6-node CockroachDB multi-region cluster (3 nodes per region) over TLS. The subscribers table uses REGIONAL BY ROW with a crdb_region column; EU subscriber rows are pinned to eu-west1 nodes with RESTRICTED follower reads, preventing cross-region data access. Regional ElastiCache Redis 7 clusters serve session tokens locally with no cross-region replication. Cognito regional user pools issue JWTs with a home_region custom claim used by API middleware to enforce region-scoped query routing.
target_output.technology_stack: Database: CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region cluster 6 nodes (3 us-east1, 3 eu-west1) with REGIONAL BY ROW and RESTRICTED follower reads. Compute: ECS Fargate (us-east-1 and eu-west-1) running Node.js 22 API. Routing: Route 53 latency-based with health check failover, 60s TTL. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 per region (no cross-region). Auth: Cognito regional user pools with home_region custom attribute. IaC: Terraform 1.8 with per-region workspaces. CI/CD: GitHub Actions OIDC with per-region IAM roles. Observability: Datadog multi-region APM, CockroachDB DB Console.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Active-Active ECS Fargate Deployment: identical Node.js 22 services deployed to us-east-1 and eu-west-1 via shared Terraform module. Subscriber Home-Region Assignment: at signup, billing address country code maps to home_region (US/CA/APAC->us-east-1; EU/UK->eu-west-1); assignment is immutable. CockroachDB REGIONAL BY ROW: subscribers, subscriptions, and addresses tables partitioned by crdb_region; RESTRICTED follower reads on eu-west1 partition. Route 53 Failover: latency-based routing with health check; automated region exclusion on ALB failure. Data Residency Audit Log: every cross-region query attempt logged to data_residency_audit table with denial reason. Right-to-Erasure Workflow: subscriber deletion cascades through regional partition, logged to audit_deletions, and confirmed within 30 days.
target_output.system_components: Route 53 hosted zone with latency-based A records to us-east-1 and eu-west-1 ALBs; health check failover policy TTL 60s. ALB with WAF in each region fronting ECS Fargate API service (2-20 tasks per region). ECS Fargate VPC private subnets with NAT gateway per region. CockroachDB 23.2 multi-region cluster: 3 nodes r5.xlarge in us-east-1 and 3 in eu-west-1; TLS mutual auth; REGIONAL BY ROW on subscriber tables; RESTRICTED follower reads for eu-west1 partition. ElastiCache Redis 7 r6g.large per region for session tokens. Cognito user pool per region with home_region custom attribute. AWS Secrets Manager per region for CockroachDB and Cognito credentials. Terraform workspaces/us-east-1 and workspaces/eu-west-1 sharing modules/multi-region-stack. GitHub Actions OIDC roles per region.
target_output.workflows: Subscriber Signup and Region Assignment: subscriber registers via checkout; billing address country code evaluated against region_assignment_rules; home_region set to eu-west-1 for EU/UK or us-east-1 for US/CA/APAC; Cognito account created in corresponding regional pool with home_region custom attribute; first CockroachDB write sets crdb_region on subscriber row ensuring data lands on correct regional nodes. Regional Failover: Route 53 health check polls /health on us-east-1 ALB every 10s; after 3 consecutive failures, Route 53 removes us-east-1 A record and all traffic routes to eu-west-1; ECS tasks in eu-west-1 scale out via target-tracking policy; incident commander triggers runbook to re-pin US subscribers to eu-west-1 read-only partition for emergency reads; on us-east-1 recovery, re-pin script restores home-region routing and Route 53 health check re-adds us-east-1 record after 3 consecutive successes. Right-to-Erasure: subscriber submits deletion request; API identifies home_region from JWT claim; issues DELETE on subscribers table where crdb_region=home_region; cascades to subscriptions, addresses, payment_tokens; deletion logged to audit_deletions with timestamp and confirming agent; GDPR erasure confirmation sent within 30 days.
target_output.data_model: region_configs (region_id VARCHAR PK, aws_region VARCHAR, crdb_gateway_endpoint VARCHAR, cognito_pool_id VARCHAR, is_active BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMP). data_residency_policies (policy_id UUID PK, subscriber_region VARCHAR, allowed_crdb_nodes VARCHAR[], restricted_read_enabled BOOL, validated_at TIMESTAMP, validated_by VARCHAR). replication_lag_metrics (measured_at TIMESTAMP, from_region VARCHAR, to_region VARCHAR, lag_ms INT, node_id VARCHAR, PRIMARY KEY (measured_at, from_region, to_region)). failover_routes (route_id UUID PK, triggered_at TIMESTAMP, failed_region VARCHAR, target_region VARCHAR, trigger_reason VARCHAR, resolved_at TIMESTAMP). subscriber_region_assignments (subscriber_id UUID PK, home_region VARCHAR, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, assignment_basis VARCHAR, is_migrated BOOL). audit_deletions (deletion_id UUID PK, subscriber_id UUID, home_region VARCHAR, requested_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, confirmed_by VARCHAR).
target_output.api_design: Existing platform API extended with region-aware middleware. No new public endpoints introduced. Internal ops endpoints (IAM auth): GET /internal/region/health returns regional ECS task count, CockroachDB node status, Redis connection count. GET /internal/replication/lag returns current lag_ms per region pair from CockroachDB DB Console metrics API. POST /internal/subscriber/{id}/migrate-region {target_region} executes runbook-driven home-region migration with audit log entry. GET /internal/residency/audit?subscriber_id= returns data_residency_audit entries for compliance reporting. POST /internal/erasure/confirm/{deletion_id} marks audit_deletions record as completed.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR compliance controls: CockroachDB REGIONAL BY ROW with RESTRICTED follower reads prevents eu-west1 partition data from being served by us-east1 nodes. IAM SCP with explicit Deny on cross-region data service API calls for non-break-glass roles. Cognito home_region JWT claim validated by API middleware; requests where subscriber home_region differs from serving region are rejected with 403 except during runbook-driven migration. DPA signed with CockroachDB as data processor before data ingestion. KMS per-region CMKs encrypt CockroachDB disk volumes and EBS. Schrems II SCCs documented and filed. TLS 1.3 for all CockroachDB inter-node and client connections. Right-to-erasure workflow completes within 30 days per GDPR Article 17. Data residency audit log captures every denied cross-region query attempt.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform monorepo with modules/multi-region-stack module shared by workspaces/us-east-1 and workspaces/eu-west-1. GitHub Actions pipeline: on PR run terraform plan for both workspaces and post diffs as PR comment; on main merge apply us-east-1 workspace; run integration test suite validating CockroachDB zone config, subscriber assignment, and health check endpoint; if passed apply eu-west-1 workspace. CockroachDB zone configuration applied via SQL post-cluster-bootstrap: ALTER TABLE subscribers CONFIGURE ZONE USING constraints='[+region=eu-west1]' for eu partition. Zone configuration validated after every Terraform apply via crdb SQL check job. Route 53 failover tested monthly via chaos engineering drill: manually fail us-east-1 health check, verify eu-west-1 absorbs traffic within 90s, verify CockroachDB replication continues. Rollback: per-region workspace git revert; CockroachDB zone config rollback via previous SQL; Route 53 manual override for emergency region isolation.
target_output.observability: Datadog multi-region APM: service map shows cross-region latency; per-region p99 latency and error rate panels. CockroachDB replication lag metric (crdb.replication.lag_ms) alerted at 5s threshold via PagerDuty P2; CockroachDB node liveness alert for any node down via PagerDuty P1. Route 53 health check status widget in SRE dashboard; failover event triggers PagerDuty incident automatically. Subscriber home-region assignment rate tracked to detect geo-routing anomalies. Data residency audit log query daily for any cross-region denial spikes. CloudWatch ALB 5xx error rate alert per region >1% triggers PagerDuty.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: CockroachDB 6-node cluster (r5.xlarge x6): ~$1,200/month. ECS Fargate 2-20 tasks per region at baseline: ~$400/month total. ElastiCache Redis r6g.large per region: ~$240/month total. Route 53 latency routing and health checks: ~$10/month. Datadog multi-region APM: ~$300/month. Total estimated baseline: ~$2,150/month. At 10x subscriber volume: ECS auto-scaling adds ~$200/month; CockroachDB scales with node addition at ~$200/node. APAC region expansion adds ~$800/month for 3 additional CockroachDB nodes and Fargate deployment.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - CockroachDB Multi-Region Bootstrap (Weeks 1-2): provision 6-node cluster across both regions; configure REGIONAL BY ROW on subscriber tables; apply RESTRICTED follower reads for eu-west1 partition; validate zone config via SQL checks; run residency audit query to confirm zero cross-region reads. Phase 2 - ECS and Routing (Weeks 3-5): deploy ECS Fargate API to eu-west-1 via Terraform workspace; validate Cognito eu-west-1 pool and home_region claim; configure Route 53 latency routing and health check failover; run failover drill confirming 90s regional traffic shift. Phase 3 - Compliance and Launch Gate (Weeks 6-8): implement right-to-erasure workflow; conduct GDPR data residency audit; validate DPA with CockroachDB; run penetration test on cross-region isolation controls; sign-off from legal team for global launch.
target_output.development_guidelines: All CockroachDB zone configuration changes must be applied via Terraform and validated by SQL check CI job; manual crdb SQL zone changes outside Terraform are forbidden and will cause state drift. Subscriber home_region must be set at record creation and never mutated except via the runbook-driven migration endpoint with audit log entry. IaC PRs require two SRE approvals and must include terraform plan output. CockroachDB schema changes must be backward-compatible for zero-downtime rolling deploy; REGIONAL BY ROW column additions require migration plan review. GDPR erasure requests must be tracked in audit_deletions and completed within 30 days; automated reminder alert if deletion not confirmed within 25 days.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CockroachDB zone configuration misconfiguration allows EU partition data to be read by us-east1 nodes, violating GDPR. Mitigation: SQL validation job after every Terraform apply; data residency audit query runs daily and alerts on any cross-region read. Risk: CockroachDB multi-region cluster split-brain during partial bootstrap. Mitigation: must complete full 6-node cluster join before applying zone configurations; staged bootstrap with validation checkpoint. Risk: Route 53 60s TTL means up to 60s of traffic hitting a failed region before failover completes. Mitigation: ALB returns 502 for failed tasks; clients retry on 502; ECS minimum 2 tasks per region reduces single-task failure impact. Tradeoff: RESTRICTED follower reads on eu-west1 partition prevent EU subscribers from reading stale data during US-region replication lag; acceptable latency tradeoff for GDPR compliance.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CockroachDB 23.2 selected over 24.1 for multi-region RESTRICTED follower reads stability. Subscriber home_region set immutably at signup; migration only via explicit runbook. ElastiCache Redis kept regional with no cross-region replication to avoid EU session tokens reaching us-east-1. SCCs filed for Schrems II compliance before EU data ingestion begins.

metadata.notes: Greenfield multi-region AWS infrastructure for GDPR-compliant global subscription box launch; round 1 first pass with compliance_context for personal data and high risk.

project_goal.value: A data pipeline that pulls order, inventory, and advertising data from marketplace seller APIs (Amazon SP-API, eBay Analytics API, Etsy Shops API) and consolidates it into a unified seller reporting data warehouse in BigQuery
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates manual cross-marketplace reporting for seller analytics team

target_users.value: Seller analytics team querying unified BigQuery tables; data engineers maintaining the pipeline; an automated daily data quality report consumer
target_users.rationale: Internal analytics consumers, not external customers

project_class.rationale: Core deliverable is scheduled ETL from multiple external APIs into a data warehouse -- no application UI

capabilities.value: data, batch_jobs, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Batch ingestion from external marketplace APIs into BigQuery warehouse

complexity_level.rationale: Three marketplace integrations with different API schemas; nightly batch cadence; no real-time streaming requirement

risk_level.rationale: Internal analytics data only; no customer PII in scope; marketplace API keys are the primary secret

data_sensitivity.rationale: Sales and inventory data is business-sensitive but not regulated personal or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs on GCP Cloud Composer; outputs are BigQuery tables accessed only by internal analytics team

access_model.value: GCP service accounts with per-dataset IAM roles; analytics team accesses BigQuery via Looker with row-level security by seller_account_id
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege service account per DAG; Looker for governed BI access

feature_scope.value: Nightly order ingestion from Amazon SP-API, eBay Analytics, and Etsy Shops API; inventory snapshot ingestion; advertising spend ingestion; unified schema transformation; cross-marketplace reconciliation; daily data quality report published to Slack
feature_scope.rationale: Full coverage of seller performance data sources

mvp_scope.value: Amazon SP-API order and inventory ingestion, unified BigQuery schema, daily data quality reconciliation report for one seller account
mvp_scope.rationale: Single marketplace proves pipeline pattern before multi-marketplace expansion

security_baseline.value: Marketplace OAuth tokens stored in GCP Secret Manager; all GCP service accounts use Workload Identity Federation; BigQuery datasets encrypted with CMEK; no marketplace credentials in code or Airflow variables UI
security_baseline.rationale: Credential security is the primary control plane for this pipeline

frontend_stack.value: none; no application UI -- BigQuery outputs consumed by Looker and Slack notifications
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class with no frontend

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow on GCP Cloud Composer 2; custom marketplace API clients in Python
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow for DAG orchestration; Cloud Composer for managed Airflow on GCP

data_platform.value: BigQuery as the unified data warehouse; GCS as staging area for raw API response files
data_platform.rationale: BigQuery native to GCP, columnar storage ideal for analytical queries

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Composer 2 (managed Airflow); BigQuery; GCS for staging
hosting_target.rationale: Fully managed GCP-native stack

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw API response files in GCS retained 30 days then deleted; BigQuery staging tables retained 7 days; BigQuery reporting tables retained indefinitely with partition expiry at 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Short retention for raw data; long retention for analytics layer

future_scope.value: Walmart Marketplace and Shopify integrations; hourly inventory sync for high-velocity products; ML-based sales forecasting layer on top of unified data
future_scope.rationale: Pipeline designed to be extensible to additional marketplace connectors

constraints.value: Amazon SP-API rate limits: 1 request/second for orders endpoint; pipeline must complete within a 4-hour nightly window; marketplace API downtime should not block downstream tables for other marketplaces
constraints.rationale: API rate limits and scheduling constraints

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for Cloud Composer task metrics; DAG run success/failure alerts to Slack; BigQuery data freshness checks via Great Expectations
observability_baseline.rationale: Pipeline health and data quality visibility for data engineering team

execution_preference.value: Modular DAG per marketplace with a shared unified transformation DAG triggered after all marketplace DAGs complete; marketplace DAGs run independently so one failure does not block others
execution_preference.rationale: Failure isolation per marketplace source

project.goal: Unified seller data warehouse from Amazon, eBay, and Etsy marketplace APIs
project.target_users: Analytics team, data engineers

backend.clients: Custom Python API clients per marketplace

security.auth: GCP service accounts with Workload Identity Federation
security.secrets: GCP Secret Manager for marketplace OAuth tokens

reasoner_reviews.product: The unified BigQuery reporting schema is the core value delivered to the analytics team. The modular per-marketplace DAG design with independent failure isolation is the right pattern -- the Amazon SP-API is notoriously unreliable during nightly maintenance windows, and it should not block the eBay and Etsy ingestion.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cloud Composer 2 with GKE Autopilot worker nodes is well-suited for this workload. The Amazon SP-API rate limit of 1 req/sec on orders means the order ingestion task must use a token bucket throttle -- at 10,000 orders/day this takes ~167 minutes, which fits the 4-hour window but leaves little headroom. For expansion to 5 marketplaces, consider switching Amazon ingestion to SP-API Reports API which pulls bulk settlement files rather than paginating the Orders API. dbt Core for transformation with BigQuery as target is a natural fit.
reasoner_reviews.security: GCP Secret Manager for marketplace OAuth tokens is correct. Workload Identity Federation for service accounts eliminates long-lived key files. Ensure each marketplace DAG uses a separate service account with access scoped to only its staging GCS bucket and BigQuery staging dataset -- prevents a compromised marketplace token from affecting other pipelines.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-hour nightly window starting at 02:00 UTC is sufficient for current seller count. BigQuery CMEK adds 2-5ms per write operation -- negligible for batch loads but confirm with BigQuery team before committing to very high-frequency writes.

backend.service_design: Four Airflow DAGs: amazon_ingest_dag (orders, inventory via SP-API), ebay_ingest_dag (transactions, listings via eBay Analytics API), etsy_ingest_dag (orders, listings via Etsy Shops API), unified_transform_dag (triggered by TriggerDagRunOperator after all three complete; runs dbt Core models against BigQuery staging tables).
backend.api_patterns: Amazon SP-API: OAuth2 LWA token refresh handled by python-amazon-sp-api library; orders paginated via Orders.getOrders with createdAfter parameter set to previous day UTC; rate-limited via tenacity retry with 1 req/sec. eBay: OAuth2 client credentials; Analytics API bulk download via downloadReport endpoint. Etsy: OAuth2 PKCE; Shops API paginated receipts endpoint.
backend.background_jobs: GCS retention cleanup job runs weekly via Cloud Scheduler triggering a Cloud Function that deletes staging files older than 30 days. dbt test suite runs after unified_transform_dag to validate row counts and null rates on key columns.
backend.failure_handling: Each marketplace DAG uses Airflow retries=3 with exponential backoff. On API authentication failure, DAG raises AirflowException and sends Slack alert with marketplace name and error type. Marketplace DAGs are independent -- unified_transform_dag uses a PythonSensor to check for staging table freshness before proceeding.

security.auth_design: Separate GCP service accounts: sa-amazon-ingest, sa-ebay-ingest, sa-etsy-ingest, sa-dbt-transform. Each has IAM bindings scoped to its staging GCS bucket and BigQuery dataset only. Workload Identity Federation binds each SA to the corresponding Cloud Composer worker pool.
security.secrets_management: Marketplace OAuth tokens stored as GCP Secret Manager secrets accessed via Airflow connection using the Secret Manager backend provider. Tokens rotated on 90-day schedule; rotation triggers Slack alert to data engineering team.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs capture all BigQuery data access and GCS bucket operations. Airflow task logs written to GCS log bucket with 90-day retention.

data.entities: marketplace_credentials (id, marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/etsy, oauth_token_secret_ref, token_expiry, seller_account_id); order_records (id, source_marketplace, marketplace_order_id, seller_account_id, order_date, item_count, gross_revenue_cents, currency, status, ingested_at); inventory_snapshots (id, source_marketplace, sku, available_qty, fulfilment_channel, snapshot_date, ingested_at); advertising_metrics (id, source_marketplace, campaign_id, spend_cents, impressions, clicks, attributed_sales_cents, report_date, ingested_at); etl_run_logs (id, dag_id, run_id, marketplace, rows_extracted, rows_loaded, status ENUM success/partial/failed, started_at, completed_at); unified_reporting_tables in BigQuery: dim_sellers, dim_products, fact_orders, fact_inventory_daily, fact_ad_spend.
data.storage_design: GCS staging bucket per marketplace (gs://pipeline-staging-amazon/, gs://pipeline-staging-ebay/, gs://pipeline-staging-etsy/) for raw JSON API responses, partitioned by date. BigQuery datasets: {marketplace}_staging (raw loaded Parquet/JSON, 7-day expiry), unified_reporting (dbt-transformed tables, partition expiry 3 years, CMEK encrypted).
data.migration_notes: Schema evolution handled via dbt model versioning; breaking schema changes require a new dbt model version and parallel load period before deprecating old table.

devops.deployment_topology: GCP Cloud Composer 2 environment (Airflow 2.8) with GKE Autopilot worker nodes in us-central1. BigQuery datasets in us-central1 for data residency alignment. GCS buckets in us-central1. Cloud Scheduler triggers amazon_ingest_dag, ebay_ingest_dag, etsy_ingest_dag at 02:00 UTC daily.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Python pytest for DAG unit tests (task logic, schema mapping), dbt test compile check, DAG import validation via airflow dags list, Cloud Composer DAGs bucket sync via gcloud storage rsync on main branch merge.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana dashboard sourcing Cloud Composer metrics (task_instance_duration, dag_run_duration, task_failure_count) via Prometheus exporter. Daily data quality report generated by Great Expectations suite and posted to Slack #data-quality channel with marketplace-level row count deltas and null rate summaries.
devops.devops_risks: Cloud Composer environment upgrades (Airflow version bumps) require DAG compatibility testing before upgrade; maintain staging Cloud Composer environment for pre-upgrade validation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Building a moderate-complexity multi-marketplace ETL pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer. Key decisions: per-marketplace independent DAGs with failure isolation so Amazon SP-API rate limits and downtime do not block eBay and Etsy ingestion; GCS staging with 30-day retention for raw API responses; dbt Core for BigQuery transformation triggered after all marketplace ingestion DAGs complete. Token bucket throttle on Amazon SP-API orders pagination to respect 1 req/sec rate limit. This is a weak-quality row -- some sections are thinner than optimal.
target_output.title: Multi-Marketplace Seller Data Warehouse Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly batch data pipeline orchestrated with Apache Airflow on GCP Cloud Composer 2 that ingests orders, inventory, and advertising data from Amazon SP-API, eBay Analytics API, and Etsy Shops API into a unified BigQuery reporting warehouse. Independent per-marketplace DAGs isolate failures, and dbt Core models produce unified fact and dimension tables for seller analytics. GCP Secret Manager stores marketplace OAuth tokens; Workload Identity Federation eliminates long-lived service account keys.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three independent Airflow DAGs execute nightly at 02:00 UTC: amazon_ingest_dag, ebay_ingest_dag, and etsy_ingest_dag. Each DAG authenticates to its marketplace API using OAuth tokens retrieved from GCP Secret Manager, paginates the previous day's orders and inventory data, writes raw JSON responses to a per-marketplace GCS staging bucket, and loads records to a BigQuery staging dataset. A unified_transform_dag is triggered by TriggerDagRunOperator after all three ingest DAGs reach success state; it runs dbt Core models that normalize staging records into unified fact_orders, fact_inventory_daily, and fact_ad_spend tables with a common seller-agnostic schema. A daily Great Expectations data quality suite runs post-transform and publishes a pass/fail report to Slack.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on GCP Cloud Composer 2 with GKE Autopilot workers. Runtime: Python 3.12 with python-amazon-sp-api, ebaysdk, and custom Etsy OAuth client. Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 against BigQuery. Warehouse: BigQuery with CMEK and partitioned tables. Staging: GCS buckets per marketplace. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager via Airflow Secret Manager backend. Auth: Workload Identity Federation per service account. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Data quality: Great Expectations 0.18. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus for Cloud Composer metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Amazon Order and Inventory Ingestion: SP-API Orders.getOrders paginated with 1 req/sec throttle, SellerCentralReports for inventory snapshots. eBay Transaction Ingestion: eBay Analytics API downloadReport bulk extract for previous day sales. Etsy Order Ingestion: Etsy Shops API paginated receipts by min_created date. Unified Transformation: dbt models normalize marketplace-specific schemas into dim_sellers, dim_products, fact_orders, fact_inventory_daily, fact_ad_spend. Cross-Marketplace Reconciliation: reconciliation dbt model compares cross-marketplace order_ids against seller ERP export, flags mismatches. Daily Data Quality Report: Great Expectations suite checks row count deltas, null rates, and date range completeness per marketplace table.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment (Airflow 2.8, GKE Autopilot workers, us-central1). Four Airflow DAGs: amazon_ingest_dag, ebay_ingest_dag, etsy_ingest_dag, unified_transform_dag. GCS staging buckets per marketplace with 30-day lifecycle policy. BigQuery: {marketplace}_staging datasets (7-day partition expiry) and unified_reporting dataset (3-year expiry, CMEK). GCP Secret Manager for OAuth tokens. dbt Core models for unified schema transformation. Great Expectations for post-transform data quality. Cloud Scheduler for DAG triggers at 02:00 UTC.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Ingest (per marketplace): Cloud Scheduler fires DAG trigger at 02:00 UTC; DAG fetches OAuth token from Secret Manager, calls marketplace API with previous day date range, writes raw JSON responses to GCS staging, BigQuery Load Job imports GCS files to {marketplace}_staging dataset, etl_run_log record written with row counts and status. Unified Transform: unified_transform_dag waits for all three ingest DAGs via PythonSensor checking staging table freshness; runs dbt build for unified_reporting models; Great Expectations checkpoint runs post-transform; passes output to Slack webhook with row count summary and any test failures. Reconciliation: dbt reconciliation model joins fact_orders with seller ERP export table loaded daily via manual GCS upload; flags order_ids missing in either direction to a reconciliation_flags table reviewed by analyst team weekly.
target_output.data_model: marketplace_credentials (id UUID PK, marketplace ENUM amazon/ebay/etsy, oauth_token_secret_ref TEXT, token_expiry TIMESTAMPTZ, seller_account_id TEXT). order_records (id UUID PK, source_marketplace TEXT, marketplace_order_id TEXT UNIQUE PER MARKETPLACE, seller_account_id TEXT, order_date DATE, item_count INT, gross_revenue_cents BIGINT, currency TEXT, status TEXT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). inventory_snapshots (id UUID PK, source_marketplace TEXT, sku TEXT, available_qty INT, fulfilment_channel TEXT, snapshot_date DATE, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). advertising_metrics (id UUID PK, source_marketplace TEXT, campaign_id TEXT, spend_cents BIGINT, impressions INT, clicks INT, attributed_sales_cents BIGINT, report_date DATE, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). etl_run_logs (id UUID PK, dag_id TEXT, run_id TEXT, marketplace TEXT, rows_extracted INT, rows_loaded INT, status ENUM success/partial/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). BigQuery unified_reporting: fact_orders, fact_inventory_daily, fact_ad_spend, dim_sellers, dim_products.
target_output.api_design: No REST API exposed -- this is an internal batch pipeline. Integration interfaces: Amazon SP-API Orders.getOrders (GET, createdAfter/createdBefore params, 1 req/sec rate limit, LWA OAuth); Amazon SellerCentralReports API for inventory bulk reports. eBay Analytics downloadReport API (POST report request, GET result when status=SUCCESS). Etsy Shops API GET /v3/application/shops/{shop_id}/receipts (paginated, min_created/max_created params). Slack Incoming Webhook for daily quality report POST. BigQuery Data API for staging table loads via google-cloud-bigquery Python client.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP Secret Manager stores each marketplace OAuth token as a versioned secret; Airflow Secret Manager backend retrieves tokens at DAG execution time without persisting to Airflow metadata DB. Separate GCP service accounts per marketplace DAG with IAM bindings scoped to their staging GCS bucket and BigQuery staging dataset only -- sa-amazon-ingest cannot read eBay staging data. Workload Identity Federation: no service account key files exist; Cloud Composer workers assume service account identity via Kubernetes workload identity binding. BigQuery unified_reporting dataset encrypted with CMEK key in Cloud KMS. Cloud Audit Logs enabled on BigQuery and GCS for all data access events.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest for DAG Python unit tests, dbt compile for model syntax validation, airflow dags list import check. On main branch merge, GitHub Actions syncs DAG files to Cloud Composer GCS DAGs bucket via gcloud storage rsync. dbt models deployed to production via dbt compile artifact uploaded to GCS and referenced by Airflow BashOperator. Cloud Composer environment upgrades tested in a staging Composer environment before production upgrade. Marketplace OAuth token rotation: Secret Manager new version created, Airflow connection updated, old version disabled after confirmation.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboard sourcing Cloud Composer metrics via Prometheus: dag_run_duration_seconds per DAG, task_instance_duration_seconds per task, task_failure_count 7-day rolling. Slack alerts on DAG failure via Airflow SlackWebhookOperator with DAG name and failed task. Great Expectations Slack report includes per-marketplace row count delta (vs. prior day), null rate on marketplace_order_id and order_date columns, and date range completeness check. etl_run_logs table queryable in BigQuery for historical run auditing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Composer 2 Small environment ~$400/month. BigQuery storage ~$50/month for staging and reporting tables at current volume. GCS staging ~$5/month. Cloud KMS for CMEK ~$10/month. Total ~$470/month at single-seller volume. Scaling to 50 seller accounts adds minimal Cloud Composer cost (more parallel tasks); BigQuery cost scales linearly with data volume at ~$5/TB scanned.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Amazon MVP (6 weeks): amazon_ingest_dag for orders and inventory, BigQuery staging schema, basic unified fact_orders dbt model, etl_run_log table, Slack failure alerts. Phase 2 -- Multi-Marketplace (4 weeks): ebay_ingest_dag and etsy_ingest_dag, unified_transform_dag with TriggerDagRunOperator, dbt models for all three marketplaces, Great Expectations data quality suite. Phase 3 -- Quality and Reconciliation (3 weeks): cross-marketplace reconciliation dbt model, data quality Slack report with row count deltas, Grafana dashboard, GCS lifecycle cleanup job.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Airflow DAG Python files must pass pylint and mypy checks in CI. No hardcoded credentials or marketplace-specific values in DAG code -- all config via Airflow Variables or Secret Manager. dbt models versioned with ref() only -- no direct schema-qualified table references.
