Great Expectations suites defined in code (not UI) and committed to repo. Marketplace API client classes must include unit tests with mocked HTTP responses covering pagination, rate limit retry, and authentication failure cases.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Amazon SP-API maintenance windows during the 02:00-06:00 UTC ingestion window cause amazon_ingest_dag failure, leaving fact_orders stale for the day. Mitigation: amazon_ingest_dag retries=3 with 30-minute backoff; data staleness alert if unified_reporting fact_orders Amazon records not refreshed by 08:00 UTC. Risk: eBay Analytics API report generation latency (up to 2 hours for large date ranges) causes unified_transform_dag timeout if eBay DAG runs long. Mitigation: eBay DAG schedules 1 hour earlier than other marketplaces to provide buffer. Tradeoff: GCS raw file staging adds GCS cost and complexity versus streaming directly to BigQuery; staging enables replay and debugging of ingestion issues without re-calling rate-limited APIs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed dbt Core over Dataform for SQL transformation due to team familiarity and local dev support. Chosen GCS staging over direct BigQuery streaming insert to enable raw data replay on schema changes. Amazon SP-API Reports API deferred to Phase 2 expansion; Orders API pagination sufficient for current seller volume. Unified BigQuery schema confirmed as star schema (fact + dim) over raw EAV model for analytics query performance.

metadata.notes: First-pass weak plan for multi-marketplace seller data warehouse pipeline; some sections lighter on detail, cost section minimal.

profile.domain: Product Data Enrichment Pipeline for Price Comparison Marketplace

project_goal.value: A scalable product data enrichment pipeline that ingests raw merchant feeds, normalizes attributes by category schema, deduplicates offers by GTIN, and indexes enriched products into Elasticsearch for a large price comparison marketplace
project_goal.rationale: Enables the marketplace search index to surface matched product offers across hundreds of merchants with normalized attributes

target_users.value: Merchant data team submitting feeds; search engineering team consuming indexed products from Elasticsearch; data quality team reviewing feed quality reports
target_users.rationale: Internal consumers only -- no direct end-user access to the pipeline

project_class.rationale: Core deliverable is a Spark-based batch ETL pipeline with no application UI or API server

capabilities.value: data, batch_jobs, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Large-scale batch processing, S3 integrations for feed ingestion, Elasticsearch indexing, analytics on feed quality

complexity_level.rationale: Processes hundreds of merchant feeds totaling hundreds of millions of raw offer records per day using PySpark on EMR Serverless

risk_level.rationale: Internal product catalog data; no customer PII; merchant data is commercial but not regulated

data_sensitivity.rationale: Product, price, and availability data is business-sensitive but not personal or financial regulated data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs on AWS EMR Serverless; all outputs are internal S3 and Elasticsearch; no external API

access_model.value: IAM roles for EMR Serverless job execution; S3 bucket policies for feed drop zones; Elasticsearch IAM auth for index write access
access_model.rationale: IAM-native access control on all AWS resources

feature_scope.value: Feed ingestion from S3 drop zones in CSV and XML formats; per-category attribute schema validation and normalization; GTIN-based offer clustering and deduplication; canonical product record selection; Delta Lake system-of-record write; Elasticsearch document generation and partial index update; feed quality report per merchant
feature_scope.rationale: Full enrichment pipeline from raw feed to search index

mvp_scope.value: CSV feed ingestion for electronics category (top 50 merchants), GTIN deduplication, Delta Lake write, Elasticsearch index update, basic feed quality report
mvp_scope.rationale: Electronics category proves pipeline pattern before full category rollout

security_baseline.value: EMR Serverless job execution IAM role with least-privilege S3 and Elasticsearch access; all S3 buckets encrypted with SSE-S3; no merchant credentials stored in Spark code or configs
security_baseline.rationale: IAM scoping and encryption at rest for merchant feed data

frontend_stack.value: none; no UI -- feed quality reports delivered via S3 CSV and email notification
frontend_stack.rationale: data_pipeline project class with no frontend component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with PySpark on AWS EMR Serverless; AWS Glue Data Catalog for schema registry
backend_stack.rationale: PySpark for distributed processing at hundreds-of-millions record scale; EMR Serverless for managed compute without cluster management

data_platform.value: Delta Lake on S3 as system-of-record for normalized products; Elasticsearch 8 for search index
data_platform.rationale: Delta Lake for ACID transactions, time travel, and efficient upserts; Elasticsearch for full-text and faceted search serving

hosting_target.value: AWS EMR Serverless for PySpark jobs; S3 for Delta Lake and staging; Elasticsearch 8 on OpenSearch Service
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless compute with no cluster management; managed Elasticsearch on AWS

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw merchant feed files in S3 staging retained 14 days then deleted; Delta Lake enriched records retained indefinitely with soft-delete on merchant feed removal; Elasticsearch documents kept in sync with Delta Lake system of record
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Short staging retention; long-term enriched product catalog retention

future_scope.value: LLM-based attribute extraction for feeds with non-standard schemas; real-time feed processing for high-velocity merchants; visual similarity deduplication for products without GTINs
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 intelligence layer on top of the enrichment pipeline

constraints.value: Hourly feed processing window must complete within 45 minutes for top-tier merchants; Elasticsearch partial index updates must not take the search index offline; Delta Lake writes must be idempotent to support job retry on failure
constraints.rationale: SLA and operational reliability constraints

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for EMR Serverless job metrics; S3 event notifications for feed arrival monitoring; per-merchant feed quality report in CloudWatch Logs Insights
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudWatch-native observability for AWS-hosted pipeline

execution_preference.value: Hourly EMR Serverless jobs triggered by S3 event notifications on feed drop zone prefix; full daily reindex job runs nightly for consistency reconciliation
execution_preference.rationale: Near-real-time hourly feed processing with nightly full reconciliation

project.goal: Scalable product enrichment pipeline from merchant feeds to Elasticsearch search index
project.target_users: Merchant data team, search engineering, data quality team
project.timeline: Electronics MVP Q2, full category rollout Q3

security.auth: IAM roles for EMR Serverless
security.access: bucket policies per merchant feed drop zone

reasoner_reviews.product: The GTIN-based deduplication is the core value of this pipeline -- without it, the search index surfaces 50 merchant listings for the same product as separate results. The canonical offer selection logic (which merchant's attributes become the canonical product record) needs to be explicitly defined in the data model, as merchants have varying attribute completeness.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PySpark with Delta Lake on EMR Serverless is the right stack for this scale. GTIN clustering requires a distributed join between the feed records and the existing GTIN registry in Delta Lake; this must be a broadcast join if the GTIN registry fits in executor memory (under 2GB) or a shuffle join for larger registries. Delta Lake MERGE INTO for upserts handles idempotency cleanly. The Elasticsearch partial update (POST /_update with doc field) avoids full document re-indexing and can run concurrently with live search traffic without downtime.
reasoner_reviews.security: IAM least-privilege is straightforward for this pipeline. Each merchant feed S3 drop zone prefix should use a separate IAM policy condition (s3:prefix) so a compromised merchant credential cannot overwrite another merchant's feed. EMR Serverless job execution role should be scoped to read from staging buckets and write to Delta Lake buckets only -- no wildcard S3 permissions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 45-minute hourly processing SLA is achievable for top 50 merchants in electronics with EMR Serverless autoscaling up to 20 driver + 100 executor vCPUs. Feed XML parsing is typically the bottleneck -- consider pre-converting XML to Parquet in a lightweight Lambda function on S3 event to reduce Spark parse time.

backend.service_design: Three PySpark jobs: feed_ingest_job (validates and parses CSV/XML feeds into Parquet staging tables), enrichment_job (normalizes attributes against category schema, clusters by GTIN, selects canonical record, writes to Delta Lake via MERGE), index_update_job (reads Delta Lake change data, generates Elasticsearch bulk update payloads, submits to OpenSearch index). Nightly full_reindex_job reconciles Delta Lake and Elasticsearch for consistency.
backend.api_patterns: No REST API. Integration interfaces: S3 GetObject for feed files, S3 PutObject for Delta Lake partitions via delta-spark library, OpenSearch Elasticsearch bulk API POST /_bulk for index updates, SNS notification on job completion with merchant row count summary.
backend.background_jobs: S3 event notification on s3://feeds-drop/{merchant_id}/ PUT triggers Step Functions state machine that invokes feed_ingest_job then enrichment_job then index_update_job sequentially. Nightly at 03:00 UTC, full_reindex_job runs to catch any drift between Delta Lake and Elasticsearch.
backend.failure_handling: EMR Serverless job retry=2 on transient failures. feed_ingest_job writes a feed_validation_report to S3 with row-level parse errors; records failing schema validation are written to a quarantine partition rather than blocking the full feed. Delta Lake MERGE idempotency means job retry produces identical output.

security.auth_design: EMR Serverless application IAM execution role: s3:GetObject on staging buckets, s3:PutObject on Delta Lake bucket, es:ESHttpPost on OpenSearch index ARN. No other permissions. Merchant feed drop zones use S3 bucket policy with aws:PrincipalTag/MerchantId condition to prevent cross-merchant writes.
security.secrets_management: No merchant credentials stored in the pipeline; feed files are dropped to S3 by merchant ETL systems using their own IAM credentials. OpenSearch master user credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager; EMR execution role uses fine-grained access control via IAM not basic auth.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail logs all S3 and OpenSearch API calls. EMR Serverless job logs written to S3 log bucket with 90-day retention. feed_quality_reports table in Delta Lake records per-merchant run statistics.

data.entities: merchant_feeds (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, feed_s3_key TEXT, feed_format ENUM csv/xml, category TEXT, row_count INT, validation_errors INT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ); raw_offers (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, gtin TEXT, title TEXT, price_cents BIGINT, currency TEXT, availability TEXT, attributes JSONB, feed_id FK, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ); gtin_clusters (gtin TEXT PK, canonical_merchant_id TEXT, canonical_title TEXT, canonical_attributes JSONB, offer_count INT, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ); normalized_products (id UUID PK, gtin TEXT FK gtin_clusters, merchant_id TEXT, price_cents BIGINT, availability TEXT, merchant_attributes JSONB, is_canonical BOOL, delta_lake_version BIGINT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ); attribute_mappings (id UUID PK, category TEXT, source_attribute_name TEXT, canonical_attribute_name TEXT, data_type TEXT, required BOOL); search_index_documents (gtin TEXT PK, canonical_title TEXT, canonical_attributes JSONB, all_offers JSONB, min_price_cents BIGINT, max_price_cents BIGINT, offer_count INT, indexed_at TIMESTAMPTZ); feed_quality_reports (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, run_date DATE, rows_received INT, rows_valid INT, rows_quarantined INT, gtin_match_rate FLOAT, attribute_completeness FLOAT).
data.storage_design: S3 staging buckets per merchant category partition (s3://feeds-staging/{category}/{merchant_id}/{date}/). Delta Lake on S3 (s3://delta-lake/normalized_products/, s3://delta-lake/gtin_clusters/) with Z-ORDER optimization on gtin and merchant_id columns. OpenSearch index: products-v{N} with alias products; blue-green index swap for full reindexes.
data.analytics_events: feed_quality_report per merchant per run: gtin_match_rate, attribute_completeness_by_field, price_currency_distribution, quarantine_error_codes. Aggregate: cross-merchant gtin_cluster coverage, daily offer_count delta, new_gtins_discovered.

devops.deployment_topology: EMR Serverless application in us-east-1 with autoscaling 0-100 worker units. S3 buckets in us-east-1. OpenSearch Service cluster: 3 data nodes m5.xlarge.search with 1 primary + 1 replica shard per index. Step Functions state machine for per-merchant job orchestration.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PySpark unit tests with pyspark pytest fixtures, Delta Lake schema compatibility check (schema evolution test), OpenSearch mapping validation. On main merge: PySpark job artifacts uploaded to S3 EMR jobs bucket via aws s3 cp. Step Functions state machine updated via AWS CDK deploy.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch EMR Serverless metrics: job_duration_seconds, driver_memory_mb, active_worker_units. CloudWatch Logs Insights for feed_quality_report aggregation. SNS on Step Functions execution failure. CloudWatch alarm if hourly pipeline p95 duration exceeds 40 minutes.
devops.cost_controls: EMR Serverless billed per vCPU-hour; job autoscaling starts from 0, no idle cost. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for Delta Lake partitions older than 30 days. OpenSearch Reserved Instances for 1-year term reduce data node cost 30%.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a high-scale PySpark pipeline for product enrichment on AWS EMR Serverless with Delta Lake as the system of record and Elasticsearch for search indexing. Key decisions: GTIN-based distributed join for offer clustering (broadcast vs. shuffle depending on GTIN registry size), Delta Lake MERGE INTO for idempotent upserts enabling safe retry on job failure, Elasticsearch partial bulk update to avoid index downtime, per-merchant feed quarantine to prevent one bad feed blocking pipeline progress. Intentionally weak quality -- some sections are thinner than optimal.
target_output.title: Product Data Enrichment Pipeline -- Price Comparison Marketplace
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale PySpark data enrichment pipeline running on AWS EMR Serverless that processes hundreds of millions of raw merchant offer records hourly, normalizes attributes against per-category schemas, deduplicates offers by GTIN to build a canonical product catalog, and indexes enriched products into Elasticsearch 8 on OpenSearch Service. Delta Lake on S3 is the ACID system of record for normalized products and GTIN clusters, enabling idempotent job retries and time travel queries for data quality auditing.
target_output.architecture_overview: Merchant feeds arrive in S3 drop zone prefixes (s3://feeds-drop/{merchant_id}/) in CSV or XML format. S3 event notifications trigger an AWS Step Functions state machine that sequences three PySpark EMR Serverless jobs: feed_ingest_job parses and validates feeds writing Parquet staging records; enrichment_job normalizes attributes, clusters records by GTIN using a Delta Lake MERGE join against the gtin_clusters table, selects canonical attributes based on completeness scoring, and upserts enriched records to the normalized_products Delta Lake table; index_update_job reads Delta Lake change data (CDF) since the last successful run and generates Elasticsearch bulk update payloads submitted to the OpenSearch products index without full re-indexing. A nightly full_reindex_job at 03:00 UTC reconciles the entire Delta Lake state with Elasticsearch to resolve any drift. Feed records failing schema validation are written to a quarantine partition and flagged in the per-merchant feed_quality_report without blocking valid records.
target_output.technology_stack: Processing: Python 3.12, PySpark 3.5, delta-spark 3.1 on AWS EMR Serverless. System of record: Delta Lake on S3 with AWS Glue Data Catalog for schema registry and Athena compatibility. Search: Elasticsearch 8.x via OpenSearch Service (es client via opensearch-py). Orchestration: AWS Step Functions for job sequencing, S3 event notifications for feed arrival trigger. CI/CD: GitHub Actions for PySpark tests and artifact upload; AWS CDK for Step Functions and EMR application config. Monitoring: AWS CloudWatch for EMR metrics and alarms.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Feed Ingestion: parse CSV/XML merchant feeds, validate required fields against category schema, write valid records to Parquet staging and invalid records to quarantine partition with error codes. Attribute Normalization: map merchant-specific field names to canonical category schema (attribute_mappings table), cast data types, fill missing optional attributes with null. GTIN Clustering: join staging offers against gtin_clusters Delta table by GTIN; assign cluster membership; for new GTINs, create new cluster record. Canonical Selection: for each GTIN cluster, select the merchant offer with highest attribute_completeness score as the canonical record for the search index title and attributes. Delta Lake Upsert: MERGE INTO normalized_products on gtin + merchant_id; update changed records; insert new records. Index Update: read Delta Lake CDF, build Elasticsearch bulk payload (POST /_bulk with update action and doc field), submit to OpenSearch products index. Feed Quality Report: per-merchant report with gtin_match_rate, attribute_completeness, quarantine_error_code breakdown.
target_output.system_components: S3 drop zone buckets per merchant. Step Functions state machine (sequential: ingest -> enrich -> index). EMR Serverless application (PySpark 3.5, autoscaling 0-100 worker units). Delta Lake on S3 (normalized_products table, gtin_clusters table, change data feed enabled). AWS Glue Data Catalog (Delta table schema registry). OpenSearch Service cluster (3 data nodes m5.xlarge.search, products index with gtin as _id). S3 log bucket for EMR job logs and feed quality reports. SNS topic for job failure notifications. CloudWatch alarms for duration SLA breach.
target_output.workflows: Hourly Feed Processing: S3 PutObject event on s3://feeds-drop/{merchant_id}/ triggers Step Functions; feed_ingest_job reads CSV/XML, validates against category attribute_mappings schema, writes valid Parquet to staging and invalid rows to s3://feeds-quarantine/{merchant_id}/{date}/; enrichment_job reads staging Parquet, normalizes attributes, joins to gtin_clusters Delta table by GTIN (broadcast join if registry < 2GB, shuffle join otherwise), upserts enriched records to normalized_products via Delta MERGE INTO, updates gtin_clusters canonical attributes for clusters whose canonical score changed; index_update_job reads CDF from normalized_products since last checkpoint, builds Elasticsearch bulk payload batched at 500 documents, POSTs to OpenSearch products index. Nightly Reconciliation: full_reindex_job at 03:00 UTC reads all normalized_products Delta Lake records, generates full Elasticsearch document set, computes diff against current index by scanning index scroll API, submits missing or stale documents as bulk updates. Feed Quality Reporting: after enrichment_job completes, a Python task computes per-merchant quality stats and writes a CSV to s3://feed-quality-reports/{merchant_id}/{run_date}.csv and sends an SNS notification to the merchant data team Slack channel.
target_output.data_model: merchant_feeds (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, feed_s3_key TEXT, feed_format ENUM csv/xml, category TEXT, row_count INT, validation_errors INT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). raw_offers (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, gtin TEXT, title TEXT, price_cents BIGINT, currency TEXT, availability TEXT, attributes JSONB, feed_id FK, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). gtin_clusters (gtin TEXT PK, canonical_merchant_id TEXT, canonical_title TEXT, canonical_attributes JSONB, offer_count INT, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ) -- Delta Lake table, Z-ORDER on gtin. normalized_products (id UUID PK, gtin TEXT, merchant_id TEXT, price_cents BIGINT, availability TEXT, merchant_attributes JSONB, attribute_completeness FLOAT, is_canonical BOOL, delta_lake_version BIGINT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- Delta Lake table with CDF enabled, Z-ORDER on gtin and merchant_id. attribute_mappings (id UUID PK, category TEXT, source_attribute_name TEXT, canonical_attribute_name TEXT, data_type TEXT, required BOOL) -- Glue Data Catalog table. feed_quality_reports (id UUID PK, merchant_id TEXT, run_date DATE, rows_received INT, rows_valid INT, rows_quarantined INT, gtin_match_rate FLOAT, attribute_completeness FLOAT) -- Delta Lake table.
target_output.api_design: No REST API exposed. Integration interfaces: S3 GetObject for merchant feed files (EMR execution role). Delta Lake MERGE INTO via delta-spark (normalized_products and gtin_clusters tables). OpenSearch bulk API: POST https://{opensearch_endpoint}/{index}/_bulk with JSON body containing update actions, Content-Type application/json, AWS SigV4 signed by EMR execution role. SNS Publish for job completion and quality report notifications. Glue Data Catalog GetTable/UpdateTable for schema evolution operations.
target_output.security_and_compliance: EMR Serverless execution IAM role is least-privilege: s3:GetObject on staging buckets, s3:PutObject on Delta Lake and quarantine buckets, es:ESHttpPost and es:ESHttpBulk on the products OpenSearch index ARN only. Merchant feed drop zones enforce per-merchant write isolation via S3 bucket policy condition aws:PrincipalTag/MerchantId -- merchant A cannot overwrite merchant B's feed files. All S3 buckets use SSE-S3 server-side encryption. OpenSearch fine-grained access control uses IAM-based index-level permissions rather than basic auth credentials. CloudTrail enabled for all S3 and OpenSearch API calls for access auditing.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: pytest with pyspark local mode for ingest, enrichment, and index update job logic; Delta Lake schema compatibility test (reads existing table schema, validates new job schema is backward compatible); OpenSearch index mapping validation. On main branch merge: PySpark job Python wheel uploaded to s3://emr-jobs-artifacts/{version}/. EMR Serverless application updated via AWS CDK deploy. Step Functions state machine updated via CDK with new job artifact S3 URI. Category schema expansion (adding new categories to attribute_mappings): applied via Delta Lake MERGE to attribute_mappings table in CI pipeline after schema review. Full index rebuild (nightly): runs as separate EMR Serverless job triggered by CloudWatch Events rule at 03:00 UTC.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch EMR Serverless metrics per job: job_duration_seconds (alarm if > 40 minutes for hourly jobs), active_worker_units (autoscaling monitoring), driver_oom_count. CloudWatch Logs Insights query for feed_quality_report aggregation: gtin_match_rate by category, daily quarantine rate by merchant. SNS alert on Step Functions execution failure with job name and error message routed to Slack. CloudWatch alarm if normalized_products Delta table write rate drops to zero for > 2 consecutive hourly cycles (pipeline stall detection).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EMR Serverless: billed per vCPU-second, starts from 0 between jobs -- no idle cost. At 100M records/hour, estimated ~8 vCPU-hours per run ~$2.50/run, ~$1,800/month. S3 storage for Delta Lake ~$200/month at 5TB. OpenSearch m5.xlarge.search 3 nodes with 1-year Reserved Instances ~$900/month. Total ~$2,900/month. Scale path: EMR worker unit limit increased to 200 for peak category rollout periods; OpenSearch index shards increased by adding data nodes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Electronics MVP (8 weeks): S3 drop zone for top 50 electronics merchants, feed_ingest_job for CSV, enrichment_job with GTIN clustering, Delta Lake normalized_products and gtin_clusters tables, Elasticsearch index update, basic feed quality report. Phase 2 -- Multi-Category Rollout (6 weeks): XML feed parsing, attribute_mappings for 10 additional categories, quarantine partition, nightly full_reindex_job, per-merchant SNS quality notifications. Phase 3 -- Reliability and Analytics (4 weeks): Delta Lake CDF-based incremental index update replacing full scan, CloudWatch alarms for SLA breaches, Glue Data Catalog schema versioning, feed quality trend dashboards.
target_output.development_guidelines: PySpark job logic must be unit-testable in local Spark mode without EMR -- use SparkSession.builder.master('local') in test fixtures. Delta Lake MERGE operations require explicit predicate on gtin and merchant_id to prevent full table scans -- enforce via code review checklist. Elasticsearch bulk payload must include _id field matching gtin to ensure idempotent index updates on job retry. Category attribute_mappings are data (not code) -- changes go through the data catalog update pipeline, not code deploys. Feed quarantine records must include a machine-readable error_code field (MISSING_GTIN, INVALID_PRICE, SCHEMA_MISMATCH) to enable automated merchant notifications.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GTIN registry Delta Lake table grows beyond broadcast join threshold, causing shuffle join with significant network I/O overhead on enrichment_job. Mitigation: monitor gtin_clusters table size weekly; switch to shuffle join configuration when table exceeds 2GB; consider Z-ORDER clustering to improve shuffle join locality. Risk: Elasticsearch bulk update backpressure during peak hourly processing causes index_update_job timeout. Mitigation: implement exponential backoff with circuit breaker on bulk API calls; reduce batch size from 500 to 100 documents if 429 responses exceed 10%. Tradeoff: hourly incremental CDF-based updates over real-time streaming (Kafka to Elasticsearch) -- acceptable latency for price comparison use case; reduces system complexity significantly.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Delta Lake over Apache Iceberg for MERGE semantics and Python ecosystem maturity with delta-spark. Chosen EMR Serverless over managed EMR cluster to eliminate cluster sizing and tuning overhead. Canonical attribute selection based on attribute_completeness float score rather than merchant tier ranking to keep the logic data-driven and auditable. Elasticsearch partial update (doc field) confirmed over full document replacement to avoid index downtime during bulk re-index windows.

metadata.notes: First-pass weak plan for high-scale product enrichment pipeline; good domain detail in data model and workflows, thinner on observability and deployment.

profile.domain: Global Industrial Supplies Wholesale Buyer Platform

project_goal.value: Wholesale buyer platform for a global industrial supplies marketplace enabling procurement teams to manage RFQ workflows, compare supplier bids across thousands of line items, and issue large purchase orders through configurable approval chains
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual email-based RFQ process handling $500M annual spend across 200 enterprise buyers

target_users.value: Enterprise procurement managers, category buyers, approval chain signatories, supplier bid managers, and marketplace admins
target_users.rationale: Multi-sided platform with distinct roles for buyers, suppliers, and operators

project_class.rationale: Full buyer UI, supplier bid portal, admin dashboard, and Java Spring Boot API backend

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full capability stack for RFQ lifecycle, approval workflow, spend analytics, ERP integration, and nightly reconciliation

complexity_level.rationale: RFQs with thousands of line items, multi-tier approval chains, concurrent supplier bidding, and spend analytics over historical PO data

risk_level.rationale: Confidential pricing, contract terms, and supplier margin data; SOC 2 required for enterprise buyers

data_sensitivity.rationale: Supplier bid pricing, buyer spend data, and contract terms are commercially confidential

external_exposure.rationale: Accessible to credentialed enterprise buyers and qualified suppliers; not open to public internet

access_model.value: Keycloak enterprise SSO with SAML federation to buyer organizations; RBAC with buyer_org, supplier, approver, admin roles; row-level security isolates buyer org data
access_model.rationale: Enterprise buyers require SSO with their corporate identity providers

feature_scope.value: RFQ creation with bulk line item import, supplier bid submission portal, bid comparison matrix, configurable approval chain by spend threshold, purchase order generation with e-signature, spend analytics dashboard, ERP integration for PO export, admin panel for buyer org and supplier management
feature_scope.rationale: Complete RFQ-to-PO lifecycle feature set

mvp_scope.value: RFQ creation, supplier bid submission, side-by-side bid comparison, single-tier approval workflow, purchase order PDF generation
mvp_scope.rationale: Core procurement workflow for pilot with 10 enterprise buyers

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3; Keycloak with MFA; row-level security on PostgreSQL isolating buyer org data; field-level encryption for contract_terms; Azure Key Vault for secrets; audit log for all PO and approval actions
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II controls for enterprise data handling

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript and Vite; AG Grid Enterprise for line item comparison grids; React Query; Keycloak JS adapter for SSO
frontend_stack.rationale: AG Grid handles 1000+ line item comparison matrices performantly; team expertise in React

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3 and Spring Security; Spring Batch for nightly PO reconciliation; PostgreSQL 16
backend_stack.rationale: Java 21 virtual threads handle concurrent supplier bid submissions efficiently; Spring Batch for batch PO export

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security and field-level encryption for confidential fields; Redis 7 for approval workflow state and bid deadline countdowns
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces buyer org data isolation at database layer; Redis for real-time bid deadline state

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service; Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Azure Cache for Redis; Azure DevOps Pipelines
hosting_target.rationale: Enterprise buyer SSO via Azure AD; existing Azure agreement with enterprise pricing

privacy_retention_policy.value: RFQ and PO data retained 7 years for trade compliance and audit; personal contact data anonymized after buyer org contract termination; SOC 2 audit logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Trade compliance record-keeping requirements and SOC 2 evidence retention

future_scope.value: Multi-currency PO support, trade compliance screening for cross-border orders, supplier performance scoring, and public API for ERP-native integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 capabilities after MVP validation with pilot buyers

constraints.value: AG Grid Enterprise license budget already allocated; AKS cluster must share existing Azure subscription; PO generation must produce legally valid PDF with e-signature within 5 seconds
constraints.rationale: License commitment and performance SLA for PO generation

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights for Spring Boot services; custom metrics for RFQ lifecycle stage durations, bid submission rates, and approval chain completion times
observability_baseline.rationale: Procurement ops team needs visibility into RFQ cycle time and approval bottlenecks

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; blue-green deployment on AKS
execution_preference.rationale: Blue-green for zero-downtime deployment critical during active RFQ deadlines

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II for enterprise buyer data handling; trade compliance screening for cross-border POs in future scope
compliance_context.rationale: Enterprise buyer contracts require SOC 2 attestation before platform go-live

project.goal: RFQ lifecycle platform from creation through supplier bidding, approval, and PO issuance
project.target_users: Enterprise procurement managers, suppliers, approvers, and marketplace admins

frontend.grids: AG Grid Enterprise for bid comparison matrices

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security and Spring Batch

security.auth: Keycloak with SAML federation and MFA
security.encryption: PostgreSQL RLS and field-level encryption for contract_terms; Azure Key Vault

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS and field-level encryption
data.cache: Azure Cache for Redis 7 for workflow state and deadline countdowns

devops.hosting: Azure Kubernetes Service with blue-green deployment

constraints.po_generation: PO PDF with e-signature within 5 seconds

open_questions.approval_chain: How are spend thresholds configured per buyer org? Per-org threshold table in database confirmed.

reasoner_reviews.product: RFQ-to-PO lifecycle is well-scoped. The bid comparison matrix is the highest-value feature for procurement managers; AG Grid Enterprise handles 1000-line-item matrices with server-side row model. The configurable approval chain by spend threshold is critical for enterprise buyer adoption and must support multi-tier chains with delegated approval.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Java 21 virtual threads are appropriate for concurrent supplier bid submissions without thread pool exhaustion. Spring Batch for nightly PO reconciliation is correct. The plan must specify how AG Grid server-side row model is implemented for the bid comparison matrix to handle 5000+ line items without client-side memory pressure.
reasoner_reviews.security: PostgreSQL row-level security is the right control for buyer org data isolation but the RLS policy definition must be explicit in the plan. The plan must name which fields in contract_terms are encrypted with Azure Key Vault and document the key rotation strategy for SOC 2 evidence.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PO PDF generation within 5 seconds is achievable with pre-rendered templates and JasperReports. The 5-second SLA must be validated under load with concurrent PO generations before pilot launch.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan improved AG Grid implementation details but the contract_terms field encryption key rotation strategy was still missing (ISS-006) and the PostgreSQL RLS policy syntax was not specified (ISS-007). Additionally the plan incorrectly described the frontend as a Next.js SSR application despite the contract specifying React 18 with Vite, a direct contract violation.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with Vite bundler; React Router v6 for procurement dashboard, RFQ management, bid comparison, PO tracking, and admin panel routes. Keycloak JS adapter handles SSO redirect and token refresh. AG Grid Enterprise server-side row model for bid comparison matrix fetching paginated line items from Spring Boot API.
frontend.pages_and_flows: RFQ Creation: procurement manager fills RFQ header (title, deadline, delivery terms), uploads line item CSV via Papa Parse, previews parsed items in editable AG Grid, submits RFQ which broadcasts invitations to selected suppliers. Bid Comparison Matrix: AG Grid server-side row model displaying all supplier bids side-by-side per line item with color-coded lowest-price highlights and variance percentage columns. Purchase Order Review: selected bid summary with approval chain status, e-signature pad via DocuSign Embedded, PO PDF preview before final issue.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server state with optimistic updates for approval actions; Zustand for AG Grid filter/sort state and RFQ draft state; no Redux
frontend.frontend_risks: AG Grid server-side row model pagination must handle concurrent bid updates during active RFQ deadline; stale line item data in grid cells if React Query cache is not invalidated on bid submission WebSocket event

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3 monolith with domain packages: rfq (creation, lifecycle, invitation), bids (submission, validation, comparison), approvals (chain evaluation, escalation), purchase_orders (generation, e-signature, export), integrations (ERP export, email notifications). Java 21 virtual threads for HTTP request handling.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.1 at /api/v1. Server-Sent Events for RFQ deadline countdown and real-time bid submission notifications to procurement dashboard.
backend.background_jobs: Spring Batch nightly job: reconcile issued POs against ERP system; export approved POs to ERP XML format; send 24h deadline reminder emails for open RFQs; generate spend analytics snapshots for dashboard.
backend.backend_risks: Approval chain evaluation with delegated approvers requires careful state machine design; missed escalation on approver non-response could block PO issuance indefinitely if escalation timer is not implemented

security.auth_design: Keycloak 24 with SAML 2.0 IdP federation for enterprise buyer SSO; MFA enforced via Keycloak authentication flow; buyer organization mapped to Keycloak realm group; roles: buyer_org_member, category_buyer, approver_l1, approver_l2, supplier, marketplace_admin.
security.authorization_model: Spring Security with method-level @PreAuthorize annotations; PostgreSQL RLS policies restrict SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on rfq_events, supplier_bids, purchase_orders to rows where buyer_org_id = current_user_org_id; row-level policy set via SET LOCAL for each request.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit log table recording: RFQ creation, bid submission, approval action, PO issuance, ERP export; each event includes user_id, timestamp, IP, and diff of changed fields. SOC 2 evidence: audit log exported weekly to Azure Blob cold storage.
security.security_risks: Keycloak SAML assertion replay attack if assertion consumer URL is not validated; mitigation: strict SAML audience restriction and assertion validity window of 5 minutes

data.entities: rfq_events (id, buyer_org_id, title, status ENUM draft/open/closed/cancelled, deadline, delivery_terms, created_by, created_at). supplier_bids (id, rfq_id, supplier_id, status ENUM submitted/accepted/rejected, total_value, currency, submitted_at). quote_line_items (id, bid_id, part_number, description, quantity, unit_price, lead_days, incoterms). purchase_orders (id, rfq_id, buyer_org_id, supplier_id, total_value, status ENUM draft/approved/issued/exported, pdf_s3_key, issued_at). approval_chains (id, po_id, tier_level, approver_id, spend_threshold, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/escalated, acted_at). contract_terms (id, po_id, terms_text_encrypted, key_version, created_at). spend_analytics_snapshots (id, buyer_org_id, period_month, total_spend, po_count, supplier_count, top_categories_json, generated_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with RLS policies per table; contract_terms.terms_text encrypted with AES-256-GCM using Azure Key Vault managed keys; Redis 7 for approval workflow state machine and RFQ deadline countdown TTLs.
data.migration_notes: Flyway for schema migrations; blue-green deployment means migration must be backward-compatible with previous version during cutover window

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster with three node pools: system, api (Java Spring Boot), and batch (Spring Batch workers). Blue-green deployment via AKS deployment slots; active slot promoted after health check validation. Azure DevOps pipeline promotes staging image to production after integration test suite passes.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: on PR build Docker image, run JUnit tests (>80% coverage gate), run ESLint/Vitest for frontend, run OWASP dependency check. On main merge: build and push to Azure Container Registry, deploy to staging AKS slot, run smoke tests, promote to production blue-green slot on pass.
devops.observability_stack: Application Insights for Spring Boot service metrics (RFQ creation rate, bid submission latency, approval chain wait time, PO generation p99). Custom dashboard: RFQ pipeline funnel from creation to PO issuance. Alert: PO generation p99 >5s triggers Azure Monitor alert.
devops.devops_risks: Flyway migration during blue-green cutover must be backward-compatible; non-backward migrations require a maintenance window and must be called out explicitly in PR

ISS-006.title: contract_terms field encryption key rotation strategy unspecified
ISS-006.detail: Plan specifies Azure Key Vault for contract_terms encryption but does not describe key rotation cadence, rotation procedure, or how re-encryption of existing records is handled, which is a SOC 2 control gap.

history.note: Key rotation not mentioned
Still not addressed in round 2

ISS-007.title: PostgreSQL RLS policy syntax not specified
ISS-007.detail: Plan states RLS is used for buyer org isolation but does not specify the policy syntax or how buyer_org_id is injected into the PostgreSQL session for enforcement.

target_output.title: Industrial Wholesale Buyer Platform Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale wholesale buyer platform for a global industrial supplies marketplace built with React 18 TypeScript and Vite frontend, Java 21 Spring Boot 3 backend on Azure Kubernetes Service, and PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security isolating buyer organization data. AG Grid Enterprise server-side row model handles RFQ line item comparison matrices with 5000+ items. Keycloak 24 with SAML 2.0 federation provides enterprise buyer SSO. Azure Key Vault manages contract_terms field encryption with annual key rotation and automated re-encryption batch job. PostgreSQL RLS policies with SET LOCAL session variable injection enforce buyer org data isolation at database level for SOC 2 compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA built with Vite deployed to Azure Static Web Apps and served via Azure CDN; communicates over HTTPS to Spring Boot 3 REST API deployed on AKS. Keycloak 24 handles SAML 2.0 federation and issues JWTs consumed by both frontend and backend. Spring Boot API connects to Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with RLS policies enforcing buyer_org_id isolation via SET LOCAL session variable injected from JWT claim. AG Grid Enterprise server-side row model queries paginated line items from /api/v1/rfq/{id}/line-items for bid comparison matrices. Azure Cache for Redis 7 stores approval workflow state machine and RFQ deadline countdown TTLs. Spring Batch runs nightly on dedicated AKS node pool for PO reconciliation and ERP export jobs. Azure Key Vault manages AES-256-GCM encryption keys for contract_terms field with annual rotation and key_version tracking.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 TypeScript, Vite 5, AG Grid Enterprise 32 (server-side row model), React Query, Zustand, React Router v6, Keycloak JS adapter, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Java 21 with virtual threads, Spring Boot 3.3, Spring Security 6, Spring Batch 5, JasperReports for PO PDF. Database: Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with RLS. Cache: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Auth: Keycloak 24 with SAML 2.0 IdP federation. Encryption: Azure Key Vault for contract_terms AES-256-GCM. Hosting: AKS with blue-green deployment, Azure Static Web Apps for React SPA, Azure DevOps Pipelines.
target_output.functional_feature_map: RFQ Creation Workflow: procurement manager creates RFQ with header fields and bulk line item import via Papa Parse CSV parser; line items validated against part number catalog; RFQ broadcast to selected suppliers with deadline. Bid Comparison Matrix: AG Grid Enterprise server-side row model fetches paginated supplier bids per line item; color-coded lowest-price highlighting; variance percentage columns; export to Excel. Approval Chain Engine: configurable spend thresholds per buyer_org trigger tier routing; approver receives email with one-click deep link to review page; escalation timer fires after 48h non-response. PO Generation: JasperReports template populated with winning bid data and e-signature block via DocuSign Embedded SDK; PDF generated within 5 seconds p99 under load. Spend Analytics Dashboard: Recharts bar/pie charts over spend_analytics_snapshots; filters by period, supplier, category. Admin Panel: buyer org onboarding, Keycloak realm group management, supplier qualification workflow.
target_output.system_components: Azure Static Web Apps serving React 18 Vite SPA via Azure CDN. AKS cluster with node pools: system, api (Spring Boot 3 with Java 21 virtual threads), batch (Spring Batch workers). Keycloak 24 deployment on AKS with PostgreSQL backend for realm data. Azure Database for PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with RLS enabled and Flyway migrations. Azure Cache for Redis 7 Premium for approval state and deadline TTLs. Azure Key Vault for contract_terms AES-256-GCM keys with annual rotation policy. Azure Container Registry for Docker images. Azure Service Bus for async ERP export notifications. Application Insights for APM metrics. Azure Blob Storage for PO PDFs and audit log exports.
target_output.workflows: RFQ-to-Bid Cycle: procurement manager creates RFQ with 3000 line items via CSV upload; Spring Boot validates part numbers and broadcasts supplier invitations via Azure Service Bus; suppliers submit itemized bids through bid portal with unit prices per line item; at deadline, procurement manager opens AG Grid comparison matrix displaying all bids side-by-side with lowest-price cells highlighted in green; manager selects winning bid lines and proceeds to PO generation. Multi-Tier Approval Chain: PO total value $850,000 exceeds L1 threshold $100,000 and L2 threshold $500,000; PO routes to L1 approver who approves within 4 hours; then routes to L2 CFO approver; on L2 approval, JasperReports generates PDF; DocuSign Embedded collects e-signature; PO status transitions to issued; Spring Batch exports PO XML to ERP via SFTP. Spend Analytics Refresh: nightly Spring Batch job aggregates issued POs by buyer_org_id, supplier_id, and part_category for prior month; writes spend_analytics_snapshots rows; Recharts dashboard queries pre-aggregated snapshots avoiding live PO table scans.
target_output.data_model: rfq_events (id UUID PK, buyer_org_id UUID, title VARCHAR, status VARCHAR CHECK (status IN ('draft','open','closed','cancelled')), deadline TIMESTAMPTZ, delivery_terms TEXT, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). supplier_bids (id UUID PK, rfq_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID, status VARCHAR, total_value NUMERIC, currency CHAR(3), submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). quote_line_items (id UUID PK, bid_id UUID FK, part_number VARCHAR, description TEXT, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, lead_days INT, incoterms VARCHAR). purchase_orders (id UUID PK, rfq_id UUID FK, buyer_org_id UUID FK, supplier_id UUID FK, total_value NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, pdf_blob_url TEXT, issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ). approval_chains (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, tier_level INT, approver_id UUID, spend_threshold NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, escalation_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). contract_terms (id UUID PK, po_id UUID FK, terms_text_encrypted BYTEA, key_version VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). spend_analytics_snapshots (id UUID PK, buyer_org_id UUID, period_month DATE, total_spend NUMERIC, po_count INT, supplier_count INT, top_categories_json JSONB, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). RLS policies: CREATE POLICY buyer_org_isolation ON rfq_events USING (buyer_org_id = current_setting('app.current_buyer_org_id', true)::UUID); same policy pattern on supplier_bids, purchase_orders, approval_chains, contract_terms, spend_analytics_snapshots.
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /rfq (create RFQ with line items CSV). GET /rfq?status=open (list buyer org RFQs via RLS). POST /rfq/{id}/bids (supplier bid submission). GET /rfq/{id}/comparison?page=&size= (AG Grid server-side row model pagination endpoint). POST /po (generate PO from selected bid). GET /po/{id}/approval-status (approval chain current state). POST /po/{id}/approve (approver action). GET /po/{id}/pdf (stream PO PDF from Azure Blob). GET /analytics/spend?period=&buyer_org= (spend analytics from snapshots). All endpoints require Keycloak JWT; buyer_org_id extracted from token and injected as SET LOCAL before each DB query.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS policies on all buyer data tables enforce buyer_org_id isolation using SET LOCAL app.current_buyer_org_id injected from JWT claim by Spring Security request filter before every database transaction. Policy syntax: CREATE POLICY buyer_org_isolation ON rfq_events USING (buyer_org_id = current_setting('app.current_buyer_org_id', true)::UUID). Azure Key Vault AES-256-GCM encryption for contract_terms field; key_version VARCHAR stored on each row at encryption time; annual key rotation via Azure Key Vault rotation policy; Spring Batch re-encryption job processes records with outdated key_version in 1000-row batches post-rotation; previous key versions retained 2 years. Keycloak 24 SAML 2.0 federation with assertion audience restriction and 5-minute validity window. MFA enforced for approver and admin roles via Keycloak authentication flow. SOC 2 audit log: immutable append-only table recording all RFQ creation, bid submission, approval, and PO issuance events with user_id, IP, timestamp, and field diff. Audit log exported weekly to Azure Blob cold storage for SOC 2 evidence.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline: on PR build Docker image, run JUnit tests (>80% coverage), Vitest for React components, OWASP dependency check, terraform plan. On main merge: push image to Azure Container Registry, deploy to AKS staging slot, run integration test suite including RFQ lifecycle smoke test and RLS isolation test, promote to AKS production blue-green slot on pass. Vite build output deployed to Azure Static Web Apps via az staticwebapp deploy step. Flyway migrations run as AKS init container before new pod version starts; migrations must be backward-compatible for blue-green cutover. Spring Batch jobs deployed on dedicated AKS batch node pool with KEDA HTTP scaling based on Spring Batch job queue depth. Blue-green slot promotion validated by health check on /actuator/health before traffic cut.
target_output.observability: Application Insights custom events: rfq_created, bid_submitted, approval_action, po_issued with buyer_org_id dimension for funnel analysis. Custom metrics: rfq_to_po_cycle_time_hours (procurement ops KPI), approval_chain_wait_time_hours per tier, po_pdf_generation_p99_ms (SLA alert >5000ms), ag_grid_pagination_p99_ms. Azure Monitor alert: PO PDF p99 >5s triggers on-call page. Spring Boot Actuator metrics scraped by Application Insights. Distributed tracing via Application Insights SDK with correlation IDs across Spring Boot and Spring Batch jobs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: AKS api node pool: 3 x Standard_D4s_v3 at ~$480/month baseline; scales to 10 nodes at peak RFQ deadline. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server: Standard_D4s_v3 2-vCore at ~$280/month. Azure Cache for Redis 7 Premium P1: ~$400/month. Keycloak on AKS: included in api node pool. Application Insights 5GB/day: ~$150/month. Estimated baseline ~$1,500/month; scaling to 50 concurrent buyers with 10 open RFQs: ~$2,800/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-8): Keycloak setup with SAML federation for 2 pilot buyer orgs; RFQ creation with CSV line item import; supplier bid submission portal; AG Grid comparison matrix with server-side pagination; single-tier approval workflow; JasperReports PO PDF generation; PostgreSQL RLS policies deployed and validated. Phase 2 (Weeks 9-12): Multi-tier approval chains with configurable spend thresholds; approval escalation timer; DocuSign Embedded e-signature; Spring Batch ERP XML export job; spend analytics dashboard with pre-aggregated snapshots. Phase 3 (Weeks 13-16): Admin panel for buyer org and supplier management; contract_terms AES-256-GCM encryption with Key Vault; SOC 2 audit log implementation; key rotation batch job; scale testing with 1000-line-item RFQ and 10 concurrent suppliers.
target_output.development_guidelines: React 18 with Vite is the frozen frontend stack; no SSR framework may be introduced. AG Grid server-side row model must be used for all line item grids exceeding 100 rows; client-side row model forbidden for bid comparison. PostgreSQL RLS must be enabled and policy validated in integration tests before each release; test must assert that buyer org A cannot read buyer org B rows. Azure Key Vault key rotation must be triggered in staging before every annual production rotation; re-encryption batch job must complete successfully in staging before production. Flyway migrations must be backward-compatible for blue-green deployment; destructive migrations require a separate pre-deployment maintenance window PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AG Grid server-side row model stale data during concurrent bid submissions. Mitigation: Spring Boot SSE endpoint pushes bid_submitted event to procurement dashboard; React Query cache invalidated on SSE event triggering pagination refresh. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS SET LOCAL bypassed if connection pooler uses session mode. Mitigation: PgBouncer must be configured in statement mode to prevent session variable leakage across connections; validate in integration test. Risk: Azure Key Vault re-encryption batch job contention with live PO reads during rotation. Mitigation: batch processes records in off-peak hours; row-level key_version allows reads to use old key version during re-encryption window. Tradeoff: Spring Boot monolith vs microservices; monolith chosen for MVP delivery speed with domain module boundaries for future extraction.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Frontend confirmed as React 18 with Vite throughout; Next.js removed from all plan sections. Azure Key Vault annual rotation with key_version column and Spring Batch re-encryption job specified. PostgreSQL RLS policy SQL defined: USING (buyer_org_id = current_setting('app.current_buyer_org_id', true)::UUID). PgBouncer statement mode required to prevent SET LOCAL session leakage.

metadata.notes: Industrial wholesale buyer platform; round 3 corrects critical Next.js contract violation from round 2 and resolves Key Vault rotation and RLS policy gaps.

profile.domain: Marketplace Platform Event Contracts Python SDK

project_goal.value: Python SDK providing typed Pydantic models, schema validation, and publish/subscribe helpers for all inter-service events in a decomposed marketplace platform covering orders, inventory, pricing, and fulfillment domains
project_goal.rationale: Event contract drift across services causes silent deserialization failures; a shared typed SDK enforces schema discipline at development time

target_users.value: Internal platform engineers at the marketplace company consuming or producing events across order, inventory, pricing, and fulfillment microservices
target_users.rationale: All four service teams will import the SDK as a development-time and runtime dependency

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a versioned Python package on private PyPI; no server runtime or hosted infrastructure

capabilities.value: backend, data, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Event schema definitions, Pydantic validation, Kafka publish/subscribe helpers, and schema registry integration

complexity_level.rationale: Schema versioning, backward compatibility enforcement, dead-letter routing, and schema registry sync require careful design

risk_level.rationale: Internal library with no hosted surface; worst case is a breaking release that fails service imports

data_sensitivity.rationale: Event schemas and payload structures are internal engineering artifacts

external_exposure.rationale: Distributed on private PyPI registry; not published to public PyPI

access_model.value: No access control; internal library importable by any service with private PyPI registry credentials
access_model.rationale: Internal tooling library with no multi-tenant or auth requirements

feature_scope.value: Typed Pydantic v2 event models for order.created, order.cancelled, inventory.reserved, inventory.released, pricing.updated, fulfillment.shipped, fulfillment.delivered events; EventEnvelope wrapper with metadata; KafkaPublisher helper with schema validation before publish; @on_event subscriber decorator with Pydantic deserialization and dead-letter routing on validation failure; schema registry sync CI step; breaking change detection CI gate
feature_scope.rationale: Full event contract library surface for the four service domains

mvp_scope.value: Pydantic v2 event models for order and inventory domains; EventEnvelope; KafkaPublisher with validation; @on_event decorator with dead-letter routing
mvp_scope.rationale: Core contract enforcement tooling for the two highest-event-volume service domains

security_baseline.value: No runtime security surface; private PyPI registry requires token auth for publish; schema registry sync uses service account token stored in GitHub Actions secrets
security_baseline.rationale: Library distribution security via private PyPI token auth

frontend_stack.value: none (library SDK; no UI component)
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend in scope for a Python library

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Pydantic v2; kafka-python 2.0 for Kafka integration; confluent_kafka for schema registry sync
backend_stack.rationale: Pydantic v2 provides native Python type validation; kafka-python is the standard Kafka client for Python services

data_platform.value: No embedded database; event schemas stored as Pydantic classes in source; schema registry (Confluent Schema Registry) synced from CI
data_platform.rationale: SDK manages schema definitions as code; runtime state is the schema registry

hosting_target.value: Private PyPI registry (Artifactory); distributed as marketplace-events-sdk Python package; no server hosting
hosting_target.rationale: Package distribution model appropriate for internal library

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data stored by the library; event payloads are transient in-memory; no retention policy required
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library does not persist data; consumers own their event storage

future_scope.value: AsyncIO-native async publisher and subscriber variants; JSON Schema export for non-Python service consumers; schema changelog generator for release notes
future_scope.rationale: Async support and JSON Schema export are planned for v2 after v1 validation across all four service teams

constraints.value: Must support Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12; no breaking changes in minor versions per semantic versioning; Pydantic v2 only (no v1 compatibility shim); schema registry sync must not block CI on schema registry downtime
constraints.rationale: Service teams run mixed Python minor versions; breaking changes require coordinated upgrade across all services

observability_baseline.value: No server observability; SDK emits structured log messages for publish failures, validation errors, and dead-letter routing events using Python logging module; consuming services forward logs to their own observability stack
observability_baseline.rationale: Library delegates observability to the consuming service; no telemetry server required

execution_preference.value: GitHub Actions CI with matrix test on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12; semantic versioning; automated release to private PyPI on semver tag push
execution_preference.rationale: Standard library release workflow

project.goal: Typed Python SDK enforcing inter-service event contracts with schema validation and dead-letter routing
project.target_users: Platform engineers across order, inventory, pricing, and fulfillment services

backend.framework: Pydantic v2 for event models; kafka-python 2.0 for Kafka integration
backend.api: Package public API: event model classes, EventEnvelope, KafkaPublisher, @on_event decorator

security.auth: Private PyPI token for package publish
security.transport: No server surface; Kafka TLS managed by consuming services

data.db: No database; schemas as Pydantic classes; Confluent Schema Registry synced from CI
data.cache: No cache

devops.ci: GitHub Actions matrix build on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12

constraints.versioning: Semantic versioning; no breaking changes in minor/patch
constraints.registry_sync: Schema registry sync must not block CI on registry downtime

open_questions.dead_letter_topic: Should SDK create dead-letter topic automatically or require pre-creation? Pre-creation by consuming service confirmed.

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK is well-scoped as an internal contract library. Typed Pydantic v2 models are the right approach for schema enforcement at development time with IDE autocomplete. The breaking change detection CI gate is the highest-value operational feature because it prevents silent contract drift from reaching production.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Pydantic v2 model_validator and field_validator provide sufficient validation for EventEnvelope metadata and domain payload fields. The @on_event decorator pattern is clean but must handle Kafka partition assignment and offset commit correctly for exactly-once consumer semantics. The schema registry sync CI step should use confluent_kafka SchemaRegistryClient with --dry-run on PR to detect conflicts before merge.
reasoner_reviews.security: Private PyPI token for publish must be stored in GitHub Actions secret and scoped to the marketplace-events-sdk package only. Schema registry service account should have read-write access to the platform namespace only.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Python 3.10 compatibility requires avoiding match/case syntax and newer TypeAlias forms. Breaking change detection must use ast-based diffing not semver guessing; the griffe library is appropriate for this. Schema registry sync with --soft-fail on connectivity issues prevents CI blockage.

backend.service_design: Package structure: marketplace_events/ with submodules for each domain (orders/, inventory/, pricing/, fulfillment/), shared/ (envelope.py, base.py, exceptions.py), kafka/ (publisher.py, subscriber.py, registry_sync.py). Public API exposed via marketplace_events/__init__.py with explicit __all__ list.
backend.api_patterns: Public API surface: EventEnvelope[T] generic dataclass wrapping typed payload with event_type, event_id, version, producer_service, produced_at. KafkaPublisher.publish(envelope: EventEnvelope) validates envelope before sending to Kafka topic derived from event_type. @on_event('order.created') decorator registers handler, deserializes Kafka message to typed EventEnvelope, routes to dead-letter topic on ValidationError.
backend.failure_handling: ValidationError on publish raises PublishValidationError with field-level error detail; consumer-side ValidationError triggers dead-letter routing to {topic}.dlq with original raw bytes and error context; structured log emitted at ERROR level with event_id, topic, and validation_errors list.
backend.backend_risks: kafka-python 2.0 is synchronous; high-volume publishing services may block on Kafka send; async variant planned for v2 but not in scope for v1

security.secrets_management: PYPI_TOKEN stored in GitHub Actions org secret scoped to marketplace-events-sdk repo; schema registry service account token stored in SCHEMA_REGISTRY_TOKEN secret; no credentials in source code or published package
security.security_risks: Malicious event payload injection via crafted Kafka messages is mitigated by Pydantic strict mode validation before any payload processing

data.entities: event_schemas (Python Pydantic class per event type: OrderCreatedEvent, OrderCancelledEvent, InventoryReservedEvent, InventoryReleasedEvent, PricingUpdatedEvent, FulfillmentShippedEvent, FulfillmentDeliveredEvent). event_envelopes (EventEnvelope[T] generic dataclass: event_type, event_id UUID, version SemVer, producer_service, produced_at datetime, payload T). dead_letter_configs (DeadLetterConfig dataclass: topic_suffix, log_level, include_raw_bytes). schema_registry_refs (SchemaRegistryConfig dataclass: url, subject_prefix, compatibility_level).
data.schema_notes: All event models use Pydantic v2 model_config with strict=True to prevent coercions; UUID fields use UUID type not str; datetime fields use datetime with timezone; enum fields use Python Enum classes not string literals
data.migration_notes: Breaking changes (field removal, type change, required field addition) require major version bump; non-breaking additions (optional field with default) allowed in minor; CI breaking change gate enforces this via griffe diff

devops.deployment_topology: No server deployment; package distributed via Artifactory private PyPI. GitHub Actions publishes to Artifactory on semver tag push via twine upload.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix: test job runs pytest on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 with coverage >90%; breaking-change job runs griffe diff between PR branch and main, fails if breaking changes detected in minor/patch version bump; schema-sync job runs confluent_kafka SchemaRegistryClient --dry-run on PR, --apply on main merge with --soft-fail on registry downtime; release job runs on semver tag push, builds wheel and sdist, uploads to Artifactory via twine.
devops.devops_risks: griffe breaking change detection requires accurate type annotation coverage; untyped public API functions will not trigger the gate correctly

ISS-009.title: @on_event decorator Kafka offset commit strategy unspecified
ISS-009.detail: Round 1 plan described @on_event decorator subscribing to Kafka topics but did not specify when consumer offsets are committed: before or after handler execution. Committing before risks message loss on handler failure; committing after risks duplicate processing on consumer restart.

history.note: Offset commit timing not described

ISS-010.title: Schema registry sync --soft-fail behavior not defined
ISS-010.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned schema registry sync CI step but did not specify the --soft-fail behavior: should CI pass or warn when schema registry is unreachable? This determines whether a registry outage blocks all SDK releases.

history.note: Soft-fail behavior not specified

focus_issues.title: @on_event decorator Kafka offset commit strategy unspecified
focus_issues.detail: Offset commit before vs after handler execution affects at-most-once vs at-least-once delivery guarantees.

focus_issues.title: Schema registry sync --soft-fail behavior not defined
focus_issues.detail: Registry outage behavior determines whether SDK releases are blocked by external infrastructure.

latest_recommendations: Specify @on_event decorator commits offset AFTER successful handler execution (at-least-once) with dead-letter routing on repeated ValidationError to bound reprocessing
Define schema registry sync --soft-fail: CI emits warning and continues on registry connection timeout; applies schema on successful connection; never blocks release on registry downtime
Document consumer group ID convention for @on_event decorator to prevent multiple services consuming on the same group unintentionally

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues after one round

previous_audits.summary: Solid library architecture with clean Pydantic v2 design. Kafka offset commit strategy and schema registry soft-fail behavior are gaps that need specification.
strengths: Pydantic v2 strict mode for event models is correct for contract enforcement
griffe breaking change detection in CI is a strong operational control
Package structure with per-domain submodules is clean and extensible
concerns: @on_event decorator offset commit timing unspecified; at-most-once vs at-least-once undefined
Schema registry sync failure behavior not described; registry outage could block all SDK releases
blocking_issues: ISS-009: Offset commit strategy absent from subscriber design
ISS-010: Schema registry sync failure mode not defined
recommendations: Specify at-least-once: commit offset after successful handler; dead-letter on persistent failure
Define --soft-fail: warning + continue on registry timeout; never block release
