previous_plan.title: Marketplace Events SDK Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python SDK for typed marketplace inter-service event contracts using Pydantic v2 and kafka-python, distributed on private PyPI with CI-enforced breaking change detection.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Package with per-domain event model submodules, EventEnvelope generic wrapper, KafkaPublisher with pre-publish validation, @on_event subscriber decorator with dead-letter routing.
previous_plan.data_model: Pydantic v2 event model classes per event type; EventEnvelope[T] generic dataclass; DeadLetterConfig; SchemaRegistryConfig.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix CI on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12; griffe breaking change gate; schema registry sync; twine publish to Artifactory on semver tag.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-009 by specifying at-least-once offset commit after successful handler execution with dead-letter routing on repeated failure, and ISS-010 by defining the schema registry --soft-fail behavior as warning-and-continue on timeout. Library SDK means no FrontendAgent; no server infrastructure means no DevOps topology. Active specialists are backend, security, data, devops (for CI/CD). No compliance_context needed: risk_level=low, data_sensitivity=internal.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified at-least-once delivery: @on_event decorator commits Kafka offset AFTER successful handler return. On handler exception: dead-letter routing to {topic}.dlq with original bytes and exception context; offset committed after dead-letter publish. On repeated ValidationError (3 consecutive for same event_id): skip offset commit and raise to allow consumer restart with manual intervention. Consumer group ID defaults to '{service_name}.{event_type}' and must be explicitly set per handler registration.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Consuming services understand the delivery guarantee (at-least-once) and dead-letter behavior; offset strategy is auditable in the plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined schema registry sync soft-fail: on PR, schema-sync CI job runs --dry-run; if schema registry connection times out (3s), job emits WARNING log and exits 0 (does not block PR merge); applies schema on success. On main merge, --apply mode runs; if registry unreachable after 3 retries with backoff, job emits ERROR and exits 0 (does not block release) but creates a GitHub issue tracking the missed sync. Schema registry sync is best-effort for releases but blocking for PRs that introduce new event types.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SDK releases are never blocked by schema registry downtime; PRs introducing new event types require successful registry dry-run; missed syncs are tracked via automated GitHub issues.

target_output.title: Marketplace Events SDK Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Python SDK (marketplace-events-sdk) providing typed Pydantic v2 event models, generic EventEnvelope, KafkaPublisher with pre-publish validation, and @on_event subscriber decorator with at-least-once delivery semantics and dead-letter routing for all inter-service events in a decomposed marketplace platform. Distributed on a private Artifactory PyPI registry. GitHub Actions CI enforces schema compatibility via griffe breaking change detection gate and Confluent Schema Registry sync with soft-fail on connectivity issues.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure Python library with no server runtime or hosted infrastructure. Package structure: marketplace_events/ with per-domain submodules (orders/, inventory/, pricing/, fulfillment/) plus shared/ (envelope.py, base.py, exceptions.py) and kafka/ (publisher.py, subscriber.py, registry_sync.py). Public API exported via marketplace_events/__init__.py. Event models are typed Pydantic v2 classes with strict mode enabled. EventEnvelope[T] is a generic dataclass wrapping any typed payload with standard metadata fields. KafkaPublisher validates EventEnvelope via Pydantic before serializing and publishing to the derived Kafka topic. @on_event decorator subscribes a function to a Kafka topic, deserializes to typed EventEnvelope, and commits offset after successful handler return (at-least-once). Dead-letter routing sends failed messages to {topic}.dlq. GitHub Actions CI runs pytest matrix on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12, griffe breaking change gate, and Confluent Schema Registry sync with soft-fail on connectivity issues.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12 (supports 3.10, 3.11, 3.12). Pydantic v2 with strict=True for event model validation. kafka-python 2.0 for Kafka producer and consumer. confluent_kafka SchemaRegistryClient for schema registry sync. griffe for AST-based public API breaking change detection. pytest with pytest-cov (>90% coverage gate). twine for Artifactory publish. GitHub Actions matrix CI. Artifactory private PyPI for package distribution. Semantic versioning enforced by griffe CI gate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Typed Event Models: Pydantic v2 classes per event (OrderCreatedEvent, OrderCancelledEvent, InventoryReservedEvent, InventoryReleasedEvent, PricingUpdatedEvent, FulfillmentShippedEvent, FulfillmentDeliveredEvent) with strict type validation and UUID/datetime fields. EventEnvelope[T]: generic dataclass with event_type, event_id UUID, version SemVer string, producer_service, produced_at datetime with timezone, payload T. KafkaPublisher: validates EventEnvelope before publish; derives Kafka topic from event_type using configurable prefix; raises PublishValidationError on validation failure with field-level detail. @on_event Decorator: registers handler for named event type; consumes Kafka topic; deserializes to typed EventEnvelope; commits offset after successful handler return (at-least-once); routes to {topic}.dlq on ValidationError with structured dead-letter log. Breaking Change Gate: griffe diff in CI fails PR if public API breaking change detected on minor/patch version bump. Schema Registry Sync: confluent_kafka SchemaRegistryClient syncs Pydantic schemas to Confluent Schema Registry on main merge with soft-fail on connectivity timeout.
target_output.system_components: marketplace_events/orders/events.py: OrderCreatedEvent(buyer_id UUID, items List[OrderLineItem], total_amount Decimal, currency str, created_at datetime). marketplace_events/inventory/events.py: InventoryReservedEvent(sku VARCHAR, warehouse_id UUID, quantity_reserved int, reservation_id UUID, reserved_at datetime). marketplace_events/shared/envelope.py: EventEnvelope[T](event_type str, event_id UUID, version str, producer_service str, produced_at datetime, payload T). marketplace_events/kafka/publisher.py: KafkaPublisher(bootstrap_servers, topic_prefix) with publish(envelope: EventEnvelope) method. marketplace_events/kafka/subscriber.py: EventSubscriber with @on_event(event_type, consumer_group_id) decorator. marketplace_events/kafka/registry_sync.py: SchemaRegistrySyncer with sync_all() and dry_run() methods. marketplace_events/shared/exceptions.py: PublishValidationError, DeadLetterRoutingError, SchemaRegistryConflictError.
target_output.workflows: Publishing an Event: service imports OrderCreatedEvent and KafkaPublisher; constructs OrderCreatedEvent with order fields; wraps in EventEnvelope with producer_service='order-service' and version='1.0.0'; calls publisher.publish(envelope); publisher validates envelope via Pydantic model_validate; on success serializes to JSON and sends to topic 'marketplace.order.created'; on ValidationError raises PublishValidationError with field errors list; structured log emitted at DEBUG on success, ERROR on failure. Subscribing to an Event: service decorates a function with @on_event('order.created', consumer_group_id='inventory-service.order.created'); on Kafka message receipt, subscriber deserializes JSON to OrderCreatedEvent EventEnvelope via model_validate; on success, calls handler function, then commits Kafka offset; on ValidationError, publishes raw message bytes to 'marketplace.order.created.dlq' with error context, commits offset, emits ERROR log with event_id and validation_errors; on handler exception, does not commit offset, emits ERROR log, retries up to 3 times before dead-lettering. Adding a New Event Type: engineer adds versioned schema class to appropriate domain submodule; runs griffe check locally to verify no breaking changes; opens PR; CI griffe gate and schema registry --dry-run run; on schema conflict, SchemaRegistryConflictError raised in CI; engineer resolves compatibility before merge; on main merge, schema_registry sync --apply runs with soft-fail.
target_output.data_model: Event schema classes as Pydantic v2 models in source: OrderCreatedEvent(buyer_id: UUID, seller_id: UUID, items: List[OrderLineItem], total_amount: Decimal, currency: str, created_at: datetime). OrderLineItem(sku: str, quantity: int, unit_price: Decimal). OrderCancelledEvent(order_id: UUID, reason: str, cancelled_by: UUID, cancelled_at: datetime). InventoryReservedEvent(sku: str, warehouse_id: UUID, quantity_reserved: int, reservation_id: UUID, reserved_at: datetime). InventoryReleasedEvent(reservation_id: UUID, sku: str, quantity_released: int, released_at: datetime). PricingUpdatedEvent(sku: str, new_price: Decimal, currency: str, effective_from: datetime, updated_by: UUID). FulfillmentShippedEvent(order_id: UUID, tracking_number: str, carrier: str, shipped_at: datetime, estimated_delivery: datetime). FulfillmentDeliveredEvent(order_id: UUID, tracking_number: str, delivered_at: datetime, signature_required: bool). All models: model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, frozen=True). EventEnvelope[T]: event_type str, event_id UUID, version str (SemVer pattern), producer_service str, produced_at datetime, payload T.
target_output.api_design: Public package API (marketplace_events/__init__.py __all__): Event model classes per domain. EventEnvelope generic dataclass. KafkaPublisher(bootstrap_servers: list[str], topic_prefix: str, serializer: Callable) with publish(envelope: EventEnvelope[Any]) -> None and close() methods. on_event(event_type: str, consumer_group_id: str, bootstrap_servers: list[str], dead_letter_suffix: str = '.dlq') decorator factory returning EventSubscriber. DeadLetterConfig(topic_suffix: str, log_level: str, include_raw_bytes: bool). SchemaRegistrySyncer(registry_url: str, subject_prefix: str, compatibility: str) with sync_all(dry_run: bool, soft_fail: bool) -> SyncResult. PublishValidationError, DeadLetterRoutingError, SchemaRegistryConflictError exception classes. Usage: from marketplace_events import OrderCreatedEvent, EventEnvelope, KafkaPublisher, on_event.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PYPI_TOKEN secret in GitHub Actions org secrets scoped to marketplace-events-sdk repository; used by twine upload step for Artifactory publish. SCHEMA_REGISTRY_TOKEN secret for Confluent Schema Registry service account with read-write access to platform namespace only. No credentials embedded in package source or wheel. Pydantic strict=True prevents type coercion attacks via crafted Kafka message payloads. Published package wheel is signed via twine --sign with GPG key held by platform team. No network server surface; no inbound connections; no authentication layer required.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflows: test.yml matrix on Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 runs pytest with --cov threshold 90%; breaking-change.yml runs griffe diff between PR branch and main, fails job if breaking changes detected and version bump is minor or patch; schema-sync.yml on PR runs --dry-run with 3s connection timeout and soft-fail (exit 0 on timeout, emit warning), on main merge runs --apply with 3-retry backoff and soft-fail (exit 0 on timeout after retries, creates GitHub issue via gh cli for missed sync tracking); release.yml triggered on semver tag push (v*.*.*): builds wheel and sdist via python -m build, uploads to Artifactory private PyPI via twine upload --repository-url. Package consumers pin to SDK version in pyproject.toml and update via Dependabot PR with automatic breaking-change-gate check.
target_output.observability: SDK emits structured log messages via Python logging module using getLogger('marketplace_events'). Log events: publish_success (DEBUG, includes event_type, event_id, topic, latency_ms), publish_failure (ERROR, includes event_type, event_id, validation_errors list), dead_letter_routed (ERROR, includes original_topic, dlq_topic, event_id, error_context), offset_committed (DEBUG, includes topic, partition, offset), schema_registry_sync_warning (WARNING, includes registry_url, timeout_seconds), schema_registry_conflict (ERROR, includes event_type, conflict_detail). Consuming services configure marketplace_events logger level and forward to their observability stack (Datadog, Grafana, etc.).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No infrastructure cost; library runs in-process within consuming services. GitHub Actions CI costs: matrix test (3 Python versions) plus breaking-change gate plus schema sync runs approximately 8 minutes per PR; at $0.008/minute for ubuntu-latest, cost is under $0.10 per PR. Artifactory private PyPI hosting included in existing enterprise license. No operational scaling concern; library scales with the consuming service instances.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core Contract Models (Weeks 1-2): Pydantic v2 event models for orders and inventory domains; EventEnvelope generic dataclass; PublishValidationError exception; pytest suite with 90%+ coverage; GitHub Actions matrix CI. Phase 2 - Kafka Integration (Weeks 3-4): KafkaPublisher with pre-publish validation and PublishValidationError; @on_event decorator with at-least-once offset commit and dead-letter routing to {topic}.dlq; DeadLetterConfig; griffe breaking change gate in CI. Phase 3 - Full Domain Coverage and Registry (Weeks 5-6): pricing and fulfillment domain event models; SchemaRegistrySyncer with --dry-run on PR and --apply with soft-fail on main merge; release workflow with twine Artifactory publish; consumer group ID convention documentation and enforcement via decorator validation.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public API functions and classes must have type annotations; untyped symbols are not detected by griffe and will not trigger the breaking change gate. Event model fields must use Python Enum classes for string enum values, not raw strings. UUID fields typed as uuid.UUID not str; datetime fields include timezone (datetime with tzinfo). Consumer group ID must be set explicitly in @on_event decorator as '{service_name}.{event_type}'; default consumer_group_id is forbidden to prevent accidental group collision across services. No breaking changes in minor or patch releases; breaking changes require major version bump and coordinated upgrade PR to all four service repos. Schema registry sync failure on PR for new event types must be resolved before merge; failure on main merge creates GitHub issue and must be resolved within 1 business day.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: griffe breaking change gate fails to detect breaking changes in untyped public functions. Mitigation: mypy strict check in CI enforces full type annotation coverage; untyped public API symbols fail mypy. Risk: at-least-once delivery causes duplicate event processing in consuming services if handler is not idempotent. Mitigation: SDK documentation requires consuming handlers to implement idempotency keyed on event_id UUID; example idempotency pattern provided in README. Risk: schema registry --soft-fail on main merge causes schema to drift from registry after repeated outages. Mitigation: missed syncs tracked via automated GitHub issue with 1 business day SLA; SchemaRegistrySyncer.sync_all() can be run manually as a one-off job. Tradeoff: at-least-once vs exactly-once; exactly-once requires Kafka transactions and idempotent producer which adds significant complexity; at-least-once with idempotent handlers is sufficient for the current marketplace event volume.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Offset commit strategy: at-least-once; commit after successful handler return; dead-letter on persistent failure. Schema registry soft-fail: warning-and-continue on timeout; never block release; missed syncs tracked via GitHub issue. Consumer group ID: must be set explicitly as '{service_name}.{event_type}'; no default. Dead-letter topic name: '{original_topic}.dlq'; pre-created by consuming service before deploying subscriber.

metadata.notes: Marketplace inter-service event contracts Python SDK; round 2 resolves Kafka offset commit strategy and schema registry soft-fail behavior.
